title: Key Account Manager
description: PropertyGuru is Southeast Asia’s leading PropTech company, and the preferred destination for over 32 million property seekers monthly to connect with over 50,000 agents monthly to find their dream home. PropertyGuru empowers property seekers with more than 2.1 million real estate listings, in-depth insights, and solutions that enable them to make confident property decisions across Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand and Vietnam. PropertyGuru.com.sg was launched in Singapore in 2007 and since then, PropertyGuru Group has made the property journey a transparent one for property seekers in Southeast Asia. In the last 18 years, PropertyGuru has grown into a high-growth PropTech company with a robust portfolio including leading property marketplaces and award-winning mobile apps across its markets in Singapore, Malaysia, Vietnam, Thailand as well as the region’s biggest and most respected industry recognition platform – PropertyGuru Asia Property Awards , events and publications across Asia. About the Role As a Key Account Manager , you will play a pivotal role in growing PropertyGuru’s business by developing strategic client relationships, uncovering new opportunities, and driving value for some of our most important partners. You will combine deep market understanding with strong commercial acumen to deliver solutions that help our clients win and fuel PropertyGuru’s continued leadership in the region. As a Guru you will be accountable for: Drive strategic account growth by identifying new and existing business opportunities and expanding PropertyGuru’s presence within key client segments. Build and nurture long-term partnerships , ensuring clients receive consistent value and an exceptional service experience. Conduct detailed market and competitor analysis to identify trends, opportunities, and emerging client needs. Drive sales initiatives and conduct trainings to increase product adoption, drive revenue growth, and exceed sales targets. Develop compelling proposals and presentations that clearly communicate our value propositions and data-driven insights. Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Marketing, Operations, to align on solutions with broader business objectives and client requirements. Support and work alongside with senior key account manager to achieve team targets We're looking for someone who: Proven experience in business development or key account management , with a track record of meeting or exceeding sales goals. Hands-on experience with cold calling and face-to-face client meetings , with the confidence and communication skills to engage prospects, pitch value propositions, and convert opportunities into revenue. You are comfortable initiating conversations, handling objections, and building rapport quickly in both virtual and in-person settings Strong strategic and commercial thinking , with the ability to identify opportunities and execute effective growth plans. Exceptional relationship management skills , including the ability to influence, consult, and build trust with clients. Analytical and data-driven mindset , capable of interpreting market insights to guide recommendations and decision-making. Highly proactive, self-motivated, and outcome-oriented , with strong leadership qualities and a passion for driving client success. Strong team player , you actively collaborate, support teammates, and contribute to a positive, high-performance team culture. Ability to work effectively with minimal supervision , demonstrating maturity, accountability, and strong organizational skills. You can prioritize tasks, manage time independently, and consistently deliver results in a fast-paced environment. Our commitment to you: Hybrid flexible working that focuses on outcomes over hours. Holistic rewards package covering your financial, physical & mental health. Multi-directional career development across all levels. Inclusive benefits like equal paternity leave, supporting all employees in work-life balance.

title: Marketing Analytics Specialist
description: PropertyGuru is Southeast Asia’s leading PropTech company, and the preferred destination for over 32 million property seekers monthly to connect with over 50,000 agents monthly to find their dream home. PropertyGuru empowers property seekers with more than 2.1 million real estate listings, in-depth insights, and solutions that enable them to make confident property decisions across Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand and Vietnam. PropertyGuru.com.sg was launched in Singapore in 2007 and since then, PropertyGuru Group has made the property journey a transparent one for property seekers in Southeast Asia. In the last 18 years, PropertyGuru has grown into a high-growth PropTech company with a robust portfolio including leading property marketplaces and award-winning mobile apps across its markets in Singapore, Malaysia, Vietnam, Thailand as well as the region’s biggest and most respected industry recognition platform – PropertyGuru Asia Property Awards , events and publications across Asia. For more information, please visit: PropertyGuruGroup.com ; PropertyGuru Group on LinkedIn . Recognised as a Top Employers Certified* organisation, we’re proud to be among the best workplaces in the region—celebrating an inclusive culture of excellence, growth, and well-being. About the Role We are hiring a Marketing Analytics Specialist to drive analytical thinking, data exploration, and measurement across Growth and Product Marketing. This role focuses on data analysis, insight generation, and performance measurement, enabling teams to make informed, evidence-based decisions. You will work with large, complex datasets across product, user behavior, and marketing touchpoints to uncover trends, assess impact, and translate data into clear insights for senior stakeholders. This is a individual contributor role, reporting to the Growth & Product Marketing Lead, and partnering closely with Product, Data, and Marketing teams. As a Guru, you will be accountable for : Data Analysis & Insight Generation Analyse first-party platform and marketing datasets to identify: User and agent behaviour patterns Search, intent, and engagement signals Adoption, retention, and usage trends Conduct deep-dive analyses to answer strategic questions around: Feature and product adoption Marketing effectiveness and value delivery Behavioural changes across user segments Translate complex analyses into clear, structured insights and recommendations for non-technical stakeholders Marketing & Growth Measurement Frameworks Define and own marketing and growth measurement frameworks, including: Metric definitions and success criteria Funnel, cohort, and segmentation logic Design, build, and maintain scalable dashboards and reports, ensuring: Consistent definitions across teams Data accuracy and reliability Clear interpretation of results Partner with Data and Engineering teams to: Validate data sources and pipelines Improve data quality and availability over time Experimentation & Performance Analysis Design and analyse experiments and A/B tests, including: Hypothesis definition Measurement approach Post-test analysis and readouts Evaluate growth initiatives and campaigns through: Incrementality analysis Trend and variance analysis Performance comparisons across channels and cohorts Produce structured performance summaries that inform: Prioritisation decisions Optimisation opportunities Future experimentation Analytics Enablement & Decision Support Act as a trusted analytics partner to Growth and Product Marketing stakeholders Proactively surface insights and risks, rather than responding only to ad-hoc requests Help establish a culture of data literacy and analytical rigour within Marketing What we are looking for: Experience in: Data analytics Marketing analytics Growth or product analytics Strong hands-on expertise in: Advanced SQL (complex joins, window functions, performance optimisation) Data analysis and modelling techniques Dashboarding and BI tools (e.g. Looker, Tableau, Power BI) Experience working with: Customer and behavioural data (e.g. GA4, Segment) Marketing and attribution data (e.g. Braze, AppsFlyer) Proven experience analysing: Funnels, cohorts, and user segments Experiments and A/B tests Solid understanding of paid marketing channels (e.g. Meta, SEM) from a measurement and analytics perspective Nice to Have Experience in product-led, marketplace, or platform-based businesses Familiarity with data modelling or analytics engineering concepts Strong storytelling and stakeholder communication skills Our commitment to you: Hybrid flexible working that focuses on outcomes over hours. Holistic rewards package covering your financial, physical & mental health. Multi-directional career development across all levels. Inclusive benefits like equal paternity leave, supporting all employees in work-life balance. At PropertyGuru, you’ll be part of a Top Employers Certified* company that puts people at the heart of everything we do.

title: Senior Associate, Voice of Brand
description: About KK39 KK39 is the investment arm of Wee Hur Holdings Ltd., a Singapore-listed group with a long-standing reputation in real estate in Singapore and Australia. We are evolving into a professionalised multi-asset investment platform spanning Private Equity, Private Credit, and Venture Capital, and are actively strengthening our industry presence and relationships with LPs, GPs, and ecosystem partners. To support this growth, KK39 is seeking a Voice of Brand (Senior Associate) who will spearhead our branding, communications, and engagement efforts to ensure KK39’s presence in the investment community is visible, credible, and differentiated. Role Overview The Voice of Brand (Senior Associate) will act as KK39’s branding and communications lead, responsible for building a consistent external voice for the firm. This role blends strategic communications, event curation, and stakeholder engagement to elevate KK39’s profile in the investment ecosystem. You will work closely with the investment team, translating KK39’s activities, perspectives, and initiatives into compelling narratives and touchpoints that resonate with LPs, GPs, and partners. Key Responsibilities 1. Brand Strategy & Positioning · Develop and refine KK39’s brand narrative to reflect our unique positioning as a listed-group-backed investment office. · Ensure consistency of tone, voice, and design across all communication channels. · Build branding frameworks (templates, messaging guides, outreach playbooks) that the investment team can easily adopt. 2. Events & Community Engagement · Lead the end-to-end planning, coordination, and execution of KK39-hosted events (eg. LP/GP networking sessions, roundtables, thought-leadership dinners). · Manage event logistics, invitations, attendee engagement, and follow-up communications. · Build and maintain a calendar of industry events and conferences; coordinate KK39’s participation, visibility, and speaking opportunities. 3. Content & Communications · Craft thought-leadership content (LinkedIn posts, press releases, newsletters, and op-eds) to highlight KK39’s activities and perspectives. · Produce post-event summaries and follow-up campaigns to ensure engagement is maximised beyond the event itself. · Coordinate with external partners (media, PR, design vendors) as needed. 4. Stakeholder Engagement · Work alongside the investment team to strengthen relationships with LPs, GPs, and ecosystem partners by creating professional and timely touchpoints. · Track attendee feedback, engagement data, and industry response to measure brand-building effectiveness. · Support development of investor-facing materials (event decks, thought pieces, conference presence). 5. Internal Enablement · Free up the investment team by owning the operational and execution-heavy aspects of branding and events. · Maintain an internal repository of content, event templates, and standard communications for reuse. Job Requirements · Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or related field. · 3–5 years of experience in branding, corporate communications, or marketing within financial services, investment firms, or professional services. · Strong understanding of brand strategy, positioning, and messaging. · Excellent written and verbal communication skills; ability to craft compelling narratives. · Familiarity with content creation for LinkedIn, press releases, newsletters, and thought-leadership pieces. · Ability to manage external vendors (PR agencies, media, design). · Strong organizational skills and attention to detail. · Proficiency in digital marketing tools and social media platforms. · Comfortable working in a fast-paced, multi-asset investment environment.

title: Relationship Manager
description: PropertyGuru is Southeast Asia’s leading PropTech company, and the preferred destination for over 32 million property seekers monthly to connect with over 50,000 agents monthly to find their dream home. PropertyGuru empowers property seekers with more than 2.1 million real estate listings, in-depth insights, and solutions that enable them to make confident property decisions across Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand and Vietnam. PropertyGuru.com.sg was launched in Singapore in 2007 and since then, PropertyGuru Group has made the property journey a transparent one for property seekers in Southeast Asia. In the last 18 years, PropertyGuru has grown into a high-growth PropTech company with a robust portfolio including leading property marketplaces and award-winning mobile apps across its markets in Singapore, Malaysia, Vietnam, Thailand as well as the region’s biggest and most respected industry recognition platform – PropertyGuru Asia Property Awards , events and publications across Asia. About the Role As a Relationship Manager, you will play a critical role in expanding PropertyGuru’s customer base by engaging and supporting mass market agent clients. This role is hunter-focused: you will drive new business acquisition through proactive outreach, effective prospecting, and strong product pitches. You will be responsible for building early-stage relationships, understanding agent needs, and helping them get started on our platform. You will recommend relevant products, and ensure every agent receives professional, high-quality service. This role is ideal for someone energetic, driven, and eager to grow a career in sales within the PropTech industry. As a Guru you will be accountable for: Acquire new clients through high-volume cold calling and proactive outreach to mass market agent clients. Conduct high-volume cold calling and outreach to build a strong pipeline of prospects and generate new revenue opportunities. Be comfortable with face-to-face engagements, including client meetings, networking sessions, and training events, representing PropertyGuru professionally and confidently. Deliver effective product pitches and demonstrations, clearly explaining how PropertyGuru’s solutions can support agent success. Build early-stage relationships with agent clients, providing guidance and support as they start using the platform. Identify client needs and recommend suitable products, ensuring agents understand the value and features of our solutions. Support account onboarding and activation, including helping new clients set up, navigate their account, and optimise their usage. Maintain strong service standards, ensuring mass market clients receive professional and timely support. Gather market feedback and competitor insights to help improve product offerings and sales strategies. Collaborate with team mates to surface opportunities, share market insights, and support go-to-market initiatives. Hit and exceed monthly sales and activity targets, demonstrating consistency, resilience, and drive. Be a strong team player, work and communicate well with team mates, sharing insights, and supporting team initiatives to contribute to a positive and cohesive working environment. We're looking for someone who: Experience in sales, telesales, customer service, or business development, preferably in a high-volume or fast-paced environment. Strong hunter mentality, motivated, persistent and results-oriented. Confidence in cold calling and client engagement, with the ability to deliver clear pitches and handle objections effectively. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build rapport quickly with agent clients. Positive, energetic, and resilient, with a strong desire to learn and grow in a commercial role. Organised and self-driven, able to manage your time, track activities, and follow through consistently. Team player, willing to collaborate, share insights, and contribute to the overall success of the commercial team. Ability to work effectively with minimal supervision, demonstrating maturity, accountability, and strong organizational skills. You can prioritize tasks, manage time independently, and consistently deliver results in a fast-paced environment. Our commitment to you: Hybrid flexible working that focuses on outcomes over hours. Holistic rewards package covering your financial, physical & mental health. Multi-directional career development across all levels. Inclusive benefits like equal paternity leave, supporting all employees in work-life balance.

description: We are a fast-growing start-up Facility Tech Enterprise. Our mission is to Improve the Quality of Urban Living. We provide integrated home and office services, such as facility management, cleaning, aircon, laundry, smart homes, and etc. to clients in UK and Singapore . title: Industrial Property Consultant
description: About the Role We are seeking a motivated and analytical individual to join our consultancy team as an Industrial Property Consultant . In this role, you will work closely with industrial clients to develop a thorough understanding of their business operations, financial position, workforce profile and operational requirements. This understanding will be applied to support property-related decisions that have direct implications on the client’s operations, cost structure and long-term planning. The role is primarily analytical and report-driven. This position is well suited to individuals who are comfortable working with data, structured documentation requirements and who enjoy producing clear and substantiated reports. Key Responsibilities Business and Operational Assessment • Review clients’ financial statements. • Analyze clients’ business models and operational workflows. • Review employee headcount, manpower composition and organizational structure. • Assess operational scale, space utilization and site requirements. Property Planning Support and Reporting • Prepare structured analyses for industrial property planning. • Draft reports for property-related applications. • Develop structured strategies relating to space requirements and site usage. • Ensure reports and submissions are accurate, consistent and complete. Coordination and Engagement • Obtain information and data from clients. • Clarify data and assumptions with relevant parties. • Respond to queries on reports and supporting materials. • Coordinate documentation with internal and external parties. • Manage multiple assignments and timelines concurrently. Qualifications and Requirements • Ability to review and interpret financial statements. • Strong analytical capability. • Strong written communication and report-writing skills. • Proficiency in Microsoft Word and Excel. • Organized and detail-oriented. • Ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines. • Diploma or higher qualification in any discipline. • Prior experience in business analysis, reporting, consultancy support or property-related roles is an advantage. What We Offer • Competitive monthly remuneration. • Performance-based incentives. • Annual performance bonus and Annual Wage Supplement. • On-the-job training and guidance. • Professional and collaborative working environment.

title: Director, Transactions Management
description: About the role You will deliver Portfolio Strategy and Transaction Management (TM) services to some of the world’s largest companies. This position calls for an energetic, detail-oriented professional with excellent project management, analytical, communication and collaboration skills as well as experience of managing real estate strategies and transactions across Asia Pacific. Comfortable in working towards achieving performance targets, you will be responsible to deliver a range of possible real estate and workplace solutions to clients looking for the next generation benefits that come from a vested partnership with JLL. Portfolio Strategy & Planning – Country, City and Metro plans, Global Initiatives Assessment of portfolio performance, portfolio rationalization and divestment, lease vs. ownership structures, cost reduction initiatives and bid support to achieve business change. Lead planning process to develop strategies to lower real estate costs and improve workplace effectiveness. Identify opportunities for optimization, cost saving and improved ROI in the Client portfolio Conduct fact-based analysis using occupancy, portfolio, real estate, and business data to align stakeholders on informed real estate plans. Through strong business relationships and clear, compelling business cases, challenge status quo thinking to drive unexpected portfolio optimization opportunities Develop overarching strategies related to workplace, organization, process, sustainability, operations and finance to govern the portfolio and set a foundation for city master plans Analyze inputs from the strategic planning process and on-account systems (business requirements, costs, facility information, cap-ex requirements, forecasts, occupancy data, lease and market data) Analyze and make recommendations with respect to strategic dispositions, investment and market opportunities, hold versus sell analysis, feasibility studies, asset review and market studies, highest and best use analysis, acquisition and advice analysis and valuations for sale/leaseback purposes Develop multiple practical options supported by strong business cases that optimize NPV and enhance productivity and innovation Be a SPOC for all Portfolio related client inquiries and a representative in global calls and initiatives Manage expectations, timelines and delivery against Client requirements, represent both JLL and the Client’s interest on the long term Transaction Management You will execute a wide variety of real estate transactions, including capital and lease acquisitions, sale and leasebacks and disposition transactions through local Markets teams across Asia, demonstrating a high level of proficiency and knowledge. You will actively engage with client’s senior stakeholders to define objectives and agree requirements at the outset of a strategy and transaction. Financial Acumen You will need to: Prepare and evaluate financial analysis documents. Support and contribute to the client’s budget forecasting. Forecast revenue and initiate and close-out projects to meet monthly cashflow plan. Monitor and closely control legal budgets and resolve billing queries. Relationship Management You will build strong and sustainable working relationships and help the client navigate through the JLL delivery platform in an efficient and productive manner. You will be the face of Portfolio Strategy and Transaction Management towards the Client both regionally and globally. People Management You possess strong leadership skills to manage brokers across geographies and actively engage across the client’s wider corporate real estate team and internal stakeholders in a collaborative manner. You demonstrate a high level of proficiency and knowledge in portfolio management and possesses the ability to orchestrate complex activities across multiple countries/properties/services at once to accomplish goals and objectives. Commercial Acumen You will understand account profitability, formulate an account plan to achieve revenue and performance targets whilst meeting client service standards. Armed with a sharp business acumen, you will leverage on technology and new products within JLL to deliver the next generation of benefits for our client. Operating a Global Account Can you see the bigger picture? Can you juggle regional delivery that aligns to a global strategy? As the TM lead for Asia on a Global Account, you are proactive in bringing Asia into the forefront, highlighting successes or potential issues whilst providing tangible solutions for the client. Can you communicate clearly and guide the client and your team on the best way forward for best in class processes and portfolio strategies at an account level? Sound like you? Here is what we’re looking for: Great Communicator Can you get your ideas across clearly both verbally and in writing? You’ll be working with many diverse interest groups so the way you share information and your ability to persuasively convey information is highly important. A high EQ is must for this role. You will have exceptional client management, communication and presentation skills and the ability to absorb and translate broad concepts and ideas into clear and logical communications. Ambitious You will be results-oriented with confidence to liaise effectively with all levels of management. Have commitment to collaboration and teamwork, and would thrive to exceed team goals. Resilient Transaction Management is a highly dynamic part of JLL’s business. It’s fast paced and you’ll need to be fast paced as well. You’ll need a positive mind set and a ‘can do’ attitude when multi-tasking driving change and delivering efficiencies. Naturally proactive, you’ll possess the innate ability to identify risks in delivery before it becomes an issue. At JLL, we have a continuous improvement mentality across our business and we’ll be looking for you to have the same. A flexible, resourceful and proactive individual who is able to produce results and guide the team to deliver the same. You will have the ability to work well under pressure and support the team without compromising quality. Experienced You will be an experienced real estate professional with a minimum of 10-12 years’ experience who has delivered positive results across the APAC region. You will be familiar with working with fast moving MNC clients with a regional presence and multiple service lines and possess prior experience in managing a team.

Positions are limited. Please contact us to submit your application before slots are filled.

title: AVP, Real Estate Business Management & Operations (JR 16958)
description: GIC is one of the world’s largest sovereign wealth funds. With over 2,000 employees across 11 offices around the world, we invest in more than 40 countries globally across asset classes and businesses. Working at GIC gives you exposure to an extraordinary network of the world’s industry leaders. As a leading global long-term investor, we Work at the Point of Impact for Singapore’s financial future, and the communities we invest in worldwide. Real Estate We were an early entrant among institutional investors in real estate including traditional private real estate (brick-and-mortar assets), public equities, real estate investment trusts, and real estate-related debt instruments. Business Management & Operations (BMO) The Business Management & Operations team plays a vital role to provide business management and transaction execution support to Real Estate Investment Professionals globally. This includes developing governance structures for the Real Estate business, propagating industry best practices, improving efficiency by process design, coordinating deal execution and closings, and overseeing the process of identification and presenting data to ensure GIC Real Estate continues to retain its competitive edge. The team also manages the Real Estate portfolio insurance program to ensure appropriate protection of downside risks. We are looking for a suitable candidate to join our Real Estate Business Management & Operations team to develop best in class operational due diligence capabilities for the Real Estate business. What will you do as an AVP in the Business Management & Operations team? Co-ordinate the JV and vendor operational due diligence (ODD) program for the Real Estate business Support the continued development of the JV ODD program, including but not limited to process review and improvement Develop monitoring and reporting tools for better oversight of JV operational risk profile Review internal audit observations, co-ordinate business responses to internal audits and enhance governance standards for Real Estate Support periodic reviews of internal processes to enhance efficiency and / or controls, benchmarking processes to market best practices Proactively consider and implement process improvements, drive technology initiatives to achieve efficiencies and economies of scale Involved in any adhoc initiatives as directed by the Head of RE BMO What makes you a successful candidate? University degree in relevant field (business, accounting, or similar) At least 6 years of relevant work experience in internal audit, operational risk, operational due diligence or similar vocation within Financial Services or Real Estate Strong process re-engineering skills with good understanding of efficiency, internal controls and governance Detail-oriented with ability to work independently and manage projects Team player with good interpersonal and communication skills Inquisitive self-starter, with ability to work in a fast-paced environment Ability to drive co-ordination and project manage amongst multiple stakeholders Flexibility at GIC At GIC, our offices are vibrant hubs for ideation, professional growth, and interpersonal connection.  At the same time, we believe that flexibility allows us to do our best work and be our best selves.  Thus, our teams come into the office four days per week to harness the benefits of in-person collaboration, but have the flexibility to choose which days they work from home and adjust this arrangement as situational needs arise. GIC is an equal opportunity employer GIC is an equal opportunity employer, and we value diversity. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please email grphrodtaops@gic.com.sg at any point of the application or interview process if adjustments need to be made due to a disability. Learn more about our Real Estate Department here: https://gic.careers/group/real-estate/ To be considered for the role, please submit a formal application through the GIC Careers site at https://careers.gic.com.sg/job-invite/16958/

title: Online Education Marketing Specialist
description: Pickering Global Campus is the online learning platform, part of the SagePaths Group. We deliver accessible, high-quality academic and professional programmes designed for learners across Asia and beyond. As we continue expanding our digital education offerings, we are building a dedicated team to drive online growth, brand visibility, and student acquisition. This role is ideal for an individual who thrives in a start-up-style environment—resourceful, hands-on, adaptable—yet appreciates the structure and support of a well-established organisation. If you want to play a pivotal role in shaping a new digital learning initiative, this is your opportunity. Role Brief We are seeking a dynamic and ambitious Online Education Marketing Specialist to lead Pickering Global Campus’ digital presence, growth initiatives, and student recruitment strategy. You will design and execute marketing campaigns from scratch, experiment with new tactics, analyse performance, and directly influence PGC’s overall marketing roadmap. This role requires creativity, data-driven thinking, and ownership of the full digital funnel. Key Responsibilities Digital Strategy & Campaign Execution Develop and execute digital marketing strategies to grow brand awareness, leads, and online enrolments. Create multi-channel content and campaigns (email, social media, paid ads, landing pages) tailored to target audiences. Maximise use of CRM and automation tools (e.g., HubSpot/Brevo) for email marketing and behavioural journeys. Growth & Performance Marketing Run growth experiments such as A/B testing, landing page optimisation, and lead magnets to achieve rapid, measurable results. Monitor and analyse campaign performance using Google Analytics, HubSpot, and internal dashboards. Adapt strategies based on data insights to continually improve ROI and conversion rates. Content, Brand & Web Manage website content and ensure alignment with PGC’s brand direction. Collaborate with internal teams and freelancers (design, content, sales) to ensure cohesive and impactful messaging. Guide social media content planning alongside Brands & Communications for consistent digital presence. Market Research & Strategy Development Conduct market and competitor research to identify new opportunities and emerging trends. Contribute to shaping PGC’s marketing roadmap and prioritise high-impact initiatives. Support the development of new programmes by providing audience insights and demand analysis. Agile Collaboration Work closely with Product, Tech, and Sales teams to ensure marketing initiatives support the learner journey and platform features. Adjust campaigns quickly in response to performance data and operational needs. Take initiative and operate independently in a fast-paced, start-up-like environment. Requirements Minimum Bachelor degree with 2+ years of experience in digital marketing, growth marketing, or performance marketing. Strong understanding of digital fundamentals: SEO, PPC, social media, content marketing, email marketing, and funnel optimisation. Comfortable with analytics tools (Google Analytics, HubSpot, Meta Ads Manager, etc.). Creative problem-solver with a willingness to test new ideas and iterate rapidly. Excellent written and verbal communication skills; attention to detail is essential. Ability to work independently and thrive in a dynamic, entrepreneurial environment. Advantageous: Experience building/launching new online products, start-up experience, EdTech or education industry exposure.

title: Marketing & Growth Lead
description: We are a small but fast-growing tuition centre in Singapore, built on strong academic outcomes and modern, content-driven student acquisition. As a lean and ambitious team, we move fast, experiment often, and expect real ownership. We are looking for a Marketing & Growth Lead who will own the end-to-end marketing and sales process, from campaign ideation to student conversion. Role Overview This is a high-ownership role. You will be responsible not just for creating content, but for designing, championing, and executing marketing campaigns that directly drive enrolments. You will plan the full funnel — awareness, engagement, trial, conversion — and work closely with tutors to turn teaching content and TikTok Live sessions into real business growth. This role is ideal for someone who thinks like a founder or growth operator, not a task-based marketer. Key Responsibilities End-to-End Marketing & Sales Ownership Design and own the entire student acquisition funnel Plan how leads are generated, nurtured, and converted into signups Champion marketing campaigns from concept to execution and post-campaign review Take responsibility for outcomes (e.g. enquiries, trial sign-ups, enrolments) Marketing Strategy & Campaign Planning Craft a clear, actionable marketing plan aligned with enrolment targets Plan and launch specific campaigns (e.g. crash courses, exam-season pushes, TikTok Live series, free trial funnels) Identify target segments (students vs parents, level-specific strategies) Track performance metrics and continuously iterate Content Creation & Social Media Plan/design/shoot/edit/create short-form videos, images, captions, and posts Manage posting schedules and content calendars Repurpose TikTok Live content into clips for sustained reach Ensure consistency in branding, messaging, and credibility TikTok Live & Tutor-Led Marketing Plan TikTok Live teaching and engagement strategies as a key growth channel Guide tutors on structuring live lessons for engagement and conversion, communicating value clearly to students and parents, and handling basic sales messaging naturally during live sessions Coordinate live schedules and coach tutors on basic on-screen presence and messaging Beneficial Prior Experience title: Senior Finance Analyst
description: Key Responsibilities 1. Financial Modelling & Investment Analysis Build and maintain development and acquisition financial models (DCF, IRR, NPV, equity waterfall, sensitivity analysis). Analyse GDV, construction costs, land cost, financing costs, exit values and cash-flow timing. Evaluate different ownership and investment structures (direct holding, SPV, JV, fund structures). Stress-test assumptions under various market, interest-rate and sales-velocity scenarios. 2. Singapore Property Market Analysis Conduct micro-market research by district, sub-market, and asset class (residential, industrial, commercial). Track URA data, transaction trends, price indices, rental yields, take-up rates, and supply pipelines. Assess the impact of government policies (ABSD, BSD, SSD, LTV limits, cooling measures, GLS/IGLS supply). Monitor URA Master Plan and infrastructure developments affecting asset values. 3. Deal Evaluation & Due Diligence Support feasibility studies for new developments, redevelopment and value-add opportunities. Review key due-diligence inputs: valuation reports, cost plans, sales assumptions and consultants’ reports. Assess project risks (planning, construction, sales, financing, regulatory) and propose mitigation strategies. 4. Investment Committee & Stakeholder Support Prepare investment memoranda, IC papers, and executive summaries for senior management and investors. Translate complex financial outputs into clear insights and recommendations. 5. Portfolio & Performance Monitoring Track actual vs forecast performance for live projects and stabilised assets. Analyse deviations in costs, sales velocity, pricing and cash flows. Support periodic asset revaluation and portfolio reporting. Requirements Technical & Analytical Strong proficiency in Excel financial modelling (advanced formulas, scenario analysis). Solid understanding of real estate finance metrics: IRR, NPV, DSCR, LTV, ICR, GDV. Familiarity with Singapore property regulations and tax framework. Ability to interpret market data and translate it into investment insights. Professional Background Degree in Finance, Real Estate, Economics, Accounting, or related discipline. 2–5 years of experience in real estate investment, development, advisory, valuation, or fund management. Experience covering Singapore property is strongly preferred. Soft Skills High attention to detail with strong commercial judgement. Clear written and verbal communication skills (able to brief senior stakeholders). Ability to work across multiple projects and tight timelines. Strong ethics and professionalism in handling confidential information.

title: Assistant Manager/Manager - Portfolio & Asset Management (Real Estate)
description: This is a newly created position and working very closely with an established senior management team that embrace an excellent work culture of performance. They are a multi-asset firm with real estate as one of their biggest portfolios. With their value-add strategies, you will be looking to be very hands-on in managing their asset enhancement initiatives. Their portfolio will comprise of logistics, living and healthcare. You will be responsible for a wide scope of work including portfolio management, asset management, lease, project and development management. This will include formulating and executing overall fund strategy, including overseeing asset allocation and asset management plans. You will oversee, prepare and manage annual asset plan / budgets and financial reports, with the assistance of the asset management and finance teams. You will recommend asset enhancement, redevelopment, divestment opportunities and managing the sale process. You will participate in any potential development/ investment transaction process. You will be managing and work closely with the appointed property agents to ensure the assets are well-managed. You will liaise with various stakeholders in the conceptualization, formulation and implementation of asset enhancement. You will be supporting the investment team in property acquisitions, fund raising, fund management, general corporate finance work too. You will be responsible for carrying out financial modelling and assist in feasibility studies to support projects identified for major real estate asset enhancements, redevelopment and transformation. You will assist in analysing, planning of real estate assets within the portfolio to determine its highest and best use. As part of the Lease Management, you will maintain good tenant relationships and oversee in lease negotiations to ensure high occupancy rate including setting leasing strategy, re-gearing leases and negotiate surrender/re-letting opportunities. You will be responsible for driving the global leasing of properties within the portfolio, including attending to securing tenants. You will be analysing and reviewing rental, renewal options and prepositions as well as negotiation. This will include reviewing and recommending lease terms, negotiating with prospective tenants to achieve desired rent and occupancy. You will also be involved in Project and Development Management such as managing the budget, resources, scheduling etc. You will oversee site entitlement and due diligence effort for new construction and re-modelling projects across portfolio of real estate. You will participate in project scoping and budgeting as well as managing all aspects of the due diligence process including but not limited to feasibility studies, geotechnical evaluations, environmental assessments, and surveys. Requirements: Degree in Finance, Real Estate, or equivalent. Preferably at least 5 years’ experience in similar capacity either Portfolio or Asset Management or Development management Strong financial skills, analytical mind, and creative thinking Excellent verbal and written communication skills Fast learner, ability to work independently, multi-task, and execute efficiently in a fast-paced dynamic environment All applications will be treated in the strictest confidence. Personal data provided will be used for recruitment purposes only. Please apply with your CV in word document format. We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. For other positions related to real estate, please go to https://careers.tri-cap.com.sg/. EA Licence: 24S2282 EA Personnel: R1109270

title: Inside Sales Consultant
description: About Propseller In 2024, Propseller has been ranked #1 Fastest-Growing Real Estate Company in APAC by the Financial Times and #10 in Singapore’s fastest-growing companies 2024 ranking by The Straits Times and Statista! In August 2022, Propseller raised a US$12M Series A led by Vertex Ventures, the VC arm of Temasek, the investment company owned by the Government of Singapore and the Series A lead investor of Grab. Propseller’s mission is to help people build wealth with real estate. We’re building an end-to-end real estate transaction platform and towards that goal, we started the journey by building a technology-powered real estate brokerage that is valuable, reliable and fairly priced. By elevating the most human part of the service with top in-house agents and industrialising all the rest with technology and centralised operations, we became Singapore's most loved real estate agent with an average Google My Business rating of 4.8/5 (700+ reviews). The next phase of the journey is to go from helping people sell their homes to becoming the No1 brand for people to move from one home to the next: sell, buy, and finance. Find out more at: propseller.com/careers/ Role Description We are looking for a self-starter, and highly ambitious hustler to accelerate our growth and bring in more property owners and seekers into our pipeline. As an Inside Sales Consultant, you are the first point of contact with the leads (property owners and seekers), driving engaging conversations and laying the foundations for their journey with Propseller. You will be responsible for managing the pre-sales consultative selling process from initial contact, lead qualification and nurturing, to the introduction to one of Propseller’s in-house Real Estate Consultants (i.e. property agents). As a trusted advisor to the leads, you will play a fundamental role in helping them achieve their goals whilst ensuring a smooth sales process. Duties & Responsibilities Manage the pre-sales process via various channels: calls, emails, chat, etc; Introduce Propseller’s services and value proposition in a pitch to potential clients; Understand the lead’s needs and provide thorough advice about the property market, regulations, and transaction steps (you’ll get extensive training); Convert Marketing Qualified Leads who have contacted us via our website, or other channels into Sales Qualified Leads who will meet one of our Real Estate Consultants; Drive high activity volumes - make 50+ calls daily; Build strong relationships with leads and internal stakeholders; Represent Propseller with top-level professionalism; Drive Propseller growth by over-achieving KPIs and milestones; Propose, launch and deliver commercial and operational projects within Inside Sales. Ideal Candidate This is a role for you if you stand for a high work ethic and customer-centricity. You may not be a natural-born sales person but you have the hunger to learn more about sales and real estate. Unfazed by rejections, you never give up and embrace the challenge. You want to develop yourself in a constructive work environment, in which you can get frequent feedback, from your manager and your peers, and improve every single day. You cannot wait to make an impact and be part of the PropTech revolution. Requirements Bachelors’ degree with at least 1 year of relevant experience in sales, sales support or real estate OR Diploma degree with at least 3 years of relevant experience in sales, sales support, or real estate Fluent in English and Mandarin with excellent oral communication skills enabling effective communication with potential clients; Exceptional poise and presence over the phone; The drive and resilience to maintain 50+ calls per day; High level of client centricity: ability to manage multiple clients while exceeding client expectations; Comfortable with using sales tools, technologies and systems; Adaptable and flexible to work in a fast-paced environment Work authorisation is required for this role; Available to work one weekend day (Saturday or Sunday) as part of a five-day workweek, with shift hours (e.g., 8 AM - 5 PM; 12 PM - 9 PM) on a rotational basis; Nice to Have (not required): Real Estate Experience or License. You Get Central, iconic office near Lavender, Bendemeer, and Farrer Park MRTs Extensive training in Sales, Real Estate, CRMs. Be ready to learn a lot Advanced business tools: MacBook Pro, Jabra Headset, fully-integrated CRM, etc Opportunity to make history and help thousands of people with the biggest financial decision of a lifetime Compensation On-target-earning (OTE) of $4,500 - $5,500 (80% base and 20% variable) with uncapped variable bonus based on target achievement; Employee Stock Option Plan, so that you can participate in our long-term success; Medical insurance including dental with HSBC; Annual leave of 18 days. Possible Career Paths Inside Sales Consultants are Propseller’s next generation of Senior Inside Sales Consultants, Team Leads, Real Estate Consultants, and Sales Managers. Recruiting Process: 1. Introductory Call (45 min) – Online Q&A session about your experience and motivation with the Talent Acquisition Specialist Role-play exercise 2. Super Day – In Person Experience the excitement of Propseller Superday: a full day of live challenges, real coaching, and high-impact interviews designed to uncover our next top performer. One day, one chance, immediate outcome . No waiting for weeks, no delays . Demonstrate your potential and walk out with an offer on the spot! Start at 9:00 AM and finish by 5:00 PM, progressing through: Round 1: Live calls Round 2: Coached Calls Round 3: Panel Interview Round 4: CEO discussion After each round, you either move forward or are dismissed. Apply Now No reply in 3 days? Beer’s on us!

description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description: About NUS Business School Founded in the same year that Singapore gained independence, NUS Business School stands today among the world’s leading business schools. It is distinctive for offering the best of global business knowledge with deep Asian insights, preparing students to lead Asian businesses to international success and to help global businesses succeed in Asia. For more than 50 years, NUS Business School has offered a rigorous, relevant and rewarding business education to outstanding students from across the world. That would not have been possible without a group of dedicated professionals who are passionate about building a positive environment to grow and groom future business leaders. And we are looking to add to our BIZ family! To learn more about the NUS Business School, please visit https://bschool.nus.edu.sg/ Job Description As part of the Student Life and Experiential Learning team, the Manager/Assistant Senior Manager will manage the Experiential Learning components of the NUS BBA, with primary responsibility for the Field Service Projects (FSP), a credit-bearing graduation requirement that integrates academic learning with real-world business problem-solving. This role provides end-to-end leadership of the FSP ecosystem, working closely with faculty, industry partners, students, and internal stakeholders to ensure academic rigor, operational excellence, and a high-quality student experience. At the same time, this role will also work closely with the Team Lead to oversee that Business Internships (BI), a key element of the Work Experience Milestone for NUS BBA students. Programme Ownership and Strategy Provide overall stewardship of Field Service Projects (FSP), including special series FSP with distinct objectives, as a core graduation requirement of the NUS BBA. Review and enhance FSP policies, frameworks, and processes to ensure alignment with programme learning outcomes and evolving industry expectations. Contribute to broader work experience and experiential learning strategy within the undergraduate curriculum. Industry and Faculty Engagement Develop, manage, and sustain relationships with industry partners to source meaningful, high-impact FSP projects. Work closely with faculty advisors to support project scoping, academic supervision, assessment alignment, and quality assurance. Serve as the key liaison between external partners, faculty, and the School. Student Experience and Delivery Oversee student lifecycle for FSPs, including project matching, briefings, timelines, issue management, and completion. Ensure consistent and transparent communication to students on expectations, requirements, and milestones. Manage student-related escalations and provide sound judgment on complex or exceptional cases. Operations and Governance Lead and execute end-to-end operational planning and execution of FSP cycles, including schedules, project approvals, grading liaison, and graduation clearance. Ensure compliance with University and School policies, assessment standards, and audit requirements. Analyse programme data and feedback to inform continuous improvement. Stakeholder and Team Collaboration Work closely with colleagues across the Undergraduate Studies Office, Career Services (BIZCareers), faculty teams, and central units. Where applicable, supervise or guide junior staff supporting experiential learning administration and delivery. Prepare management updates, reports, and recommendations for senior leadership. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree required; postgraduate qualification is an advantage. At least 5–8 years of relevant experience in higher education administration, programme management, or experiential learning initiatives. Experience managing complex, multi-stakeholder programmes is highly desirable. Strong programme management and organisational skills, with attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple timelines. Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills, including confidence working with faculty and industry partners. Sound judgment, maturity, and discretion in handling student and academic matters. Comfortable working in a structured yet evolving academic environment. Data-informed, improvement-oriented mindset. (Appointment job grade will commensurate with the selected candidate's experience)

title: Land Surveyor

description: 🌟 Events Fun Crew – Be the Voice, Vibe & Vision of Our Events! 🌟 While we've seen the rise of online platforms as companies in every sector move towards digitalisation, more and more these companies however realize there's a gap between their digital service platform and their end user. title: Accelerated Growth Consultant, Google Customer Solutions - Singapore
description: Product area Google Customer Solutions (GCS) sales teams are trusted advisors and competitive sellers who maintain a relentless focus on customer success by bringing the best Google has to offer to small- and medium-sized businesses (SMBs), which are the backbone of our communities. As a member of our team, you’ll have the opportunity to work with company owners and make a real difference in their businesses by helping them grow.  Together, we help shape the future of innovation for customers, partners, and sellers...and we have fun doing it. Job description Businesses of all shapes and sizes rely on Google’s unparalleled advertising solutions to help them grow in today's dynamic marketing environment. You bring a passion for sales, knowledge of online media, and commitment to maximize customer success. You act like an owner, move with velocity through change, finding innovative and strategic ways to consistently deliver extraordinary and incremental outcomes for both Google and your customers. You build trusted relationships with customers, uncovering their business needs and translating them into powerful solutions to achieve their most ambitious goals. You achieve as a team with sellers, shape the future of advertising in the AI-era, and make a real impact on the millions of companies and billions of users that trust Google with their most important goals. Additional job description The Accelerated Growth Team (AGT) is a sales team designed to grow small and medium-sized businesses in South East Asia with significant expansion potential. AGT specializes in building full-funnel strategies to increase customers' return on investment, scale customer business base, and ultimately make businesses more profit. As an Accelerated Growth Consultant, you will be establishing key strategic relationships and consulting to accelerate any type of business, including startups and known brands. You provide Google-caliber client service, research, and market analysis that enables these companies to become market leaders. You strive to persistently explore and uncover the business needs of Google's highest-potential clients, developing an understanding of how the range of solutions can grow their business. Qualifications Job responsibilities Deliver against ambitious quarterly business and product growth targets, while prioritizing and delivering an outstanding experience to Google's advertisers within enSEA cluster (Singapore, Malaysia, Philippines, Pakistan). Build and manage a pipeline through proactive identification of high-potential business in addition to close partnership with the account management teams. Establish relationships via phone calls, e-mail, and face-to-face engagements, and design effective marketing strategies, and deliver outstanding customer sales experience to drive long-term success for the customers. Consult with C-level clients to achieve national and international online marketing and business goals through utilizing the full suite of Google’s marketing solutions (e.g., Search, YouTube, and AI solutions). Minimum qualifications Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. 5 years of industry experience in digital advertising, consultative sales, business development, or digital marketing, or 3 years of experience with an advanced degree. Preferred qualifications Experience in technology, account management, and achieving business quotas to drive client growth. Ability to assess and solve client needs through effective questioning, objection handling and competitive selling. Ability to manage multiple projects while paying strict attention to detail and working individually and with others. Ability to leverage data to uncover insights to support proposals and analyze impact. Excellent investigative, presentation and communication skills with peers, management, cross-functional teams and clients.

description: Business Development / Student Enrollment Manager (International School) Location: Woodleigh Working hours: 8am – 5pm / Fully on-site The Student Enrollment Manager serves as a key brand ambassador for the school, with a strong understanding of the school’s value proposition and the ability to address the diverse needs of prospective parents and students. title: Digital Marketing Specialist
description: ABOUT US: MOVE [REPEAT] is a collective of specialist boutique fitness lifestyle brands dedicated to delivering exceptional experiences across select modalities. Committed to quality, sustainable growth, and brand excellence, we aspire to be “best-in-class” in Asia. We are a movement fueled by innovation, striving to seamlessly integrate studio design, service delivery, tech, and class programming. Join us as we Build, Brand, Move, and Repeat. THE ROLE IN A NUTSHELL: As the Digital Marketing Specialist at MOVE [REPEAT], you will be responsible for the planning, execution, optimisation, and reporting of paid digital marketing efforts across our  brand portfolio. This is a hands-on, performance-driven role focused on lead generation, conversion, and retention across multiple markets, including Singapore and Hong Kong. You will work closely with the Brand & Engagement Manager and internal stakeholders to translate campaign objectives into high-performing digital executions. This role is ideal for someone who is comfortable owning campaigns end-to-end, moving quickly, and making data-led decisions. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: Paid Media Strategy & Execution (40%) Plan, launch, and optimise paid media campaigns across Meta (Facebook & Instagram), Google Ads, and other relevant platforms Own campaign structure, targeting, budgets, creatives, and optimisation strategies Execute full-funnel campaigns covering lead generation, retargeting, conversions, and retention Manage campaigns across multiple brands and markets concurrently Performance Tracking, Reporting & Optimisation (25%) Track and report on key metrics including CPA, ROAS, CAC, conversion rates, and funnel performance Analyse campaign performance and identify opportunities for optimisation and scaling Prepare regular performance reports with clear insights and recommendations Monitor trends, audience behaviour, and creative performance to inform future campaigns Performance Tracking, Reporting & Optimisation (25%) Track and report on key metrics including CPA, ROAS, CAC, conversion rates, and funnel performance Analyse campaign performance and identify opportunities for optimisation and scaling Prepare regular performance reports with clear insights and recommendations Monitor trends, audience behaviour, and creative performance to inform future campaigns Creative & Campaign Collaboration (20%) Work closely with internal creatives to brief ad concepts, formats, and iterations Translate campaign objectives into clear creative requirements Continuously test creatives, copy, formats, and audiences to improve performance Support integrated campaigns alongside CRM, content, and brand initiatives practices Platform, Tools & Market Support (15%) Support tracking setup and maintenance (Meta pixel, GA, events, basic attribution) Assist with CRM-related paid initiatives such as retargeting and lead nurturing Support regional execution across Singapore and Hong Kong Stay updated on platform changes, ad policies, and best practices REQUIREMENTS: 2–4 years of hands-on experience in performance or digital marketing Strong experience running Meta Ads independently Comfortable managing budgets, analysing data, and optimising campaigns Highly organised, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple campaigns at once Bonus: experience in fitness, lifestyle, subscription, or multi-location businesses Bonus: experience working across multiple markets WHY JOIN MOVE [REPEAT]: Own real budgets and real performance outcomes Work across a portfolio of leading fitness and lifestyle brands Fast-paced, high-autonomy environment with room to grow Opportunity to expand into a regional role as the business scale

title: Senior (Quantitative Analytics and Treasury Services) - Financial Services FAAS
description: At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture and technology to become the best version of you. And we’re counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better, too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all. The opportunity Over the last 20 years, innovative new products have dramatically increased the complexity of the financial markets, from complex preference shares issuance in venture capital world, to structured products allowing clients to repackage and transfer risk, or other more exotic instruments designed to meet almost any goal two parties can agree on. We are looking to grow our Quantitative Analytics and Treasury Services (“QATS”) team within the Financial Services - Financial Accounting Advisory Services (“FAAS”) service line. To be specific, we are looking for valuation and quant finance individuals to support Assurance teams with independent valuation and quantitative analysis on various financial instruments and investments into private enterprises. Included are opportunities to develop individual consulting skillsets and to provide services in model governance and valuation frameworks. The service we provide within EY covers a broad spectrum of responsibilities with respect to financial instruments and business valuation. You will experience ongoing professional development through your interactions with internal and external clients, in conjunction with exposure to various asset classes (e.g. FX, Fixed Income, Equity, Commodity, Interest Rate, Credit, etc.) and industry sectors (e.g. banking, private equity, hedge funds, transportation, commodity, utilities, oil & gas, REITS, e-commerce, fintech, etc). The QATS team at EY uses sophisticated techniques from the field of financial engineering and quantitative finance to assist clients in developing quantitative models for pricing, valuation, risk management and regulatory compliance purposes. Your key responsibilities: Financial Derivatives Valuation and Business Valuation Work together within a team on multiple client engagements in daily independent valuation for a diverse range of financial instruments Assist in developing valuation models to assess a variety of linear and non-linear financial instruments by adopting option pricing theory, fixed income and structured finance models, Monte Carlo simulation, statistical analysis, and other quantitative analysis Perform model validation on derivative pricing models, risk management models and model parameter calibration models Provide assistance in review of fair valuation approach, methodology, and assumptions adopted by private equity and venture capitalist clients Perform portfolio valuation review for private equity, venture capital, and asset management companies with investments Value debt instruments such as straight bonds, convertible bonds, redeemable convertible preference shares Build financial models, such as Option Pricing Model and Binomial Tree Model, to value hybrid instruments Credit Modelling Perform model development and model validations on credit risk models for Basel and IFRS 9 purposes Identify sources of model risk through comprehensive review of all model components and developmental evidence and challenging the assumptions and basis of which the components are being derived Employ statistical modelling methodologies in performing validation and produce results to be analyzed from statistical as well as business perspective. Keep updated on industry developments in terms of regulatory requirements and best practice modelling techniques Write high quality model validation reports and/or presentations Project Management Technically contribute to managing projects which includes packaging overall project findings into clear, concise and high-quality deliverables Ensure all modelling tasks are completed timely Data Analytics Design meaningful application of data analytics to be used for auditing financial numbers including developing toolkit for data mining and work paper documentation standards Use data analytics to provide actionable, timely and accurate data to drive business insights around customer, regulatory risk, compliance, operations and financial to achieve greater return on investment. General Maintain and develop positive, productive, and professional relationships with clients Build valuable relationships with external clients and internal peers Challenge yourself to continually learn and teach, mentoring others while developing your own career through various exposures in client projects Take ownership of your projects, while collaboratively working with other team members Skills and attributes for success Work effectively as a team member, sharing responsibility, providing support, maintaining communication and updating team members on progress Highly motivated, ability to multi-task, work independently under minimum supervision and deliver top-quality results in a fast-paced, dynamic environment Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills Strong written and verbal communication skills Advanced written and verbal communication skills Demonstrate integrity, values, principles, and work ethics within a professional environment Good project management skills The successful candidate must be able to display several achievements below. Within 6 months of joining, the candidate should Understand the mechanics and valuation methodology behind various derivative financial instruments and perform valuation for such financial instruments using EY developed tools Be able to explain the valuation differences between client’s and EY’s independent valuation through analyzing how various input parameters and model choices and assumptions drive the valuation output Be able to perform credit risk assessment, focusing on quantitative analysis based on financial standing, for both listed and non-listed companies Understand how various credit risk parameters are developed and validated Gain basic understanding on programming languages such as VBA and Numerix, and apply it on Lattice Model, Monte Carlo Simulation and other model implementations when working on valuation engagements and R, Python, SAS and VBA when working on credit modelling engagements. Be able to work independently with minimal supervision and take ownership of projects assigned to be under the candidate’s ownership Within 1 year of joining, the candidate should Confidently manage clients and resolve issues typically encountered by clients with regard to financial instrument and business valuations Understand the valuation approach and ability to explain the economics underpinning various types of interests and exposures in private equity and venture capitalist businesses (e.g. liquidation preferences of preference shares), and perform valuation for such financial instruments Understand the Hedge Accounting concept under Singapore Financial Reporting Standard (SFRS) framework and be able to perform the quantitative approach for the purpose of hedge effectiveness assessment Understand the Incremental Borrowing Rate concept under Singapore Financial Report Standard (SFRS) framework and be able to perform the quantitative approach for the purpose of Incremental Borrowing Rate assessment Be able to explain to general non-technical audiences on the basics of how various financial instruments function, and the benefits and risks that they bring Explain to general non-technical audiences on the rationale of the assumptions, methodologies and movements of various credit risk parameters Be adaptive and committed to an intense environment and tight deadlines in providing deliverables to stakeholders Possess excellent time management and prioritize various projects allocated to the candidate Be able to work together within the team and actively provide guidance to junior staff members in order to deliver high quality work products After 1 year, candidates who demonstrate strong competency in quantitative finance has the opportunity to join the Quantitative Development Team to develop pricing model templates To qualify for the role, you must have Bachelor's or master's degree in quantitative finance, financial engineering, finance, statistics, mathematical finance, or engineering with programming skills A good programming skill with demonstrated aptitude in quantitative analysis and understanding of at least one Finance software package (e.g. Numerix, Murex and Fincad), Bloomberg, Reuters, MS Excel/VBA Ideally, you’ll also have Prior working experience in the banking industry in valuation control, pricing/credit model development and validation, market risk function Prior working experience in global professional services organizations or mid-tier firms The Chartered Valuer and Appraiser designation or be on track to be a CVA holder all of which will be highly advantageous in your application What we look for Highly motivated individuals with excellent analytical skills and the ability to prioritize shifting workloads in a rapidly changing industry. An effective communicator, you’ll be a confident team player that collaborates with people from various teams while looking to develop your career in a dynamic organization. The successful candidate must also be committed to staying at the cutting edge of both the latest financial instruments valuation techniques, business developments and accounting standards. We're seeking leaders with the ability to quickly anticipate and respond to the issues of a networked and constantly changing global economy. What we offer Continuous learning: You’ll develop the mindset and skills to navigate whatever comes next. Success as defined by you: We’ll provide the tools and flexibility, so you can make a meaningful impact, your way. Transformative leadership: We’ll give you the insights, coaching and confidence to be the leader the world needs. Diverse and inclusive culture: You’ll be embraced for who you are and empowered to use your voice to help others find theirs.

title: Junior Customer Events Executive (training provided)
description: Company Overview We are a fast-growing organization specializing in events, roadshows, and customer engagement campaigns. We focus on developing individuals through hands-on experience, structured training, and clear career progression.
