title: Graduate Electrical Engineer-Building Services
description: The Team We are an award winning global consultancy and have been named Building’s Engineering Consultant of the Year in 2024, 2022 and 2021. We are at the forefront of the industry, priding ourselves on our exemplary track record of designing innovative and sustainable projects. Sustainability runs through everything we do at Cundall, and our people are empowered to drive change and decarbonise the built environment. By the end of this decade, we have set ourselves an ambitious goal to only work on projects that meet our zero carbon design criteria, a commitment we have called our Zero Carbon Design 2030 goal. We need engineers who share this passion for sustainability and thrive in designing energy efficient buildings and communities. Our Building Services teams at Cundall have a mission: they want to play a key role in making buildings more energy efficient, cost effective to run and, ultimately, more sustainable. To do this, we’re looking for graduates who have a desire and enthusiasm to make a difference: in our homes, our offices, our projects, our industry and our community. Working across a variety of industry sectors, our Building Services engineers are a source of world leading expertise; producing innovative and award-winning sustainable engineering solutions throughout the lifecycle of any building project. Our Building Services teams have accomplished amazing projects like an innovative office for Google, the largest arts centre in Scotland, and an energy efficient data centre for Facebook. As a graduate in the team, you could play a key part in our next big project. The Role In this Graduate Engineer role based in our Singapore office, you’ll join a team who specialise in the design of some of the most exciting construction projects in Asia and worldwide. Working closely with colleagues across our multi-disciplinary practice, you’ll provide specialist consultancy input on projects across a wide range of sectors including education, healthcare, workplace, retail and critical systems (data centres). Under the guidance of our team of experienced engineers, we’ll equip you to undertake detailed building services engineering design and analysis, defining and translating clients’ needs into designs for buildings, facilities and services - all with sustainability at the forefront of the design. You’ll have the opportunity over time to assume greater levels of responsibility and autonomy as your professional skills develop and with accredited professional development programmes from CIBSE and IET, you’ll be fully supported towards achieving chartership. The Skills Accredited with, or on course to achieve an MEng degree level (or equivalent) in either Electrical, or Building Services Engineering, you’re passionate about innovative and sustainable Electrical engineering for the built environment. Ideally, you’ll have furthered this interest by undertaking some form of relevant work experience or placement/internship. You have an appreciation for the core elements of building services engineering and crucially, have a love for creative and intuitive design. Digital engineering is a big focus at Cundall, so ideally, you’ll have an understanding of Parametric Modelling Techniques (Grasshopper or Dynamo), or at least one modern programming language (e.g. Python, C#, VB). An understanding of Engineering Optimisation Techniques, the principles of structured programming and basic knowledge in the application of Machine Learning would also be desirable. You are organised and methodical in your approach to managing your tasks and priorities, you strive to meet deadlines and have a close appreciation of the commercial factors which affect design solutions. Collaborative in nature, you’ll be able to empathise with your clients’ needs and utilise excellent communication skills, both in writing and verbally, with internal and external stakeholders. You’re used to using your initiative to overcome challenges and have a positive, can-do attitude. What’s in it for you We offer a competitive starting salary which grows as you develop, as well as many training and development opportunities to make sure you keep progressing. Aside from this, those who successfully join our graduate scheme also enjoy: Professional career development and mentoring Sponsorship for professional membership Exposure to international opportunities Graduates who are keen to ap-ply, please kindly submit your applications via our Early Careers Page: https://www.cundall.com/early-careers/roles/graduate-electrical-engineer-45

title: Manager, CRM & Marketing Efficiency
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION Kaplan Higher Education Academy (Kaplan) is part of Kaplan Inc., one of the world's most diverse education providers and the largest subsidiary of Graham Holdings Company (NYSE:GHC). Kaplan has welcomed students from over 55 countries and regions. As a Lifelong Integrated Learning Partner, we offer over 450 academic and professional certification programme options* for higher learning and skills development, providing opportunities for individuals to always stay relevant. To date, these opportunities have benefitted more than 100,000 graduates^. We have a diverse team of individuals dedicated to our mission to help individuals achieve their educational and career goals, and build futures, one success story at a time. Our core values - Opportunity, Knowledge, Support, Integrity and Results - help guide the way we work. At Kaplan, our employees are our most valuable assets, and everyone's contribution is integral to our success. We have been recognised by The Straits Times, for the second successive year, as being one of Singapore's Best Employers for 2023 . This large-scale and comprehensive study assessed the attractiveness of organisations in Singapore based on an independent employee survey. We strive to continue providing a professional, positive and stimulating work enviroment with plenty of room for initiatives, so that our employees can thrive with us.  If you are seeking opportunities for growth, we welcome you to write to us! *Based on full-time and part-time study modes across programmes offered at Kaplan. ^Total Diploma and Degree graduates from Kaplan from 2007 to 2023. RESPONSIBILITIES THE ROLE: Manager, CRM & Marketing Efficiency The Manager, CRM & Marketing Efficiency is responsible for building a high-performing CRM ecosystem that supports accurate data capture, standardised processes, effective qualification, and clear forecasting. This role also leads the optimisation of contact centre operations, including agent performance, automation, and AI-driven engagement. The manager works closely with marketing, recruitment, operations, and technology stakeholders to drive efficiency and revenue growth. Key Responsibilities: CRM Optimisation Develop and enforce CRM best practices across marketing and recruitment teams to ensure consistent, high-quality data capture. Design and maintain standardised workflows that support accurate segmentation, reporting, and lifecycle tracking. Lead ongoing CRM improvements, including field structures, validation rules, automation logic, and reporting frameworks. Monitor CRM data health and implement corrective actions to improve completeness, accuracy, and usability. Support forecasting and performance analysis through reliable CRM outputs and dashboards. Contact Centre and Qualification Matrix Optimisation Oversee daily operations of contact centre agents, including performance management, training, scheduling, and process improvements. Implement automation and digital tools to reduce manual workload and improve lead response times. Develop and scale AI-powered contact centre capabilities to support qualification, nurturing, and re-engagement. Redesign and maintain a clear qualification matrix and disqualification logic aligned with customer lifecycle stages. Drive measurable improvement in lead-to-application and lead-to-enrolment conversion. Stakeholder Management Collaborate with marketing, recruitment, operations, technology, and compliance teams to develop CRM and contact centre enhancements. Facilitate alignment between departments on lead management workflows, qualification standards, and data requirements. Communicate project plans, progress, and outcomes to senior leadership and cross-functional stakeholders. Translate business needs into actionable CRM and operational requirements for technical teams. Marketing and Sales Pipeline Efficiency Analyse pipeline performance from lead to enrolment and identify friction points across channels and stages. Partner with marketing to improve lead quality, scoring, and routing logic. Partner with recruitment to improve follow-up discipline, pipeline hygiene, and forecasting accuracy. Identify opportunities to reduce processing delays, eliminate redundant steps, and optimise contact strategies. Develop dashboards and operational frameworks that support predictable, scalable growth. Quality assurance and feedback Establish QA procedures for CRM usage, contact centre interactions, and lead-handling practices. Conduct regular audits on data quality, qualification consistency, and process adherence. Provide structured feedback and training to teams to improve CRM discipline and customer experience. Monitor feedback loops from agents, applicants, and stakeholders to refine processes and tools. Risk Management and Compliance Ensure CRM processes and contact centre operations comply with internal governance, data privacy policies, and regulatory requirements. Implement controls to reduce risk linked to inaccurate data, improper follow-up, or non-compliant communication practices. Maintain documentation for workflows, qualification criteria, disqualification reasons, and system changes. Support audits and work with legal/privacy teams on risk mitigation and data-handling best practices. Support any other tasks as assigned by Supervisor or Head of Department Assist in ad hoc projects that align with the institution's broader marketing and recruitment objectives. Respond promptly to any last-minute content needs or adjustments, ensuring flexibility and responsiveness to meet evolving marketing goals. QUALIFICATIONS Essential Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business, Analytics, Information Systems, or related field. 5+ years of experience in CRM management, marketing operations, sales operations, or contact centre optimisation. Demonstrated experience owning or implementing CRM workflows, data governance, and pipeline processes. Proven track record of improving lead quality, pipeline conversion, or operational efficiency. Experience working with cross-functional teams across marketing, sales/recruitment, analytics, and technology. Experience managing or improving contact centre operations and agent performance. Familiarity with data privacy requirements related to CRM and customer communication. Strong CRM administration and optimisation ability (Salesforce, HubSpot, MS Dynamics, or equivalent). Confident with data analysis, dashboards, forecasting, and performance reporting. Ability to design workflows, diagnose bottlenecks, and implement scalable processes. Strong organisational skills, able to manage multiple projects and stakeholders. Clear communication skills, able to translate business needs into system requirements. Ability to lead operational improvements, set standards, and enforce process discipline. Strong problem-solving mindset and comfort with building new systems from scratch. English - spoken and written as required to fulfill daily tasks Microsoft Office Desirable Experience scaling AI-driven contact centre operations. Experience in education, training, or service-based industries. Background working with compliance, legal, or governance teams on system-related risk mitigation. Experience with automation tools, lead-scoring models, or customer lifecycle frameworks. Ability to create QA frameworks, SOPs, and training modules for CRM or contact centre teams. Strong change-management capability for driving adoption of new processes or systems. Ability to present operational insights and recommendations to senior leadership with clarity and confidence. OTHER INFORMATION Working Hours: Monday to Friday, 9am to 6pm. Work Arrangements: Hybrid (Blend of in-office and remote work) Location: Wilkie Edge Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - Kaplan Higher Education Academy Pte Ltd

title: Lecturer (NUS College)
description: Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUSCareer Portal Your application will be processed only if you applyvia NUSCareer Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description NUS College is the honours college of the National University of Singapore. Our focus is a rigorous and stimulating interdisciplinary education designed to help students cultivate the necessary skills to work effectively across boundaries, We plan to hire a dedicated individual  to fill a Lecturer’s position.  This is a full-time position that includes opportunities for pedagogical research funding and for promotion to an Associate Professor on the NUS Educator Track. The primary responsibility is to offer our mandatory courses, to which you will be expected to bring your own academic and intellectual expertise. Duties & Responsibilities For a start, you will be expected to offer the following, Computational Problem Solving (NPS2001) The CPS is a common curricular course designed to introduce NUS College students to the fundamental concepts, computational tools and techniques necessary to solve complex problems. The focus is on computer science and information technology, but course objectives are more general in nature, with the goal of developing essential skills for approaching and understanding new technology to critically assess potential benefits and risks. By having a comprehensive understanding of the potential of new technologies, students will be able to formulate a problem, outline potential technological solutions and set up a team to implement the solutions that can help achieve project goals. Impact Experience Project (NEP3001) The Impact Experience (IEx) is a eight-unit capstone course that all NUS College students will have to undertake for two years. Students will work in interdisciplinary teams to collaborate with non-profit partners and innovate future solutions for the benefit of communities and society in Southeast Asia, including Singapore. Each team of 6-7 students is supervised by a lecturer who specializes in the area they are working with. Students would choose to work with lecturers clustered into three broad areas: Science and Technology, Arts and Design, Civic and Community. Applicants from any field representing the Computer Sciences are welcome to apply for this position. We are interested in candidates whose work is cross-disciplinary and can be applied to better understand and tackle social, environmental and technological problems in the Singaporean context. The selected candidate's primary responsibility will be to teach the CPS together with other course instructors to ensure that students have a common curricular experience while allowing for individual instructor creativity in their seminars. Applicants should demonstrate their ability to teach such a course by providing evidence of teaching interdisciplinary computing courses in the university. The other responsibility will be to supervise IEx student teams working on projects that involve computing for social good. Successful candidates are expected to offer 2-3 sections of Computational Problem Solving in the academic year, an elective of their own design, supervise 3-6 IEx teams, and take on a small supervision load of independent study modules. Qualifications Candidates must hold a PhD. Remuneration and benefits are highly competitive on the global research university level. Expatriate benefits may include travel assistance from overseas and limited housing benefits. Appointments begin at the level of Lecturer on the Educator Track. Although the NUS Educator Track is not a tenurable line, it offers a pathway to promotion to Associate Professorship on an open contract (and beyond), as well as opportunities for pedagogical research funding, including overseas research and conference travel. The contract is for three years in the first instance and is renewable subject to good performance. Interested applicants should prepare and submit the following documents when they apply: •    A cover letter, clearly specifying which position they are applying for. •    CV: An abridged CV (5-page maximum), including details on education, employment history, past experience of teaching and supervision, research activities (including publications) and achievements, honours and awards, and service accomplishments. •    Qualifications: Include scanned copies of academic degrees. •    Teaching Portfolio: o    A Teaching Statement that commences with a preface (maximum 300 words) that makes the case for  appointment. This should be a highly distilled summary of key contributions to student learning and, if applicable, educational leadership, guided by your teaching philosophy. The rest of the Statement, of not more than 2,000 words, should outline your teaching philosophy and your educational contribution across multiple dimensions and roles. Please include evidence on how you have impact students' learning  experience and outcomes as well as how you have influenced peers and the community. o    Teaching evaluations and peer review reports, if available. Other evidence of teaching excellence and/or other contributions to education, e.g., curriculum development, pedagogical innovation etc., should also be provided. •    A proposal, of not more than 500 words, describing a prospective syllabus for the target course. •    A statement, of not more than 500 words, highlighting how you plan to contribute to pedagogical innovation and/or advance interdisciplinary honours education at NUSC. •    FOUR referees' letters (one of whom must be the applicant's PhD supervisor). Interviews will normally be conducted online. Only shortlisted applicants will be notified. More information about NUSC and our curriculum can be found on our website: https://nuscollege.nus.edu.sg/ Additional information about Academic Appointments at NUS can be found here: https://www.nus.edu.sg/careers/

title: Executive, Sales Support
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION Constellar is Asia's preferred partner for convening businesses, curating ideas and creating opportunities for sustainable business growth and global impact. Based in Singapore, we curate and develop influential trade events for key industries and sectors, connecting people, global marketplaces and networks for sustainable growth. We also manage the Singapore EXPO, Singapore's largest purpose-built venue for Meetings, Incentives, Conventions and Exhibitions (MICE). Our vision is to be a global leader made in Asia, activating impactful networks to enable cross-industry collaboration and innovation through our holistic portfolio of intellectual property (IP) in the MICE industry. RESPONSIBILITIES Support the Directors and Managers in the effective execution of Culinary and Advertising & Promotions (A&P) sales operations. This role involves strong internal coordination and external stakeholder management to achieve revenue targets while delivering an excellent experience for venue clients and patrons. Collaborate with the A&P Manager to conceptualize and implement venue advertising and promotional strategies that build a robust sales pipeline and identify new sales opportunities. Act as a key liaison between internal operations and event services teams and external partners, including advertising agencies, media houses, printing vendors, authorities, and other relevant stakeholders, to ensure seamless service delivery. Support the Culinary Manager in conducting regular inspections, evaluations, and engagement sessions with catering consultants and partners to maintain high standards of food quality and service. Assist in managing the externally appointed operator for the Exhibitor Service Centre (ESC) platform, ensuring operational excellence and adherence to service level agreements (SLAs), including: 1) Timely response to enquiries, 2) Order processing and fulfilment, 3) Content management, 4) Pricing strategy execution and 5) Onsite collection kiosk operations. Support the accurate and timely issuance of invoices, payment tracking and payment collection according to the contractual terms. Provide general administrative and operational support as required to ensure smooth day-to-day operations. QUALIFICATIONS Diploma or equivalent in Business Administration, Hospitality, or a related field. Proven experience in an administrative or sales support role. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. Strong organizational and time-management abilities. Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook). Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Detail-oriented and able to manage multiple tasks simultaneously. Experience with contract administration is a plus. Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - Constellar Venues Pte Ltd

title: Adjunct Lecturer
description:
A premier university in Asia, the Singapore Management University (SMU) is internationally recognised for its world-class research
 and distinguished teaching. Established in 2000, SMU’s mission is to generate
 leading-edge research with global impact and produce broad-based, creative and
 entrepreneurial leaders for the knowledge-based economy. SMU education is
 known for its highly interactive, collaborative and project-based approach to
 learning, and for its technologically enabled pedagogy of seminar-style
 teaching in small class sizes. The School of Computing and
 Information Systems (SCIS) offers an
 exceptionally supportive and inspirational environment for academic
 scholarship and research, applied R&D projects, and innovative education
 initiatives. We currently have over 40 tenure-track faculty members working
 across research areas in computer science & engineering, and information
 systems management. They are complemented by over 30 practice & education
 faculty members who focus on teaching, translation and pedagogical research. Our Research. We are passionate about our research . We aim for excellence both in fundamental contribution to academic
 knowledge, as well as in creating technology with practical impact. Real-world
 industry sectors provide SCIS with a testbed and laboratory for
 experimentation, as well as a fertile breeding ground for new ideas. Our
 faculty and students apply their research results to solve real problems in a
 variety of industry settings and to create IT applications and systems. At the
 same time, our faculty actively publish in top-quality Computer Science and
 Management Science conferences and journals. On our faculty are several
 world-class scholars and rising stars in their respective disciplines. We are committed
 to supporting the development of our faculty. Besides start-up support and
 career mentoring for junior faculty, their Ph.D. students are funded by the
 school rather than paid from their research grants. SCIS faculty members are
 encouraged to collaborate on innovative research projects and also on
 integrative and interdisciplinary projects. SMU as well as Singapore
 government agencies, such as the National Research Foundation, provide
 generous support for high-quality research proposals. Our Education. SCIS offers the Ph.D. in Information Systems and Ph.D. in Computer Science programmes. Ph.D. students can
 concentrate in any of our computer science oriented research areas, or in our
 management science oriented Information Systems & Management research
 area. SCIS launched a
 Professional Doctoral Degree, Doctor
 of Engineering (EngD) in 2020. The
 programme aims to train students to become IT leaders with deep technical
 expertise for innovating, designing and managing complex IT systems. SCIS also
 offers Master of IT in
 Business (MITB), a professionally oriented
 coursework programme. Drawing from thought leadership in the ambits of data
 analytics, technological platforms and business strategies, the MITB programme
 delves into five specialisation tracks: Financial Technology & Analytics, Data
 Science & Analytics, Artificial Intelligence, Digital Transformation and
 Cybersecurity. Each of these tracks uniquely resources students to lead
 competently and decisively . Additionally, the
 undergraduate programmes offerings consist of the B.Sc.
 (Computer Science) , B.Sc.
 (Information Systems), B.Sc.
 (Computing & Law) and B.Sc. Software Engineering programmes. The programmes have been remarkably successful in
 demonstrating educational innovations and creating a culture of learning,
 establishing external linkages and partnerships with industry, government and
 the social sector, and with job placement. We have an entrepreneurial student
 body, and many SCIS undergraduates have gone on to create or participate in start-ups . Job Requirement Candidate
 requirements are as follows: Capable of
      delivering postgraduate courses in the area of Payment
      Technology and Operations. Masters degree or PhD
      degree in a closely related discipline from a well-recognized university . o   For example, a
 Masters or PhD in Computer Science, Information Technology, Information
 Systems, Statistics, Management Science, Decision Sciences, Marketing
 Analytics, Computational Physics, Computational Engineering or Science, and
 the like. Outstanding
      communication skills A
      passion for learning and teaching Prior
      experience with consulting, training, or teaching is a strong advantage.

title: Senior Manager/Director - Acquisitions (Real Estate)
description:
We are recruiting for APAC Logistics Fund in modern industrial and logistics real estate in key cities in Asia Pacific. Reporting to the Head of Acquisitions, you will have the opportunity to work in a lean environment where you will be involved in the full cycle of investment and asset management such as site acquisition, underwriting and due diligence, asset management and divestment. Your main responsibilities will include deal origination, business case proposal, deal execution and supporting the asset and fund management team. You will be sourcing and evaluating of new business cases which includes but not limited to Green/brown field acquisition or development, Divestment, Asset Enhancement, Joint Venture opportunities, research and understand and penetrate new markets or product type. You will prepare and underwrite business case upon understanding of market and real estate parameters of the deal, building financial models and providing relevant supporting information to justify the case. Deal structuring will include other aspects like JV formation, banks, tax, finance and treasury. This will also include negotiation and overseeing due diligence. You will also recommend parameters in investments to manage risks and generate acceptable rate of return. You will work on crafting teasers, investment papers for approval to stakeholders. Requirements: You possess a Degree in Real Estate, Engineering, Finance or Business or related field from a recognised tertiary provider and have at least 8 years of solid experience in related investment scope such as capital budgeting and related analysis i.e., IRR, NPV, DCF, Yields. Deal experience/track record are essential for this role as they are looking to scale up with exciting growth ambitions. You possess solid understanding of financial investment evaluation and capital decision making in the Industrial or logistics real estate industry. You are commercially sound with knowledge of Asia real estate market including properties, rents, cost structures etc. You are highly numerical with advanced financial modelling skillsets. Excellent interpersonal and communication skills will also be held in high regards. All applications will be treated in the strictest confidence. Personal data provided will be used for recruitment purposes only. Please apply with your CV in word document format. We regret to
inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. For other positions related to real estate or other alternative investments, please go to https://careers.tri-cap.com.sg/. EA Licence: 24S2282 ​EA Personnel: R1109270

title: Senior ICT Engineer
description: Mott MacDonald Singapore currently has a brilliant opportunity for a Senior ICT Engineer to join an international global, management and development engineering consultancy, with opportunities for excellence every day. ​ Part of an innovative, global community of practices, solving important challenges. ​ Contributing to advancing our economic, environmental and social sustainability goals. ​ Where achieving excellence is celebrated​ We currently have an exciting opportunity for a driven Senior ICT Engineer to join our Buildings market in Singapore. ​ As a Senior ICT Engineer, you will be experienced in integrated smart building systems and services. In this role you will find opportunities for strong career progression by contributing to boutique and large scale local and international projects. As the member of our team, you will have experience in a similar role and will be given a degree of autonomy and responsibility that suits a self-starting and self-reliant person who can deliver ICT/ELV design and supporting multi-discipline development, such as commercial, residential, hospital, hotel, leisure, mixed-use, critical facilities, data centers, airports, etc. You also will have opportunity to be involved in advisory work, providing advice and high-level consultancy services to clients from various industry. We work as a connected global team, delivering impactful work that is shaping the future across design and delivery, and it is our people who are powering this change. As a proudly employee-owned business, we’re here to benefit our clients, each other and our local and global communities. Our progress enables us to invest in creating the right space for everyone to feel empowered, supported and valued. What you will typically do: Client and stakeholders management and relationships. Communicating with clients, manage clients’ requirements and expectation in alignment with the project’s contractual scope. High-level and conceptual architecting of “state-of-the-art” integrated smart building/precinct systems and infrastructure, incl. client consulting, requirement management, conduct studies, options, gap analysis and solutioning. Design and development of ICT/ELV infrastructure and systems, including spaces and pathways, fiber and copper structured cabling system, communication networking systems, integrated BMS platform, integrated security systems, building systems and data integration. Collaborate on the development of the design package for tendering, incl. functional and performance specification, schedules, drawings, integration and interface management plan, interface coordination, integration and testing plan, etc. Support the tendering process and supervise the implementation on behalf of client. Fluent use of MS Visio, PowerPoint, AutoCAD, Revit, Revu and other equivalent mark-up/engineering tools for drawing, report, and presentation production. About You: You will be a qualified Electrical, Electronics or ICT/Telecommunications Engineer or equivalent who is experienced in the design and specification of ICT/ELV systems and services within buildings. This role ideally suits someone with 4+ years of relevant experience and looking to join a growing team that holds technical excellence to high regard and offer an excellent opportunity for career progression. To be the right person for the role, you will have: Degree in Engineering Current and proven experience as ICT/ELV Engineer with design experience within Buildings, Data Centres, Mission Critical Facilities, Airports, Commercial, Transport sectors. Knowledge and experience in: Building ICT infrastructure (incl. customer-owned Outside Plant) – required Network and communication systems – required Integrated Building Management System – required Building ELV Systems - required ICT/ELV and Building Systems and Data Integration - required Smart Building, IoT, Data Analytics – required Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning – highly desirable Cybersecurity – highly desirable Sustainability - highly desirable ICT/ELV industry certification (e.g. credential from BICSI, WiredScore/SmartScore, EPI, CNET, CompTIA, UTI, Cisco, etc.) – highly desirable Knowledge of Building Industry Standard and regulations Highly developed interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills are essential. Enthusiastic, self-motivated, a team player and customer focused

title: Civil & Structural Desigjn Engineer Manager
description: Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition Ciwise Consultants - Our Operating office is at Ubi Rd. We are an international design and project management firm offering a wide range of professional services in design and consulting in the specialist field of building, infrastructure, micro-tunnelling, underground space, and trenchless technology. It brings together a team of highly specialized professionals with diverse disciplines and strong international experience. Our Strength We emphasize technical excellence in all our work, leveraging integrated multidisciplinary approaches to benefit our clients and projects. Our proven track record covers new builds and existing buildings. We believe our international experience positively impacts projects. We invest in technologies and skills to keep our team updated with international trends. Design Culture We foster a conducive working environment that promotes innovative ideas and knowledge sharing. Our team maintains skills and knowledge across a wide spectrum of design. Honest and Ethical Business Practice We highly value honest and ethical business practices at all levels of our team. Our most valuable awards are client feedback recognizing us as an “honest and trusted firm.” Ciwise Consultants values long-term partnerships with a diverse and growing customer base, including public and private stakeholders, government agencies, and educational institutions. Clients include PUB, LTA, NEA, HDB, JTC, Jurong Port, LARSEN & TOUBRO, Italian Thai Development Pty Ltd, JKR Malaysia, MATRADE, Chip Eng Seng Corporation Ltd, China Construction (South Pacific) Development Co Pte Ltd, Gammon, KTC Group, Hock Hai Group, and Good Link Group. Principal Officers Er. Cheng Kim Hua, Principal Engineer Er. Cheng is a registered Professional Engineer in Singapore and Malaysia as an ASEAN PE. He has 49 years of experience in engineering and construction, including planning, design, construction supervision, and business development of civil, infrastructure, trenchless, tunnel works, and structures. His work spans Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, Vietnam, India, and the Middle East. He has served as vice president of the Institution of Engineers, Malaysia, Board Member of the Highway Authority, Malaysia, and the Board of Engineers, Malaysia. He has contributed to the continuing education committee of the PE Board, Singapore, and served as Treasurer of the Singapore Concrete Institute. He is also a Chartered Management Accountant, UK. Applicants please submit your application by email to Qpcheng@gmail.com and/or WhatsApp +6594551399, Attn: Er Cheng. Please state your current and expected salary level, and your notice period. Job Summary Lead and execute engineering studies and design for civil infrastructure projects, ensuring compliance with authority codes and standards. Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams and stakeholders to deliver innovative, compliant, and timely engineering solutions. Responsibilities Conduct comprehensive engineering studies to analyze civil infrastructure project requirements and guide design decisions and project planning Develop civil, structural, infrastructure, and underground engineering designs that comply with current authority codes, standards, and submission requirements Coordinate design activities with Quality Planning Department (QPD), Quality Project Supervision (QPS), clients, and authorities to secure approvals and ensure effective supervision on site Manage and collaborate with internal design disciplines and sub-consultants to produce accurate design calculations, drawings, and technical reports for client and authority submissions Communicate design concepts, alternatives, and technical information clearly and persuasively to clients and approving agencies to facilitate timely design approvals and project progress Apply interpersonal skills to engage effectively with multidisciplinary teams and stakeholders, fostering collaboration and alignment on project objectives Utilize analytical and problem-solving skills to identify and resolve design and project challenges, ensuring solutions meet quality and compliance standards Demonstrate technical competency and project management capabilities to deliver projects within defined scope, schedule, and quality requirements Required competencies and certifications Possess Registered Engineer (RE) qualifications as mandated by the Professional Engineers Board (PEB) of Singapore Hold a Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering or equivalent, recognized by the Professional Engineers Board (PEB) of Singapore Preferred competencies and qualifications Hold additional certifications in data analytics, Building Information Modeling (BIM), or proficiency in relevant design software such as STAAD, Plaxis, Python, and SQL Demonstrate a minimum of 5 years of design experience in civil and infrastructure-related works; 8 years for managerial roles

title: Principal Manager, Incentives Management (SkillsFuture Credit and Programme Operations Management) (LIMD)

Responsibilities and Competencies: Policy-Operations-Technology Integration Strategic System Alignment : Ensure operational processes are supported by technology systems and aligned with SSG’s funding policies and strategic intent Operational Policy Development : Formulate and review operational policies to support programme implementation and grants management, translating policy directives into operational guides Continuous Improvement Management: Implement systematic refinements to enhance operational effectiveness while maintaining compliance and quality standards. System Development Coordination: Coordinate with internal divisions and service providers to manage system development, change requests, problem resolution, including validating requirements and testing. Vendor Performance Management: Develop workflows and work instructions for outsourced providers to implement the programmes; monitors service provider performance in operational delivery and customer service. Performance Monitoring and Reporting Performance Analytics and Reporting: Monitor, track and report programme performance including Service Level Agreements indicators and Key Performance indicators, translating metrics into actionable insights for management. Risk Assessment and Mitigation: Identify areas of risks, devise mitigating measures, and establishes control measures to ensure operations align with intended policy parameters. People Management Nurtures and provide guidance to team members and develops their leadership and functional capabilities through mentoring and coaching. What we are looking for You are passionate about solving customer issues, detail-oriented with key strengths in operations management and work processes. You are a people person and is committed to nurture, develop and give guidance to your team members. Proficient in executing job duties with required competencies. At least 7 years of relevant work experience in operations, programme or project management. Strong people management skill, with at least 3 years in a supervisory role Strong customer service attitude Strong in analysis, communication (both verbal and written), interpersonal, process and project management skills Able to multi-task, work independently and perform well under pressure and tight timelines Note: This is a team lead role. Successful candidates will be offered a 1-year contract with potential for a 1-year extension and consideration for permanent tenure thereafter. Candidates are encouraged to sign up for a Careers & Skills Passport (CSP) account and include your CSP public profile in your resume. Please check out www.myskillsfuture.gov.sg for details on the CSP.

title: Integrated Media Manager
description: As a leading global media network, with data at the core, Omnicom Media Group APAC (“OMG”) creates end-to-end solutions for clients, anywhere, swiftly and efficiently. We consider client business needs from the start and keep consumer behavior at the heart of everything we do. We are compromised of the full-service media networks OMD and PHD as well as a number of specialty media communications companies. We offer unparalleled clout in the marketplace and a depth of capabilities and experience that drive leadership and innovation in every media type. Omnicom Media Group is the media services division of Omnicom Group Inc. (NYSE: OMC), the leading global advertising, marketing and corporate communications company, providing services to over 2,500 clients in more than 100 countries. Scope of role: The Account Management Community is at the very heart of the business and critical to its market perception and business health. This community is therefore tasked with creating a recognized market-leading position in implementational / executional management and client servicing. Reporting Structure: You will report to the Account Director. You are expected to manage your team (of an Account Executive) as well as any specialist delivery services that are being deployed in your business. The role encompasses: 1. Account management and campaign leadership. 2. Involvement in briefing, ideation, and media strategy development process. 3. Identifying innovative collaboration opportunities for campaigns. 4. Identifying growth opportunities for our investment portfolios through strategic planning. 5. Ensuring error-free implementation of media plans across teams. 6. Optimizing campaigns in real-time to address business needs and challenges. 7. Delivering operational excellence by adhering to timelines across deliverables such as reporting, invoicing, etc. 8. Training and development of direct reports. 9. Proactive sharing of learnings with wider teams to ensure best practices are being implemented. 10. Networking and media partnerships. Key Responsibilities: 1. Origination of the Media Plan Working with the Account Director and client to create clear and coherent plans that bring the media strategy to life. In-depth assessment of all candidate media options and detailed recommendation of proposed communication solutions. Involvement in creative development workshops as required. Working closely with the Account Director in your team to ensure smooth and relevant translation from strategy to plan. Assess, identify, and recommend creative media initiatives that deliver greater strategy depth and impact to the media plan. Undertake the implementation of agreed initiatives, including the briefing of third parties, as and when required. 2. Management of the implementational process and client relationship management Origination of detailed briefs for relevant third-party units (e.g. the Buying Unit, Direct etc), working withthe Account Director. Day to day management and ownership of implementation process – ensure brief is delivered / brought to plan, within appropriate criteria (budget, target etc.). Principal clients’ contact for the whole implementation process (from presentation of detailed recommendations to negotiation and inventory management issues). Collation and reporting of competitive data (when relevant). Timely delivery and upkeep of all relevant reporting systems and templates (media plans, contact reports, status reports). Post campaign reporting that quantifies the value of the media strategy. 3. Development and direction of Account Executive(s) and Planner(s) Clear delegation and clarity when it comes to all basic tasks so Manager is able to step up taking on more responsibility from Account Director. Protecting our high standards Ensure that any work that leaves the agency is of the highest standard. Be part of producing ideas that inspire, motivate, engage and create commercial action. Be part of producing award winning work. Always challenge “mediocrity”. Integration Think like a solutions provider. Ensure you encourage the use of other agency services. Experience required: Tertiary qualified, 4-5 years of industry experience strong media planning & buying experience. Good understanding of media fundamentals and strategic planning capabilities. Experience in managing a team would serve as an advantage. Sound understanding of digital media platforms is a plus. Strong client facing communication & presentation skills. Attributes: Passionate, with strong opinions about communications. An instinctive desire to exceed expectations (clients and management) and deliver cutting-edge strategies. Ability to build strong relations with colleagues, clients, network partners and external agencies. Can demonstrate good organizational skills and attention to detail. Can multitask and change priorities rapidly and is effective under pressure.

Job Summary The Building Manager leads the comprehensive management and operational oversight of the building, ensuring compliance with statutory requirements and delivering high standards of maintenance and service. Reporting to senior leadership, you will drive financial stewardship, coordinate cross-functional teams, and engage stakeholders to enhance property value and resident satisfaction. Responsibilities Serve as the primary liaison to the council and residents by actively listening to inquiries and delivering effective solutions to improve community satisfaction Maintain a professional and courteous demeanor in all communications to foster a positive and supportive environment Manage overall office administration to ensure organized and efficient operations Advise the extraordinary meetings to support transparent governance Handle inquiries, complaints, and correspondence related to the Corporation, property, and subsidiary proprietors with timely and accurate responses Coordinate and engage contractors, solicitors, auditors, architects, engineers, and other professionals with prior Corporation approval to execute maintenance and project works Act on behalf of the Corporation in responding to notices or orders from public or statutory authorities concerning the property Collect and recover all monies due to the Corporation, initiating legal proceedings when necessary to secure payments Ensure timely payments to contractors and service providers for property upkeep, statutory charges, licenses, professional fees, rent, insurance, and taxes Maintain accurate financial records of all monies received and expended on behalf of the Corporation Prepare and submit annual budgets to determine expenses and recommend contribution rates for management and sinking funds Deliver financial reports on contractual matters governing the property to the Corporation for informed decision-making Review service contracts for cost-effectiveness and advise the Council on necessary changes or renewals Monitor utilities consumption and implement timely actions to achieve cost savings or recover overpayments Oversee day-to-day building operations, ensuring all maintenance and administrative staff perform to the highest standards expected by clients and subsidiary proprietors Lead teams responsible for maintenance, finance, security, fire safety, and car park management to deliver seamless property services Ensure all contract works and routine servicing comply with contractual agreements and quality standards Review contracts, agreements, and insurance policies before expiry and recommend renewals or initiate quotations and tenders Coordinate council and general meetings to facilitate effective stakeholder engagement Manage the property’s condition to comply with all relevant legislation, standards, and requirements, including BMSMA and cautionary practices Track and resolve estate matters arising from AGM/EOGM, Council, Sub-committee meetings, and resident feedback from planning through execution to closure Develop estate work plans with Portfolio Manager guidance for approved projects, major events, and tasks aligned with AGM cycles Assist in preparing specifications, calling tenders, evaluating bids, and recommending contractors to the Corporation Conduct regular property inspections to verify maintenance quality and service provider performance Implement preventive maintenance programs approved by the Corporation to sustain property condition Manage site staff performance, behavior, and attitudes to uphold the Corporation’s best interests Maintain regular communication with Portfolio Manager and Business Unit Head on major issues, breakdowns, complaints, project progress, emergencies, and liabilities You are someone who has at least 3 years of full-time wortking experience with at least 1 year of experience in organizing events from beginning to the end as a project leader 2. You are independent and proactive in getting things done without much supervision. You are honest, meticulous, responsible and mature in personality 3. title: Market Strategist & Presenter
description: So, who are we? Hello, we're IG Group. We are a publicly traded FTSE250 FinTech company who run mobile, web and desktop platforms that help our clients trade stocks & shares, leveraged products, Futures & Options and Crypto. We are ambitious. Over 340,000 people already use our platforms. We're global with offices in 18 countries and products in 16 regions. We're hungry to move faster, ship better product for our customers and grow our user base. We believe in high autonomy, and we want people who are looking to do things differently in order to create better experiences for our customers. We work in cross-functional teams and are laser focused on increasing the number of active clients we serve to drive sustainable growth. Your role in the team We are seeking a dynamic and insightful Market Strategist and Presenter to join our team. You’ll be the market face of IG Group in Singapore, delivering actionable insights, building thought leadership, and engaging with the media and the trading community. You’ll also help support our content strategy and represent IG in media and public forums – bringing expert-level credibility and personality to every screen, page, and platform you’re on. As a Market Strategist & Presenter at IG Group, you’ll be both the voice and face of the company—delivering sharp, actionable insights that resonate with traders. You’ll shape our market narrative and drive client engagement through timely analysis, compelling reports, media appearances, engaging seminars, and social content. This is a high-impact, high-visibility role for someone who thrives on real-time market moves and knows how to transform analysis into actionable insights. What You’ll Do Market Commentary : Deliver daily and weekly market commentary, providing actionable insights on macroeconomic trends and trading strategies. Content Creation : Write articles covering significant market events, including US earnings, BOJ meetings, and Singapore banks' earnings. Live Hosting : Host live shows and video content on social media channels to discuss market trends and events. Social Media Engagement : Provide regular content updates across Twitter, Telegram, and Instagram to grow the company’s social media presence. Media Representation : Act as the go-to spokesperson for market views in external media, including radio and TV interviews, to share insights and build brand credibility. Webinars and Live Streams : Host regular market webinars and live streams to drive trader engagement and education. Client/ Public Events : Support and participate in client events to enhance engagement and foster brand loyalty. Who We’re Looking For You’re a natural communicator who thrives in the market cycle and knows how to turn complex themes into compelling stories. You're equally comfortable writing macro commentary, appearing on Bloomberg or CNA, or jumping into a live market call on social. You understand the stakes, the speed, and the scale of what IG clients want – and you're ready to meet that moment, every time. What You’ll Need for This Role Education: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in finance, Economics, Business, or a related field. Professional certifications such as CFA, CMT, or similar are highly desirable. Experience : A minimum of 3 years of direct experience in a market analysis or related role within the financial industry, OR Certifications : Successful completion of the RES5 and CM-CMP examinations. Strong understanding of macroeconomic trends and financial markets. Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to distill complex information into clear, actionable insights. Proven ability to create engaging written and video content for diverse audiences. Comfortable with public speaking and media appearances, including radio, webinars, and TV interviews. Proficiency in leveraging social media platforms (Youtube, Telegram, Instagram) to build brand presence. Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple priorities effectively. How we work We try to take a thoughtful approach to our ways of working as a company. When it comes to how we deliver, there are 5 things we want everyone to do to drive high performance, better learning and career satisfaction: Lead and Inspire: Drives trust, alignment, and enthusiasm Think Big: Focus on the problems that most impact commercial outcomes Champion the client: Understand and prioritise client's needs Deliver at pace: Push for fast, sustainable growth; Raise the bar: Take ownership, be accountable and share feedback We believe that diversity is vital to success, it fuels creativity, drives innovation and sets us up for global success. We're committed to building teams with a variety of perspectives and skills to help us realise our vision and strategy, that's why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join us on this journey. Learn more about our D&I approach here . The Perks Your growth fuels our success! Thrive with tailored development programs, mentoring opportunities with leaders, and clear career progression. Expand your network through committees, sports and social clubs. Enjoy extra time off for volunteering and community work. Flexible working hours and work-from-home opportunities. Performance-related bonuses. Insurance and medical plans. Career-focused technical and leadership training in-class and online, incl. unlimited access to LinkedIn Learning platform. Contribution to gym memberships and more. A day off on your birthday. Two days’ volunteering leave per year. Join us for this exciting journey. Apply now!

title: Senior/Principal IT & AV Design Consultant
description: The Team Our dedicated ICT Consultancy (sitting within our wider Technology Group) plays a key role in digitally enabling the built environment to provide the technologically enhanced solutions commensurate with our clients’ needs. We pride ourselves on being a market leader in our field, working globally with high profile clients in data centres, fintech, HQ offices, and smart buildings. Our reputation has led to us advising on global technology standards for multi-national companies and benchmarking and technological certification of buildings across the globe. We pride ourselves on our ability to solve complex technical problems, demonstrate adaptability, work collaboratively as a team, Practice and Company, and share knowledge, problems, and solutions as a team to harness the power of all minds and perspectives. Operating globally, our Team is based in the heart of Singapore near Orchard Central, Central London right next to St Paul’s Cathedral, and in Dubai overlooking to Dubai Marina. The Role This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced and client-focused consultant to join the team at our Singapore office, to play a key role in ensuring the successful delivery of innovative ICT design solutions for some of the most exciting and high-profile projects in Singapore and internationally. Data centres are a key growth area for Cundall, and you will have the opportunity to take ownership of our new and existing projects in the APAC region. In this role, you will have the opportunity to work closely with senior and junior members of the team and wider project teams. You will lead the design and implementation of ICT and Telecoms systems for not only data centres, but across a range of different projects and sectors, with oversight of AV and Smart Building systems where required. As a Senior or Principal Consultant, you will play a key role in requirements capture, technical assessments, and developing strategies and solutions that meet project objectives, industry standards and best practices. You will have the opportunity to contribute to the development of ICT masterplans, site assessments and feasibility studies, design specifications, all whilst collaborating with internal and external multidisciplinary teams. Through your foundational knowledge of ICT systems, based on a strong academic background with plenty of work-based experience, you will: Act as technical lead for allocated projects, coordinating the production of designs with relevant team members, ensuring that designs conform to client requirements, health and safety laws, industry standards and best practice. Design ICT physical infrastructure to support passive, and at times active systems, such as structured cabling systems, outside plant, ICT rooms, Wi-Fi, containment systems and risers. Coordinate the production of project drawings (e.g. schematics, layouts, installation details, rack elevations etc.)  using Autodesk Revit and AutoCAD, in accordance with company and project standards Lead the QA and QC process as a checker and verifier. Develop complete design packages including detailed drawings, calculations, technical specifications, employer’s requirements, and tender documentation. Be involved with construction site visits to undertake compliance monitoring roles of ICT works, working alongside other disciplines. Collaborate within the wider Cundall team and external partners, working closely with colleagues of various disciplines to support the delivery of technically excellent and successful multi-disciplinary projects. Support the senior management staff within the team, embracing the collaborative and supportive behaviours Cundall exhibit. This includes line management, team mentoring and chartership mentoring. Contribute to commercial planning and management of projects. The Skills Educated to degree level in Electrical Engineering, ICT or Computer Science (MEng, BEng or BSc), and working towards, or already be, a member of a professional institute. You will be aiming for a career in ICT and telecoms systems design and construction in the built environment. We look for 5-8 years of experience working in an engineering design consultancy or for a contractor. Desired traits for the successful candidate include: Ability to take ownership for tasks and deliverables. Capacity to problem-solve and help guide the team and client towards solutions Organised and confident communicator, with the ability to gather client requirements and lead internal and external meetings Knowledge and experience in designing ICT and telecoms systems: Passive ICT systems, such as structured cabling systems and containment. Active ICT systems, such as Wi-Fi and GSM. Hyperscale and colocation data centre telecoms infrastructure, including outside plant. Satellite communications, antennas, and aerials. MEP facilities for ICT, including power and cooling. Interface between ICT and other building systems, including BMS and Security. Experienced in leading site visits for surveys, witnessing and commissioning, to support design work. Understanding of project resourcing and commercial management would be advantageous. Working knowledge of drawing and model production using Autodesk AutoCAD and Revit. Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications. Excellent communication skills in written and spoken English. WiredScore and/or SmartScore Accredited Professional would be an advantage. Interest or ability to code in languages such as Dynamo and Python would be desirable.

title: Finance / Accounting Trainer (Freelance/Part Time)
description: Position Overview: WLP Academy is looking for a skilled and experienced Accounting/Finance Trainer to join our team. The Trainer will play a key role in providing top-notch training, ensuring adult learners achieve effective learning outcomes and assessments. This role demands a strong commitment to upholding assessment integrity while contributing to a positive and engaging learning environment. The ideal candidate will be passionate about teaching and dedicated to promoting a culture of excellence. Training Delivery: •	Participate in curriculum improvements and deliver course content in alignment with WLP’s curriculum and learning standards. •	Facilitate manual and digital assessments to evaluate and track learner performance and progress. •	Delivery of Core Accounting Concepts: Double Entry, Accounting Standards & Accounting Cycle •	Financial Statement Analysis: Guide learners on how to read and interpret the Profit & Loss (Statement of Comprehensive Income) and Balance Sheet (Statement of Financial Position). •	Interpretation of financials: Teach calculation of key financial ratios (Current Ratio, ROI, Gross Margin) to assess business health. •	Data Analytics & Visualization: Guide learners on extracting raw financial data and transforming it into actionable insights. •	Ensure learners meet the specified course learning objectives through engaging and effective teaching methods. •	Conduct engaging, practical training sessions (in-person, virtual, or hybrid). •	Adapt delivery style to suit different audience types (e.g., professionals, entrepreneurs, junior staff). •	Encourage participant interaction, learning, and real-world application. •	Provide practical exercises and simulations in financial statements analysis Assessment Integrity: •	Uphold the highest standards of assessment integrity, ensuring that assessments follow WSQ guidelines and internal policies. •	Ensure that all assessment questions are marked, and learners' answers are accurately evaluated. •	Verify that all questions are answered and attempted by learners. Incomplete responses should not be marked as “pass.” •	Conduct and properly document oral recovery sessions, ensuring transparency and consistency. •	Prevent instances of “flashing” answers and address any behaviors that compromise assessment integrity. •	Ensure incorrect answers are not marked as "pass"; only correct answers aligned with the assessment criteria should be considered. Administrative Responsibilities: •	Complete and submit Assessment Summary Records (ASRs) and other required documentation in a timely manner. •	Maintain accurate records of attendance, assessments, and learner-related data. •	Ensure that all training materials remain up-to-date and relevant in accordance with industry standards. Learner Support and Engagement: •	Provide constructive feedback on assessments and offer guidance for learners to improve. •	Address learner concerns professionally, promoting a supportive and positive learning environment. •	Foster inclusivity and respect in the classroom, ensuring equal learning opportunities for all students. Requirements •	ACLP (Advanced Certificate in Learning and Performance) or ACTA (Advanced Certificate in Training and Assessment) certification is required. •	Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. •	Professional certification (e.g., CA, ACCA, CPA) is a plus. •	At least 3–5 years of practical accounting experience. •	Strong presentation, facilitation, and interpersonal skills. •	Prior training or teaching experience is highly desirable. •	Ability to simplify complex financial concepts for diverse learners.

Our team in Singapore is growing rapidly – and we know this success is made possible by our people. That’s why we make sure our people have the right tools and environment to thrive. Each individual at Grant Thornton Singapore is empowered to be themselves within an inclusive team. Our supportive structure gives our people the autonomy to shape their careers and drive positive change the firm. It’s an exciting time to join us as we grow with organisations of all sizes and industries to create change in Singapore, across the region and in the world. The Valuation team Our Valuation team blends technical expertise with a pragmatic outlook to deliver support during the financial reporting process, transactions, restructuring and disputes. The Valuations team creates meaningful value tailored to the needs of each client and is agile and adaptable to market developments. Responsibilities Perform valuations for businesses, intangible assets, financial instruments, etc., for the purpose of financial reporting, transactions, and litigation support Conduct market and industry research, construct and analyse financial models, and ensure best practices and quality in valuation work Support in pitching new projects, including preparation of proposals, engagement letters, marketing materials, and technical documents Perform purchase price allocations for post-transaction financial reporting purposes. Prepare comprehensive valuation reports and review memos which meet the requirements of internal and external clients. Assist in client management, practice development, and business development activities Build and maintain client relationships before, during, and after engagements Mentor and conduct training sessions for team member Qualifications and skills At least 5 years of relevant experience in valuation advisory in a professional services firm Recognised degree in Accounting, Finance, Business or other related fields or equivalent professional qualifications Familiar with Singapore Financial Reporting Standards, IFRS and US GAAP Strong written and verbal communication and presentation skills Good understanding of business valuation concepts and the ability to analyse situations and recommend valuation approaches and next steps Familiarity with key valuation methodologies Strong work ethic and ability to adapt to new challenges in a collaborative team environment Excellent problem-solving skills and ability to prioritise client engagements

title: Senior Construction Operations Manager - Founding Member, New Strategic Business Unit
description: An Exceptional Opportunity to Shape the Future of a Growing Medium-Sized Enterprise's Greenfield Construction Venture in Singapore The Company: A successful and growing medium-sized enterprise with a strong track record in related industries is strategically establishing a new construction business unit in Singapore to capitalize on significant market opportunities. This is a unique and high-impact opportunity to be a foundational member of this venture, leveraging the established reputation and agility of a thriving company while building a dynamic and focused operation from the ground up. We are seeking an exceptional and entrepreneurial Senior Construction Operations Manager to spearhead site operations, drive business development, and establish robust regulatory and HR frameworks. The Role: As the Senior Construction Operations Manager, you will be a pivotal figure in the success of this new strategic business unit. This multifaceted role demands a seasoned construction professional with a proven track record in managing complex projects, a strong business acumen for client acquisition, and a deep understanding of Singapore's regulatory landscape, particularly within the construction sector. You will be instrumental in setting the operational blueprint, securing initial projects, and building a high-performing team. Key Responsibilities: Strategic Leadership & Operational Blueprint: Collaborate with executive leadership to define the operational strategy and establish core procedures, workflows, and performance standards for the new construction business unit. Lead the identification, evaluation, and acquisition of essential operational resources, including initial equipment, technology platforms, and strategic supplier partnerships, aligned with the company's growth       strategy. Define and champion the company's approach to project execution excellence, stringent quality assurance protocols, and industry-leading safety management systems. Strategic Business Development & Key Client Acquisition: Spearhead business development initiatives by leveraging your extensive network and proactively identifying and engaging with key potential clients, including developers, architects, and other businesses. Contribute strategically to the development of compelling business development materials, presentations, and proposals that align with the company's brand and value proposition. Cultivate and maintain strong, long-term relationships with strategic clients, industry influencers, and potential partners to secure initial and future projects. Represent the company at relevant industry events, conferences, and networking forums to build brand presence and generate leads. Provide critical operational and costing expertise in the preparation of complex bids and tenders, ensuring competitive and profitable proposals. Establish robust tracking and reporting mechanisms for business development activities and  outcomes. Project Leadership & Elite Execution (Flagship Projects): Directly lead and oversee the execution of the company's initial, high-profile construction projects, ensuring delivery on time, within stringent budgetary parameters, and to high-quality standards that reflect the company's commitment to excellence. Build, mentor, and empower a team of capable project managers, supervisors, and skilled workers, fostering a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Serve as the primary point of operational leadership, reporting directly to the designated General Manager or Business Unit Head on all strategic and tactical matters related to site and project performance. Chair comprehensive project briefings and strategic kick-off meetings, setting clear expectations and ensuring alignment across all stakeholders. Develop and present concise, data-driven periodic progress reports for internal executive review and external client communication. Strategic Construction Planning & Resource Optimization: Provide strategic direction in establishing advanced construction planning methodologies and resource allocation strategies for the new business unit. Establish sophisticated and efficient coordination protocols between internal project teams, pre-qualified subcontractors, key clients, and external consultants, ensuring seamless project delivery aligned with master schedules. Contribute to the development of standardized, company-level cost estimation and control processes, overseeing progress claim reviews, interim certifications, and payment authorizations. Strategically forecast and secure all necessary resources (highly skilled manpower, appropriate equipment fleets, suitable machinery, and quality materials) from project inception to completion, with a strong emphasis on optimized resource utilization and adherence to the company's financial guidelines. Enterprise-Level Risk Management & Regulatory Compliance: Proactively identify and mitigate potential risks across initial projects, translating lessons learned into robust company-wide risk management frameworks and best practices. Ensure unwavering compliance with all Singaporean regulatory requirements, including stringent quality, safety, and environmental standards, establishing a culture of compliance that aligns with the company's ethical and operational standards. Oversee the submission and clearance processes with relevant Singaporean authorities, establishing efficient and compliant protocols for all future projects. Implement and enforce adherence to approved specifications and relevant industry standards,       embedding a commitment to quality assurance throughout the organization. Establish, implement, and continuously monitor a comprehensive safety management system, conducting rigorous periodic safety audits on all initial sites to set the benchmark for a safety-first culture. Strategic HR & Regulatory Framework Development: Leverage a deep understanding of Singapore's Ministry of Manpower (MOM) regulations to strategically plan and manage foreign worker quotas, work permit applications, and other critical HR practices within the construction sector, aligning with the company's HR policies. Collaborate with the company's HR function to establish the business unit's HR policies, procedures, and talent acquisition strategies, ensuring compliance with local regulations and the company's overall standards. Utilize your comprehensive knowledge of Singapore's business certification and licensing requirements to ensure the new construction firm operates with full legal compliance from its inception, working with relevant internal teams or external advisors. Lead the application and acquisition of all essential business licenses and permits, ensuring a smooth and timely setup process. Strategically plan and manage initial project staffing to optimize workforce deployment while adhering strictly to MOM regulations and quota limitations. Strategic Ad-hoc & Foundational Initiatives: Undertake strategic ad-hoc assignments directed by executive leadership, the Construction Manager, and the Business Unit Head, particularly those critical to establishing the new company's operational, business development, and regulatory frameworks in alignment with the company's overall strategy. Job Requirements: Bachelor's Degree in Construction Management, Engineering (Civil, Structural, or equivalent), or a related field from a reputable institution. Advanced degree preferred. Equivalent to 15+ years of progressive and impactful experience within the construction sector in Singapore, with significant and demonstrable success as a Senior Construction Site Manager or equivalent management role on complex, large-scale projects. Exceptional and comprehensive knowledge of best-in-class project management methodologies, tools, and technologies across all project lifecycle stages, with a strategic understanding of the unique challenges and opportunities of a new business unit within a growing enterprise. Proven and demonstrable track record of successfully soliciting new business, building strong client relationships, and contributing to significant business development wins within the Singaporean construction market. Deep and current understanding of Singapore's Ministry of Manpower (MOM) regulations concerning foreign worker quotas, work permit processes, and strategic HR management within the construction industry. Comprehensive knowledge of the necessary business certifications, licenses, and regulatory requirements for operating a construction firm in Singapore, including interactions with relevant government agencies. Mastery of MS Project and the MS Office suite, with proficiency in other relevant project management and collaboration tools. A strategic and passionate leader with exceptional people management skills, demonstrated ability to build and motivate high-performing teams in a start-up or new business unit environment, and a commitment to fostering a culture of excellence and innovation. A results-oriented and decisive individual with exceptional analytical, problem-solving, and negotiation skills, crucial for navigating the complexities of a new business and the Singaporean regulatory landscape. World-class written, communication, and interpersonal skills, essential for engaging with executive leadership, diverse stakeholders, building strategic client relationships, securing new business, and effectively interacting with regulatory bodies. Unwavering willingness to work flexibly, including extended hours as required during critical project phases, business development initiatives, and the strategic establishment of the new business unit. A proven ability to thrive under pressure, manage complex priorities, and deliver exceptional results within demanding timelines. A strategic, proactive, and highly entrepreneurial mindset with a strong personal drive to build and shape a significant new construction business unit within a growing enterprise, with a direct and measurable impact on its growth and success across operations, business development, and regulatory compliance. Working Conditions: A dynamic and challenging leadership role based in Singapore, requiring a commitment of 5 working days per week, with the understanding that significant flexibility and extended hours will be necessary, particularly during the      initial strategic phases of the business unit's establishment, critical flagship project milestones, and strategic business development activities. This is a rare and compelling opportunity to leave a lasting legacy by building a significant new construction business unit within a thriving medium-sized enterprise. If you possess the strategic vision, deep industry expertise, entrepreneurial spirit, and proven leadership capabilities to excel in this foundational role, we encourage you to submit your confidential application.
