title: Events Specialist (Members Engagement), Member & Corporate Relations
description: The Singapore National Employers Federation (SNEF) is the national trade union of employers, representing more than 3,900 employers and the interests of all sectors of the economy. In the tripartite system, SNEF is the body representing employers and it aims to be the trusted partner of the National Trades Union Congress representing the labour movement, and the Ministry of Manpower representing the Government. SNEF’s vision is to help organisations achieve sustainable growth that is supported by responsible employment practices. Therefore, the federation aims to strengthen industrial and labour relations through advancing tripartism and responsible employment practices. Industrial peace will, in turn, help organisations achieve business sustainability. The Event Specialist, Members Engagement will play a pivotal role in engaging SNEF’s membership base and partners. This function is responsible for organising events, meetings and conferences, to attract new members, driving engagement with key decision-makers, partners and organisations. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced B2B environment, enjoys interacting with senior management, and demonstrates strong ownership of projects and budgets. This is a 2-year contract role with provision of extension yearly based on performance. Responsibilities: Plan, coordinate, and execute events end-to-end, including concept development, scheduling, budgeting, logistics, and post-event evaluation. Brainstorm and initiate event ideas to increase outreach to members, partners and organisations. Coordinate with internal teams, vendors, and external partners to ensure smooth event delivery. Track event outcomes and prepare post-event summaries and reports. Be open to working weekends on event days as required (off-in-lieu will be provided) Maintain and administer the CRM system to ensure accurate data entry, data integrity, and regular data cleansing. Manage and build strong relationships with members Take on projects and tasks as assigned by the reporting officer. Requirements: Relevant experience in planning meetings, incentives, conventions, conferences and/or special events. Experience in managing outside vendors, sourcing and negotiating contract services. Highly motivated, results-driven, and resilient with the ability to operate independently while collaborating effectively within a team structure. Ability to communicate effectively both verbally and in writing. Ability to work well under pressure and adapt to ever-changing scenarios Capability of problem solving – Anticipating, initiating and resolving issues Strong relationship-building and communication capabilities, with the confidence to engage and influence external stakeholders at senior levels.

title: Environmental Designer
description: Atelier Ten is an innovative multidisciplinary firm of building services engineers environmental design consultants, and lighting designers focused on delivering high-quality building services design and sustainable design consultancy within the built environment sector. We aim to deliver highly sustainable design solutions based on the touchstones of environmental integrity, economic viability, and pragmatic engineering solutions. These principles are embedded in our collaborative and creative approach to problem solving and efficient resource utilisation. We seek to reduce complexity. A core objective is to meet the needs of our clients by developing well-integrated buildings with simple systems that work with natural laws of physics to provide comfort while reducing energy consumption and contributing back to the greater environment. At Atelier Ten, we take a wider role in the design process than traditional building services engineers and consultants: in particular we work with the design team to integrate environmental moderation and control into the fabric of the building. From this improved basis we develop efficient and well-integrated building services systems to provide comfortable and efficient internal environments. Atelier Ten has a history of innovation. Founded in 1990, we are not afraid to challenge conventional thinking. Our environmental ethos is not an additive one; rather we strive to make buildings do more with less by working from basic principles and employing rigorous analysis. Our success is built on the expert delivery of innovative and sustainable engineering solutions. To support Atelier Ten’s continued success. We have established, and continue to maintain, efficient and effective business knowledge and quality management systems in order to provide our clients - in both the private and public sector - with first-class services covering the full range of design, construction, building services, engineering and maintenance disciplines, and by providing first-class project development and management services including an award-winning design solutions consultancy service. The post holder will be responsible for supporting work on a range of projects assisting the delivery of environmental and sustainability advice on projects for both internal clients and external clients. This will involve providing consultancy on environmental design, sustainability benchmarking/implementation, building energy optimisation, daylight & shading design, sustainable systems, materials and waste. This consultancy will be demonstrated through sound analytics including building physics. Projects will range from masterplanning to detailed design of specific buildings across all scales and locations of the globe. Using industry benchmark and internal standards, the post holder will ensure not only the timely delivery of accurate project information but also provide a point of contact between engineering staff and the other members of the environmental team. Key Accountabilities: Technical ability Coordinate, complete and ensure Green Rating projects in any given green rating scheme (Green Mark, LEED etc.) Complete simulations such as energy modelling, CFD, daylighting etc. Actively committed to pursuing CPD of all relevant disciplines Demonstrate reasonable knowledge of environmental design, sustainability benchmarking/implementation, building energy optimisation, daylight & shading design, sustainable systems, materials, waste and building physics Actively committed to developing specialist skills in one, or more, disciplines Demonstrate an understanding of Atelier Ten’s approach Able to provide accurate calculations and informative design options in relevant disciplines Deliver technical solutions in accordance with client brief Help facilitate the development of client brief by supporting other members of their team Willingness to develop an awareness and understanding of other disciplines Project management Liaise with client, architect and other members of the internal or external design team Can effectively co-ordinate with in-house engineers, the environmental teamand members of the graphics team Can develop appropriate design, proposals or analysis within the agreed time scale Maintain overview of project work and provide updates as and when required to line manager/project manager Interpersonal Skills Appreciates the need for effective liaison between Atelier Ten, clients and architects Report any feedback or issues concerning the project from clients Foster positive relationships within Atelier Ten Requirements: Sustainability, Engineering, Architectural or related disciplines Bachelor’s or higher degree in a field related to environmental building design, architecture, building services engineering, and/ or building science Fresh graduates are welcome to apply. We are seeking applicants with zero to three-years’ professional experience in a sustainability consulting practice or related design firm or within an engineering environment. LEED AP in BD+C (plus point) GMM/GMP (plus point) Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, AutoCAD, Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, InDesign), and Rhino is highly desired In addition, skills with such software as Revit, “R,” Grasshopper, Ladybug/Honeybee, Radiance, eQuest, EnergyPlus, IES, D3, or general programming or data visualization skill would be of interest Solid interest in environmental and energy-efficient design Have undertaken a wide variety of project work Practical knowledge of project work coupled with an understanding of project management tools Understanding of the deliverables associated with projects within the public and private sector Understanding of project co-ordination Team player Appreciates the need for the application of consistent standards and metrics Skills Good understanding on Green rating schemes such as Green Mark, LEED etc. Manage ESD projects independently Good understanding in simulations e.g. energy modelling, CFD, daylight & glare modelling. Ability to work under limited supervision Ability to prioritise work loads Good writing skills for producing written reports Effective IT skills for producing relevant material, including knowledge and application of building physics packages Demonstrated graphic and verbal communication skills Qualifications Sustainability, Engineering, Architectural or related disciplines

title: Finance Director
description: Shape strategy. Lead finance. Create meaningful impact. We are conducting a confidential executive search for an accomplished and forward-looking Finance Director. This is a rare opportunity for a senior finance professional to join a non-profit purpose-driven organisation with a strong track record of service excellence. The ideal candidate will bring experience in complex, multi-stakeholder settings and a passion for strategic financial leadership, operational excellence, and long-term sustainability. About the Role The Finance Director will provide strategic direction and oversight of the organisation’s financial functions for various entities, supporting a broad range of programmes and services for the community. This role ensures long-term financial sustainability, compliance with relevant regulations, and effective stewardship of resources. Responsibilities span financial planning, budgeting, reporting, funding, and audit activities, in close coordination with internal teams and external stakeholders including government organisations and statutory boards. Core Areas of Responsibility 1. Strategic Financial Leadership Develop and execute a financial roadmap aligned with the organisation’s mission and goals. Guide the executive/senior leadership and governance bodies on performance, planning, and financial risk. Perform scenario modelling and financial analysis to support strategic decisions. Offer expert financial input to key functional leaders and operations teams 2. Financial Planning and Budget Oversight Lead and coordinate the annual budgeting process across departments. Revenue Optimisation and Funding Strategy Monitor financial indicators and streamline revenue processes for service units. Governance, Compliance & Risk Uphold financial and statutory compliance in accordance with regulatory standards. Lead planning and budgeting for infrastructure, equipment and technology investment. 6. People & Cross-Functional Leadership Build and mentor a capable finance team committed to continuous improvement. Collaborate across departments to ensure financial alignment with service delivery goals. Foster effective communication and integration across operational units. Solid experience in financial analysis, budgeting, grant management, and audit readiness. Strong leadership and communication skills with experience collaborating across functional areas. Comfortable navigating complex stakeholder environments. Proficient in both written and verbal communication. Preferred Background Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. title: Value Advisor - Adobe Digital Experience (JAPAC)
description: Our Company Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours! Imagination for Everyone Adobe is a place where outstanding people work. Our employees recently voted us onto the Great Place to Work list for the 10th year in a row! We’re proud to offer market-leading employee benefits developed with your lifestyle in mind. Highlights include health insurance, global days off, a wellness fund, market-leading parental leave, access to our Employee Stock Purchase Program, and programs that support ongoing career growth. We proudly empower our employees to get involved with corporate social responsibility. We offer a broad range of chances to create a bigger impact through philanthropy, employee, and community engagement. We understand that people set us apart in our business, and that's why we want to meet you. The Opportunity You’re the person who turns Adobe’s platform into business outcomes customers can see, measure, and act on. This role sits at the intersection of strategy, data, and executive influence. Your mandate: help our most strategic Digital Experience customers in the JAPAC region quantify value, accelerate time-to-impact, and build solid reasons that drive expansion, retention, and advocacy. Partnering with Customers and Adobe teams (e.g., Account teams, Ultimate Success, Professional Services), you will anchor Adobe’s work in hard numbers and strategic narratives — not fluffy marketing claims. This role will become an expert in how Adobe’s products deliver measurable business outcomes, develop scalable value tools, and generate thought leadership to encourage customer growth and innovation. What you’ll do Value Discovery & Framing Lead structured discovery with senior customer collaborators (CMOs, CDOs, CIOs, CFOs) to identify growth, efficiency, and experience opportunities tied to Adobe’s DX portfolio. Conduct Value Measurement Workshops to align key customer collaborators on priority use cases and the measurement framework/important metrics needed for Value Realisation. Build compelling, data-driven value hypotheses anchored in industry benchmarks, customer data, and Adobe telemetry. Impact Development Collaborate with customers to showcase return on investment, cost savings, efficiency improvements, and revenue impact directly tied back to Adobe technology investments. Translate technical capabilities into financial outcomes using clear economic models, Adobe tools, and benchmarks. Work together with Adobe Teams such as Sales and Customer Success to assist with renewals, expansion, and executive alignment discussions. Value Realisation & Measurement Propel progress within Adobe's Value Realisation Acceleration (VRA) framework by addressing challenges and tracking value results across consumption, use cases, and N$R. Attend frequent value meetings with Adobe teams to grasp the context more clearly and assist them in advancing value more rapidly. Attend customer meetings e.g., SBR or equivalent experience with executives to help the Adobe teams demonstrate realised return on investment and identify new value pathways. Help promote adoption of Adobe’s value methodology throughout the ecosystem and JAPAC customers. What you need to succeed: 5–10+ years in value engineering, management consulting, transformation, strategy, finance, or similar roles. Champion of value methodology. Proven success building arguments for enterprise technology, digital marketing or transformation programs. Strong financial modelling capability. Ability to translate complexity into crisp, executive-ready insights. Comfortable engaging with senior executives at large enterprises across JAPAC. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Demonstrated ability to influence with authority across cross-functional teams. Familiarity with Adobe Experience Cloud or related marketing/commerce/customer experience technologies to drive personalization and optimization programs (ideal, but not required). Past experience in program management, scaled agile delivery or similar as a squad member; Scrum master experience beneficial.

title: Mathematics Teacher
description: About Us: Kangaroo Learning Centre Pte Ltd (“KLC”) was established in Singapore in May 2022 as part of the Fang Tian Education Group, a leading education institution founded in 2014 in China, which serves over 100,000 students annually. We are one of Singapore’s fastest-growing math tuition centres. Out of 4000 students who participated in the Singapore Mathematical Olympiad (SMO), 118 students were invited for Rounds 2 and 51 are from our learning center. We are also the only centre in Singapore that offers parent sit-in during classes, a flexible refund policy, and a dual-teacher classroom model. Our unique approach and proven results have earned us a strong reputation among parents and students alike. The centre offers a range of academic and enrichment programs for students of different ages, including primary and secondary school students, as well as those preparing for the PSLE, Math Olympiad, GCE ‘O’-Level and ‘A’-Level examinations. At KLC, there's no office politics — we value direct, honest communication and a results-driven mindset. We’re not just looking for employees, but co-partners who are eager to grow with us, take initiative , and play an active role in building something meaningful together. Job Overview: At Kangaroo Learning Centre (KLC), our mission is to develop the best teachers in Singapore. We provide comprehensive training and a one-on-one mentorship program led by experienced senior teachers and management. Whether you're just starting out or looking to grow further, you'll receive the guidance, support, and structured development needed to thrive. Join us and build a rewarding teaching career with a clear path toward excellence, leadership, and long-term growth. Job Responsibilities: Create an interactive, inquiry-based, and student-centred classroom environment that guides and motivates students to enjoy learning mathematics. Communicate closely with students, provide timely feedback to parents, and proactively support students in their learning. Participate in center branding activities such as YouTube content creation, article writing and sharing, etc. Familiarity with the question-setting patterns and trends of PSLE, O-Level, A-Level, and Math Olympiad competitions (e.g., RMO, APMOPS, NMOS, SMO) is an added advantage. Support and participate in any teaching-related tasks and team activities assigned by the centre. Job Requirements: A bachelor’s degree or above from an accredited university (e.g., NUS, NTU, SMU, or equivalent). Candidates without experience are welcome to apply. Open to all nationalities (Sponsored for work passes) No prior teaching experience required — comprehensive training and mentorship will be provided. Responsible, adaptable, and willing to continuously learning. Excellent interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills, with a positive, collaborative attitude and a strong passion for working closely with students, parents, and colleagues. Able to commit weekdays (inc. evenings) & weekends (Note: Most student classes are held on weekends) What We Offer Performance-based salary reviews — high-performing teachers may receive salary of up to S$11,960 per month. Additional S$1,000 monthly Guaranteed Teaching Allowance will be paid during the first 3 months probationary period. The first year S$50,000 Guaranteed Annual Salary . We top up the balance if you didn’t get S$50,000 annual salary in the first year. Structured one-on-one mentorship and coaching. Comprehensive training — perfect for those starting their teaching career. Dual career progression paths provided — grow into either a senior teaching expert or move towards leadership and management roles. Opportunities to be involved in content creation, curriculum development, conducting activities with students and parents, and team leadership.

title: Project Manager
description:
Prospect Protection Pte Ltd is seeking a Senior Project
Manager to lead end-to-end delivery of security-related projects across clients
and internal operations. You will manage project planning, stakeholder
coordination, site implementation, vendor delivery, and handover—ensuring
projects are delivered on time, within scope and budget, and aligned with
operational and compliance requirements. This role requires a hands-on,
adaptable individual who can work across field operations, systems, and service
delivery in a fast-paced environment. Key Responsibilities 1) Project Delivery (End-to-End) Manage multiple projects from initiation to closure: scope,
schedule, budget, resources, risks, and deliverables. Develop project plans, milestones, implementation
checklists, and handover documentation. Continue Track progress, manage change requests, and provide
regular updates to stakeholders. 2) Security Operations & Site Implementation Coordinate site surveys, deployment schedules, and on-site
execution with operations teams. Ensure smooth transition from implementation to BAU
(business-as-usual) operations. Drive issue resolution quickly during deployment and
stabilization phases. 3) Security Systems / Technology Enablement (where
applicable) Lead projects involving security technology and platforms
such as: CCTV / VMS, access control, alarm systems, visitor
management Patrol/guard tour systems, incident reporting workflows Ops platforms, HR platforms, Learning Management System
(LMS) rollouts or enhancements Coordinate configurations, user access requirements,
testing, training, and go-live support. 4) Stakeholder & Client Management Engage clients, internal stakeholders, and site teams to
clarify requirements and manage expectations. Conduct project meetings, minutes, action tracking, and
issue escalation. 5) Vendor & Contractor Management Manage vendors/sub-contractors: quotations, timelines,
quality checks, delivery acceptance, and service levels. Validate invoices and manage procurement coordination where
needed. 6) Quality, Documentation & Compliance Maintain clear project documentation (SOW, SOPs, risk
registers, test results, handover packs). Ensure work is aligned with project management, safety,
security, and data handling practices relevant to the project scope. 7) Per-Sales and Post Sales Analyse the client’s industry, pain points, and technical
requirements. Develop technical solutions or prototypes aligned with customer. Ensure compatibility with client infrastructure and address
integration concerns and guide customers through setup, configuration, and
deployment of the solution Manage contract renewals and identify opportunities for
upselling or cross-selling and address post-purchase queries, troubleshoot
problems, and maintain service quality Provide user training and resources to ensure effective
adoption and gather customer feedback to improve products and services Requirements (Must Have) Diploma or Degree in Project Management / Engineering / IT /
Security Management (or equivalent experience). Minimum 5 years and above of project management experience,
preferably in a services/operations environment. Demonstrated ability to manage stakeholders, vendors, and
cross-functional teams. Strong planning, coordination, and execution discipline;
comfortable handling multiple projects concurrently. Good written and verbal communication skills (reports,
updates, documentation). Willingness to visit sites and support deployments when
required. Good to Have (Advantage) Experience in security services or physical security systems
(CCTV/VMS, access control, alarms, guard tour). Experience rolling out enterprise platforms (Ops platform,
HRIS/HR platform, LMS) or process transformation initiatives. Project management certification (e.g., PMP, PRINCE2,
Agile/Scrum). Familiarity with SLA-based service delivery and operational
readiness/handover.

title: Senior UX Designer - X Delivery
description: Who We Are Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact. To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive. We Are BCG X We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions. What You'll Do As a part of BCG X, you will work closely with consulting teams on a diverse range of advanced topics. You will have the opportunity to leverage software development methodologies to deliver value to BCG's Consulting & BCG X (case) teams, BCG X Product teams and Practice Areas (domains) through providing design and user experience subject matter expertise, and accelerated execution support. You will collaborate with teams to gather requirements, specify, design, develop, deliver and support software solutions serving client needs. You will provide technical support through deeper understanding of relevant software solutions and processes to build high quality and efficient technology solutions. Assignments will range from short term Proof of concepts/Minimum viable products to long term cases with enterprise grade software development as a critical enabler through the project level description of the role responsibilities and impact within the organization. As a UX/UI Designer you will work towards establishing a customer-centric design culture and developing a fit-for purpose self-service data product as the company moves to become a product centric organisation. Responsibilities Demonstrate solid understanding and experience of common visual and interaction design principles and practice, and create appropriate sketches, wireframes, flows, and prototypes. Value and apply existing customer insights from research to guide design decisions as well as initiating and supporting research efforts with research partners. Frame problems and opportunities clearly through analysis of qualitative and quantitative inputs, and customers and stakeholders’ perspectives. Propose creative communicative visualizations to deliver research insights to teams and stakeholders (eg. User journeys, personas, systemic maps). Triangulate stakeholder vision, user insights, and cross-functional team discussion inputs, and integrate insights into design proposition. Master the phases of design from initial sketching to wireframing and high-fidelity prototyping. Apply and maintain design systems to deliver scalable, consistent UI components and interactions; adapt designs across platforms and devices to ensure cohesive user experiences. Demonstrate experience in testing and iterating based on feedback, both with stakeholders and with users. Create routine, informative communication materials explaining design decision-making rationale to team members and stakeholders. Partner with product managers, engineers, and consultants to solve for customer problems leveraging an agile way of working. Understand and incorporate desirability, feasibility, viability and strategic stakeholder constraints into the design. Use ideation, design, and testing tools effectively (e.g. Figma, Miro, Dovetail); experience with GenAI and vibe coding tools (e.g. Bolt, Cursor, V0, Lovable) is a plus. What You'll Bring We are looking for candidates with 1–3 years of experience (Analyst Level) or 4–5 years of experience (Senior Level) in UX Strategy, UX Research, UX Design, and UI Design. You've worked with a variety of tools; you'll be able to recommend and use what you think is best e.g. Figma, Adobe Suite and others. You have a wealth of experience creating design wireframes, prototypes, and mock-ups for developers to convert to code. You have design thinking experience to discover problems and come up with creative solutions by thoroughly understanding users' goals, frustrations, and end-task. You're collaborative and pragmatic with excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, happy to mentor and develop others. You have experience working in multi-disciplinary settings (with design, engineering and product teams). Bachelor's degree or equivalent in; Visual Design, Human-Computer Interaction, User Experience, or related Design field. You have a curious and entrepreneurial mindset, and you are ready to dive into different industries, types of user and stakeholder problems.

title: Marketing Campaign Ambassador (Entry-level)

title: Principal Consultant
description: Ethos BeathChapman (EBC) is a global group of executive talent and recruitment experts in Australia, Asia and EMEA, with a broader network beyond. We provide solutions across key recruitment markets including Financial Services, Fintech & Technology, Finance & Corporate Functions, Legal, Commercial and Life Sciences - our vision is to connect the world’s exceptional companies to exceptional people. At the heart of EBC is how we deliver upon that vision via our progressive behaviours and forward-thinking way of performing. We look to break tradition and truly embrace modern technology, data and analytics as well as best in class enablement support to deliver market leading executive recruitment solutions, tailored for our clients and candidates. We are living in a changing world – and here at EBC we strive to approach it in a changing way The Role: As a Principal Consultant, you will be in an exceptional role unlike elsewhere: one which truly aims and is designed to take already very strong performers to the next level of exceptional performance. Embark on your strategic journey to develop your candidate and client base alike, as well as accelerating your start with our existing client base and global lead network. You will have the opportunity to work alongside extremely talented and experienced colleagues, as well as innovate with outcomes to deliver strong business mix to clients across search, projects, retained and executive contracting. Take the opportunity to plunge yourself into our progressive ways of working, learn our AI technology, state-of-the-art cloud-based CRM platform and engage heavily with cutting edge talent engagement, marketing, data analysis and research functions that set EBC uniquely apart to take already strong performers to your exceptional performance level. At Principal Consultant level, EBC engages with you increasingly about rounding your ability to make big impact at macro levels and top performing attributes – be it leadership abilities, account management, proposing innovative solutions to clients (across retained, SoW, projects, contracting), use of internal technology or revenue performance. You will be groomed and challenged towards Associate Directorship and be expected to make contributions and influence more broadly around the business, helping impact EBC at large. Experience and Qualifications: We are looking for high performing, entrepreneurial and collaborative recruiters/headhunters with outstanding work ethics and passion for being the best in your specialised recruitment domain. You demonstrate emerging leadership qualities and superior fee generation abilities, speak knowledgably about your specialised recruitment market and most importantly, the opportunities and key current day levers therein. You will have roughly at least 5 to 10 years’ experience as a recruitment professional working in an executive search/recruitment firm – whether in large MNC agencies, headhunters, boutiques or otherwise, we are keen to hear from you. You’re able to show evidence of very strong revenue performance, existing client networks and growing ability to broadly influence both internally and externally. Our Culture: A career with EBC revolves on several key principles. Firstly, joining a mature, high performing group of experts requires people with a strong strategic, highly focussed and collaborative approach both internally and across all clients and candidates. Secondly, our commitment to new ways of working and enablement, data-driven decision-making and AI technology is not just held by upper management but is lived and breathed throughout the organisation. We aim for all new joiners to hold good passion and commitment to innovate, share new ideas and work in forward thinking ways like these. Lastly, and perhaps most importantly, we are a boutique who values internal relationships as much as external. Being persistent in delivering a world-class service and interactions across colleagues, clients and candidates alike is paramount. Throughout your journey, we are committed to your success. On hand you will always have the high powered and finessed support – not just from the experienced mentors on your team at your doorstep, but also the supporting technology, our lead network and our best-in class enablement support function. If you aspire to be exceptional, reach out to us with your resume at hello@ethosbc.com This is what your career at EBC could look like: www.ethosbc.com/careers About the Company: Established 2005 in Sydney, Ethos BeathChapman’s mid-sized agility and solutions-focused approach enables us to flex where traditional recruitment agencies have failed, and scale beyond the reach of small boutiques with executive recruitment solutions that address the client’s specific needs. In Asia, our multidisciplinary expertise covers specialised functions across Banking & Financial Services, Fintech, Technology, Legal & Compliance, Industrial, Consumer & Healthcare recruitment markets, among others. Part of Will , EBC has five offices worldwide in Australia, Singapore, Hong Kong, Netherlands and the UK. title: Associate Professor (Practice) in Quantitative Finance
description: The Nanyang Business School at Nanyang Technological University invites applications for a faculty position at the Associate Professor (Practice) level in the Division of Banking and Finance. We are seeking an experienced industry professional with a strong background in financial engineering, financial risk analytics, or related fields, who is passionate about bridging academic learning with real-world practice. About NTU: Young and research-focused, Nanyang Technological University (NTU) is ranked No. 12 globally according to the 2026 QS World University Rankings published in June 2025. The campus spans approximately 500 acres and is frequently listed among the Top 15 Most Beautiful in the World. For more information, visit www.ntu.edu.sg. About NBS: As a premier business school in a leading technological university, Nanyang Business School (NBS) strives for world-class innovation, research, and business education. Located in Singapore, the heart of East-West culture and business, NBS strives to develop leaders who can innovate for the future, transform organizations with cultural agility, and build sustainable enterprises across Asia and beyond. NBS is AACSB- and EQUIS-accredited and is currently ranked No. 22 globally in the 2025 Financial Times (FT) Global MBA ranking. This makes it the top-ranked MBA programme in Singapore and No. 3 in Asia. We have been consistently ranked among the top 40 business schools in the FT Global rankings. About the Division: The Division of Banking and Finance (BF) is committed to research excellence. Our faculty’s research is consistently featured in top finance and actuarial/insurance journals. Based on publications in the top five actuarial science/insurance journals, BF is consistently ranked Top 5 among business schools worldwide. The undergraduate programme currently offers two specialisations, Actuarial Science (ACS) and Risk Analytics (RA). The ACS programme is the only programme in Singapore accredited by the Institute and Faculty of Actuaries and recognized by the Society of Actuaries as a Center of Actuarial Excellence (CAE). We are also in the process of launching a new Quantitative Finance track within this programme. The RA programme covers the Financial Risk Manager (FRM) exam curriculum and enhances students’ analytical skills for financial risk management. The Insurance Risk & Finance Research Centre (IRFRC), which was established in 2011, sponsors and directs primary research on insurance and insurance-related risk research in Asia Pacific. Please visit our websites for more information: IRFRC and Banking & Finance Division. Qualifications: A Master’s degree or preferably a PhD in Quantitative Finance, Financial Engineering, Actuarial Science, or a related field. A minimum of ten years of industry experience in quantitative finance, financial engineering, investment banking, risk management, asset management, fintech, or related sectors. Proven ability and/or strong passion for teaching and mentoring students in a university setting. Strong network within the finance and risk industry that can be leveraged for curriculum development, student placement, and university-industry collaboration. Key Responsibilities: Successful candidates will be expected to: Contribute to the development, design, and teaching of a range of courses, including financial risk management, financial engineering, actuarial science, and other finance-related courses, at both undergraduate and postgraduate levels. Bring professional industry insights to enhance the relevance and application of the curriculum. Lead or support the continuous improvement and innovation of academic programmes in Quantitative Finance, Actuarial Science, and Risk Analytics. Leverage strong professional networks to support student career development and job placement efforts. Additionally, candidates must demonstrate service and scholarship through, but not limited to, good-quality publications, book chapters, written cases, pedagogical research, impactful creative works, or patents. Key Benefits: NBS offers an internationally competitive salary commensurate with the candidate’s qualifications and experience, as well as funding support for research and conference travel. The University will also provide subsidized on-campus housing for eligible international faculty, whereby residents have easy access to modern sports, F&B, childcare, and healthcare facilities. Faculty members also enjoy a low-tax environment, with an average tax rate of approximately 10% on the first US$100,000 of income. Application: Review of applications and shortlisting decisions will be made on an ongoing basis until the position is filled. Applicants are invited to submit their applications (cover letter, CV, statements on research, teaching and outreach, and citation report if available) through the NTU career portal: https://ntu.wd3.myworkdayjobs.com/Careers/job/Gaia/Associate-Professor--Practice--in-Quantitative-FInance_R00021716 For enquiries, please write to wjzhu@ntu.edu.sg

Organize project reviews to summarize experiences and lessons learned, ensuring continuous improvement and smooth progress of future projects. Lead project kick-off meetings and coordinate the transfer of bidding project documents to legal and project management teams for execution. Maintain customer relationships throughout project execution, manage contract payments and changes, and participate in project budget monitoring and control. Coordinate cross-functional teams including technology, procurement, project management, and legal departments to ensure successful bidding and negotiation outcomes. Demonstrate high self-discipline, resilience under stress, and self-motivation to sustain performance in high-intensity negotiations and long-term relationship management. Uphold a transparent, trustworthy, and stable professional attitude to foster consistent business results. Required competencies and certifications Minimum 5 years of experience in offshore engineering or shipbuilding industries with practical project operation expertise. At least 2 years of experience in regional market expansion with familiarity of business environments and cultural differences in target regions. Deep understanding of the upstream, midstream, and downstream industry chain, EPCIC general contracting models, and oil and gas market trends. Proficiency in independently building key customer maps for regional markets. Comprehensive knowledge of international bidding processes (RFQ/EOI → Clarification → Technical/Communication Proposal → Final Offer) and all major engineering life cycle stages to anticipate and mitigate execution risks during business development. Understanding of offshore engineering equipment design, construction processes, and industry standards for FPSO, FLNG, FPU, Jack-up, WHP, and PP. Familiarity with EPCIC contract frameworks and ability to identify key business risks including liquidated damages, letters of intent, interface issues, changes, tax, insurance, and local compliance. Capability to identify genuine project opportunities through exhibitions, consultants, partners, and internal resources. Preferred competencies and qualifications Proven ability to coordinate multiple departments to complete bidding and negotiation processes effectively. Strong professional attitude characterized by transparency, trustworthiness, and stability under pressure.

title: Associate Director
description: Ethos BeathChapman (EBC) is a global group of executive talent and recruitment experts in Australia, Asia and EMEA, with a broader network beyond. We provide solutions across key recruitment markets including Financial Services, Fintech & Technology, Finance & Corporate Functions, Legal, Commercial and Life Sciences - our vision is to connect the world’s exceptional companies to exceptional people. At the heart of EBC is how we deliver upon that vision via our progressive behaviours and forward-thinking way of performing. Work closely alongside extremely talented and experienced business leaders in EBC, as well as hold a key gateway and relationship with our sister companies like Chapman Consulting Group (CCG) and international affiliations/lead networks. You will hold responsibility to tailor outcomes and shape strategy for yourself, team and EBC as a business with your regional clients. Use your passion for innovation to work intently with internal stakeholders and our Enablement function (talent engagement, marketing, data, research, client directors, training and development) that sets EBC uniquely apart to take already strong performers to exceptional performance levels whilst plunging yourself into our progressive ways of working, mastering our AI technology and state-of-the-art cloud-based CRM platform. At Associate Director level, EBC engages with you increasingly about rounding your ability to make big impact at macro levels and top performing attributes – whether leadership abilities, account management, proposing innovative solutions to clients (across retained, SoW, projects, contracting), use of internal technology or revenue performance. You will be groomed and challenged towards full Directorship and be expected to make contributions a little more broadly around the business, helping impact EBC at large. Experience and Qualifications: We are looking for an experienced, successful and entrepreneurial-minded executive recruiter who is well-regarded for the deep knowledge of your specialised recruitment domain, outstanding work ethics, collaborative nature and exceptional client delivery. You demonstrate emerging leadership qualities and superior fee generation abilities, speak knowledgably and passionately about your specialised recruitment market and most importantly, the opportunities and key current day levers therein. The ideal candidate should have at least 6 to 10 years of recruitment experience in a fast-paced recruitment environment. Candidates with prior experience from recruitment agencies, in-house or RPO teams will be preferred. Our Culture: A career with EBC revolves on several key principles. Firstly, joining a mature, high performing group of experts requires people with a strong strategic, highly focussed and collaborative approach both internally and across all clients and candidates. Secondly, our commitment to new ways of working and enablement, data-driven decision-making and AI technology is not just held by upper management but is lived and breathed throughout the organisation. We aim for all new joiners to hold good passion and commitment to innovate, share new ideas and work in forward thinking ways like these. Lastly, and perhaps most importantly, we are a boutique who values internal relationships as much as external. Being persistent in delivering a world-class service and interactions across colleagues, clients and candidates alike is paramount. Throughout your journey, we are committed to your success. On hand you will always have the high powered and finessed support – not just from the experienced mentors on your team at your doorstep, but also the supporting technology, our lead network and our best-in class enablement support function. If you aspire to be exceptional, reach out to us with your resume at hello@ethosbc.com This is what your career at EBC could look like: www.ethosbc.com/careers About the Company: Established 2005 in Sydney, Ethos BeathChapman’s mid-sized agility and solutions-focused approach enables us to flex where traditional recruitment agencies have failed, and scale beyond the reach of small boutiques with executive recruitment solutions that address the client’s specific needs. In Asia, our multidisciplinary expertise covers specialised functions across Banking & Financial Services, Fintech, Technology, Legal & Compliance, Industrial, Consumer & Healthcare recruitment markets, among others. Part of Will , EBC has five offices worldwide in Australia, Singapore, Hong Kong, Netherlands and the UK. title: Technical Business Analyst
description: We are looking for a techincal business analyst who are passionate about delivering high quality IT applications and solutions. You will be part of the team that helps to improve and transform the processes in the banking and finance industry. You will have opportunities to learn new skill set and acquire valuable domain knowledge as you work on the solutions that meet multifaceted technical and business requirements. Roles and Responsibilities Bridge the gap between business needs and technical solutions: Act as a liaison between various business stakeholders and the development team to ensure that business requirements are clearly understood and translated into technical solutions. Collaborate with both business and technical teams to align objectives, prioritize tasks, and address any discrepancies or challenges that may arise during the development process. Translate requirements into technical specs and actionable tasks for the development team : Take ownership of translating business requirements into detailed technical specifications, ensuring clarity and feasibility for the development team. Break down complex requirements into manageable tasks and user stories, facilitating effective communication and task management within the distributed development team. Gather requirements and document business requirements: Conduct thorough requirements gathering sessions with stakeholders to identify and prioritize business needs, objectives, and constraints. Document business requirements accurately, ensuring completeness, clarity, and traceability throughout the project lifecycle. Develop detailed user stories and acceptance criteria: Create comprehensive user stories that capture end-user perspectives, functional requirements, and acceptance criteria. Define clear acceptance criteria to ensure that deliverables meet stakeholder expectations and quality standards. Work closely with development teams to ensure timely delivery of solutions: Collaborate closely with development teams to provide guidance, clarification, and support throughout the development process. Monitor project progress, identify potential bottlenecks, and proactively address any issues that may impact the timely delivery of solutions. Conduct thorough testing of implemented solutions that meet business requirements: Plan and execute rigorous testing procedures to validate that implemented solutions fulfill specified business requirements and functional specifications. Identify and report any defects or deviations from requirements, working with the development team to resolve issues promptly. Provide support and guidance to stakeholders throughout the development lifecycle: Serve as a primary point of contact for stakeholders, offering support, guidance, and regular updates on project status, milestones, and deliverables. Address stakeholder queries, concerns, and feedback in a timely and professional manner, ensuring a high level of satisfaction and engagement throughout the development lifecycle. Identify and communicate risks, issues, and dependencies related to project delivery: Proactively identify potential risks, issues, and dependencies that may impact project delivery, and communicate them effectively to stakeholders and project teams. Collaborate with relevant stakeholders to develop mitigation strategies, contingency plans, and resolutions for addressing identified risks and issues. Requirements Bachelor’s Degree: A bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or Business Administration. Experience in the Banking Domain will be an advantage: Previous experience or exposure to the banking or financial services industry, including knowledge of banking products, processes, and regulatory requirements. Experience in Agile methodologies and knowledgeable in software development Lifecycle and software testing: Hands-on experience with Agile methodologies (e.g., Scrum, Kanban) and a strong understanding of the software development lifecycle (SDLC) and software testing practices. Familiarity with database SQL queries: Proficiency in writing and executing SQL queries to retrieve, manipulate, and analyze data from relational databases. Familiarity with JIRA and Confluence: Experience using JIRA for project management and issue tracking, as well as Confluence for collaboration and documentation.

title: Senior Experience Designer, Strategy & Talent(Singapore, India)
description: JOB SUMMARY This role could be based in Singapore and India. When you start the application process you will be presented with a drop down menu showing all countries, Please ensure that you select a country where the role is based. •    To support our focus on continuous improvement we are looking for a Senior Experience Designer to join our team. You will be working on a wide range of projects and services for Standard Chartered within our Experience Team. •    As part of the Experience Design team you will play a critical role in driving human centered design strategies and deliver exceptional product and service experiences.  Working collaboratively with cross-functional partners, you will ensure that experience drives every stage of the design and development process. This will involve planning and integration into larger delivery teams, conducting research, workshops, driving insights, creating personas, working on user journeys, and validating outputs in our offices all over the world. •    This is a role for a candidate with an ethnographic research or service design background interested in people and design. Given the nature of this role, we are looking for experienced candidates with a unique blend of innovation process improvement and functional expertise. We are looking for a professional who will bring agile thinking, an understanding of process data and can thrive in a virtual, global team. •    Work collaboratively within the experience team and programme teams to set up and run research, all within the context of business requirements Through a breadth of user research methodologies, develop an intimate knowledge of employee requirements and organisational behaviours, to enable clear definition of employee journeys •    Apply innovative methodologies (such as HCD thinking) to plan and facilitate the most efficient approach to design and improvements Deliver intuitive user experiences, translating research into user journeys, personas, all optimised for a wide range of devices and interfaces Monitor and identify design opportunities & performance within the core products and develop solutions to counter them, selling these solutions into the wider business through research output, prototyping and user-testing Clearly and effectively communicate design processes, ideas and solutions to the wider business Bring a user-centred design approach to UX decisions on potential future features and functionalities •    Champion the experience perspective across the organisation •    Support the Experience team in actively developing, monitoring and improving the global design process •    Clearly convey insights and expertise to stakeholders to inform and validate business decisions RESPONSIBILITIES Strategy Awareness of S&T Strategy and role with in the SCM/CRES to drive Experience Led Design thinking. Business In line with overall strategy ensure that Experience Design thinking is implemented to reduce experience deficits, minimise friction in processes, save time and increasing satisfaction levels and ROI. Processes Responsible for executing on Experience Design across Strategy and Talent, primarily within CRES and SCM. People & Talent •  Lead through example and champion Experience Design in all area that you work in •  Ensure that all programmes you are involved in are Ex Led, understanding and championing HCD and HCAI thinking •  Support the ongoing work to create a CoE for Experience Design •  Responsibility to conduct planning for Experience work in the projects your are involved in •  Conduct research, drive out insights and create actionable experience led change and transformation Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key Stakeholders •    SCM – P2P Regis V Jose Our Ideal Candidate •    3+years of relevant experience working on usability testing for digital projects and or ethnographic research •    Demonstrable UX design or service design experience •    User-centered design skills, including delivery of user research and testing, ethnographies, A/B testing, prototyping, usability and accessibility assessment •    Attention to detail and accuracy •    A self-starter, who is able to plan and structure work, understanding the principles involved with integrating into large scale program teams without supervision •    Excellent organizational and time keeping skills •    Global mind set, experience of working across multiple time zones •    Ability to work with stakeholders to understand detailed requirements and design complete user experience that meet business needs and vision •    A team player, who is culturally aware •    Training - Expertise in standard UX software. Adobe, In Design, Figma, JIRA, Excel and Power Point. Self development of skills to keep up to date with fast changing trends •    Languages - Business Communication Role Specific Technical Competencies •    HCD Thinking •    UX Research •    Communication Skills •    Visual Design •    Planning About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. title: Manager | Credit Risk
description: Join our Credit Risk division, which is responsible for analysing and assessing all credit and equity exposures taken by Macquarie to minimise the risk of loss arising from failure of counterparties to repay loans or honour contracts. Our division is also responsible for undertaking reviews of counterparties and transactions, and establishing appropriate limit frameworks. On principal transactions, we work with the deal team to understand risk versus reward profiles and develop risk mitigation strategies. At Macquarie, our advantage is bringing together diverse people and empowering them to shape all kinds of possibilities. We are a global financial services group operating in 31 markets and with 56 years of unbroken profitability. You’ll be part of a friendly and supportive team where everyone - no matter what role - contributes ideas and drives outcomes. What role will you play? You will conduct in-depth credit reviews and analyses of Commodities and Global Markets' counterparties in the Asia region, focusing on corporate clients. You will collaborate with global credit offices to secure credit approvals, partner with Macquarie's business groups to evaluate new products and transactions, and provide well-founded recommendations to credit approvers. Additionally, you will assist in structuring transactions, reviewing legal terms, and offering general advice and analysis. You will perform annual and new reviews of counterparties, stay updated on portfolio developments, and address ad-hoc credit requests. You will also maintain strong connections across the commodities market to stay informed of the latest news and developments. What you offer Over 5 years of regional Asia credit risk experience in a large financial institution and/or commodity trading house Physical commodities trading experience including energy products and trade finance Strong background in transactional credit risk analysis and approvals of OTC derivatives across commodities, including familiarity with negotiating ISDA and CSA documentation Proven experience in preparing counterparty credit reviews, credit recommendations and presenting to senior risk management Comfortable planning and re-prioritising workload in an unpredictable environment Confident and able to provide independent credit judgement and articulate this succinctly to the senior management team High degree of ownership, delivery and timeliness in a fast-paced, output focused environment Ability to build strong, open, dialogue-based relationships across a range of stakeholders. We love hearing from anyone inspired to build a better future with us, if you're excited about the role or working at Macquarie we encourage you to apply. What we offer At Macquarie, you’re empowered to shape a career that’s rewarding in all the ways that matter most to you. Macquarie employees can access a wide range of benefits which, depending on eligibility criteria, include: 1 wellbeing leave day and 5 additional paid Flexi-Leave days per year 20 weeks’ paid parental leave for primary caregivers along with 12 days of paid transition leave upon return to work and 6 weeks’ paid leave for secondary caregivers 2 days of paid volunteer leave per annum and donation matching Option to purchase additional annual leave Benefits and initiatives to support your physical, psychological and financial wellbeing including, comprehensive medical and life insurance cover and additional flexible wellbeing credits Access to a wide range of learning and development opportunities, including reimbursement for professional membership or subscription Hybrid and flexible working arrangements, dependent on role Reimbursement for work from home equipment About the Risk Management Group Our Risk Management Group works as an independent, and centralised function, responsible for independent and objective review and challenge, oversight, monitoring and reporting in relation to Macquarie’s material risks. We are a global team that aims to manage the risks of today and anticipate the risks of tomorrow. Our divisions include compliance, credit, financial crime risk, market risk, operational risk, aggregate risk and prudential, and central. Our commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion We are committed to fostering a diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply and welcome all identities, including race, ethnicity, cultural identity, nationality, gender (including gender identity or expression), age, sexual orientation, marital or partnership status, parental, caregiving or family status, neurodiversity, religion or belief, disability, or socio-economic background. We welcome further discussions on how you can feel included and belong at Macquarie as you progress through our recruitment process. Our aim is to provide reasonable adjustments to individuals who may need support during the recruitment process and through working arrangements. If you require additional assistance, please let us know in the application process.

title: Head of Sales & Business Development
description: PPP Coffee, formerly known as Papa Palheta, is a leading specialty coffee roaster in Singapore. We serve a network of over 50 partners across the HORECA sector and corporate offices. In addition, the group owns and operates three F&B retail outlets, including our flagship store, Chye Seng Huat Hardware. As part of our expansion plans, we are seeking a dynamic and experienced Head of Sales & Business Development to lead the strategic growth of our B2B sales channels, including cafés, restaurants, hotels, offices, and corporate partnerships across Singapore and key regional markets. Job Description The Head of Sales & Business Development will be responsible for driving and executing our B2B growth strategy. This includes leading a high-performing team, building strategic partnerships, and expanding our footprint in both local and regional markets. The role is critical in shaping how PPP Coffee delivers value to partners and clients while strengthening our brand presence in the specialty coffee space. Key Responsibilities Develop and implement the B2B wholesale and corporate sales strategy in alignment with business goals. Identify and pursue new growth opportunities across customer segments and geographic markets. Set and manage sales targets, forecasts, and performance KPIs to achieve revenue goals. Build and maintain a robust pipeline of wholesale and corporate prospects. Lead the full sales cycle from lead generation and pitching to negotiation and onboarding. Cultivate and maintain long-term relationships with key accounts and stakeholders. Hire, train, and manage a team of wholesale account managers and sales executives. Foster a high-performance, service-oriented team culture. Collaborate with Product, Marketing, and Operations teams to customise offerings and ensure service excellence. Represent the brand at trade shows, industry events, and networking platforms. Develop and implement a sales CRM system to manage the leads and opportunities pipeline to support data-driven decisions Provide regular sales updates, market insights, and strategic recommendations to the leadership team. Minimum Requirements 5 years of B2B sales and account management experience, preferably in the F&B, FMCG, or hospitality sectors. Proven success in wholesale or corporate sales, with strong negotiation and closing skills. Demonstrated experience in building and leading high-performing sales teams. Strategic thinker with an entrepreneurial mindset and hands-on execution ability. Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills. Monthly Salary Range: S$5,000 - S$8,000 (including commissions)

As part of a small Community Engagement & Partnerships team, this role provides ownership and leadership over specific communications and marketing domains, translating organisational priorities into effective narratives, campaigns and stakeholder engagement efforts that support KKCS’s mission, services and fundraising needs. Key Duties & Responsibilities 1. Communications & Fundraising Execution (Domain Ownership) 1.1. Drive the planning and execution of communications and marketing initiatives within assigned domains, including media relations, donor communications and fundraising-related outreach. 1.2. Translate organisational priorities, programmes and impact into clear, compelling communications for internal and external audiences. 1.3. Ensure consistency, clarity and alignment across all communications touchpoints. 2. Donors & Grantors Communications 2.1. Partner with internal teams to craft narratives and materials that reflect programme outcomes and funding requirements. 2.3. Ensure timely dissemination of impact updates, donor reports and engagement collaterals. 3. Fundraising & Campaign Support 3.1. Drive communications and marketing components of fundraising initiatives and events, including campaign messaging, publicity, collaterals and post-event communications. 3.2. Support donor stewardship efforts by ensuring timely, meaningful and consistent engagement before and after fundraising activities. 3.3. Deliver timely fundraising strategies and proposals and the required compliance and reporting related work. 4. Cross-Unit Collaboration 4.1. Work closely with service units to surface stories of practice, outcomes and community impact that support communications, media and fundraising needs. 4.2. Serve as a key connector between service units and Corporate Services for communications-related work such as donor and fundraising needs. 4.3. Provide professional input and recommendations on communications, media and donor engagement matters. 4.4. Provide secretarial support to select committees from time to time. 5. Brand & Content Development 5.1. Articulate KKCS’s impact and strengthen brand affinity through high-quality content — stories, videos, collaterals, speeches and digital assets. 5.2. Uphold and safeguard KKCS’s brand guidelines across all communications channels and materials. 6. Management of Communications Channels & Platforms 6.1. Manage and update key communications channels, including website, email communications, social media, SharePoint, general media other digital platforms. 6.2. Manage media engagement efforts, including pitching stories, responding to media enquiries and coordinating interviews and features. 6.3. Build and maintain relationships with relevant media partners and digital platforms to enhance KKCS’s public profile and credibility. 7. Other Duties 7.1. Perform any other duties as assigned by the Executive Director and the leadership team.

title: Project Manager - Market Risk
description: Project Description: Client is leading global financial institution with a strong presence in Asia, Americas, Europe, and the Middle East. In the Asia-Pacific region, we operate key hubs in Singapore and Hong Kong, providing comprehensive financial services across corporate banking, markets, treasury, and risk management. Client is committed to leveraging advanced technology platforms to deliver innovative solutions, enhance operational efficiency, and maintain robust risk governance. As part of this commitment, the Group invests in industry-leading systems such as Murex to support end-to-end trade processing, risk analytics, and regulatory compliance across multiple asset classes. Client has embarked on strategic initiative to consolidate it's Banking and security arm into one. Towards that end it is hiring project manager to drive this consolidation exercise leading to: -Accelerated delivery of strategic platform programs, minimizing risk and downtime. -Improved cross-team collaboration and decision-making across geographies and functions. -Ensure enhancements meet business, risk, and compliance requirements in a complex regulatory environment. -Support of scalable, efficient operations that position for future growth. Responsibilities: Responsible for end-to-end coordination, governance, and delivery oversight of middle-office initiatives, ensuring alignment with regional risk priorities, quality standards, and stakeholder expectations. 1. Project Planning & Coordination - Maintain detailed project plans, timelines, and milestones for middle office deliverables, aligned with overall project plans. - Coordinate with BA team to ensure tasks are clearly assigned, tracked, and completed on schedule. - Attend workshops and meetings with Regional, Country Risk, and the team. 2. Status Monitoring & Reporting - Collect updates from the BA team and consolidate progress reports. - Track action items, issues, and dependencies related to middle office deliverables. - Prepare regular status reports and dashboards, as required, for the Regional Risk team and other stakeholders. 3. Risk & Issue Management - Identify, log, and track risks and issues for the middle office function. - Proactively follow up on mitigation plans and escalate critical risks or roadblocks for resolution. 4. Stakeholder Communication & Documentation - Serve as a liaison for operational communications between Regional & Country Risk, the BA team, and the project management team. - Ensure all key decisions, meeting minutes, and action items are documented and distributed. - Maintain a repository of project documentation relevant to the middle office. 5. Key Responsibilities: Strategic Partnerships Development Identify, engage, and onboard corporate, institutional and community partners into CORA’s sustainability programs. Lead negotiations and ensure mutually beneficial outcomes. Monitor and report on partnership performance, impact, and ROI. Business Development & Growth Develop strategies to scale partnership programs nationally and regionally. Identify new revenue streams and sponsorship opportunities tied to sustainability initiatives. Represent CORA at industry events, forums, and stakeholder meetings. Operational Excellence Work closely with internal teams and business units to ensure strategic alignment across all partnership initiatives. Support strategic planning and forecasting activities. Prepare reports and presentations for senior leadership and stakeholders. Collaborate with Business Intelligence team for data-driven insights on recycling trends. Support operational excellence in call center and customer engagement as programs grow. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Business, Sustainability, Environmental Management, or related field. 2+ years in business development, partnerships, or sustainability programs. Strong relationship-building and negotiation skills. Proven ability to manage complex projects and multiple stakeholders. Passion for sustainability and circular economy principles.

title: Project Manager
description: About Us: Kim Hua Construction is a well-established construction company specializing in high-quality hotel, commercial, and residential developments across Singapore. With a reputation for excellence in both design and execution, we deliver innovative and sustainable solutions that meet the evolving needs of our clients. We are looking for a highly skilled and motivated Project Manager to join our team and take charge of the successful delivery of various exciting projects. Job Summary: As a Project Manager at Kim Hua Construction , you will oversee all aspects of construction projects, focusing on hotel, commercial, and residential developments. You will be responsible for ensuring that projects are completed on time, within budget, and in accordance with safety and quality standards. Your expertise will be crucial in coordinating with various stakeholders, managing the project team, and handling any challenges that arise during the project lifecycle. Key Responsibilities: Project Planning & Coordination: Lead the planning and execution of hotel, commercial, and residential construction projects. Develop and maintain detailed project schedules, budgets, and resources plans. Work closely with architects, engineers, and clients to ensure all project objectives are met. Obtain all necessary permits, approvals, and certifications for each project. Team Leadership & Collaboration: Supervise and lead a multi-disciplinary project team, including site supervisors, contractors, subcontractors, and other professionals. Resolve any conflicts or issues in a timely and professional manner to keep the project on track. Cost & Budget Management: Monitor and control project costs to ensure alignment with the approved budget. Oversee procurement activities for materials, resources, and subcontractors. Approve invoices and payment claims for suppliers, subcontractors, and vendors. Quality Control & Safety: Ensure adherence to safety protocols and regulatory requirements throughout the project lifecycle. Implement and maintain rigorous quality control processes to meet construction standards and client specifications. Conduct site inspections and audits regularly to ensure compliance with project timelines, safety, and quality. Communication & Client Management: Act as the main point of contact for clients, stakeholders, and consultants. Provide regular updates on project progress, risks, and milestones. Address client concerns or changes in scope promptly and provide appropriate solutions. Risk Management & Issue Resolution: Proactively identify potential risks and implement mitigation strategies. Manage project scope changes, ensuring that all variations are documented and approved through change orders. Ensure all legal and contractual obligations are met. Qualifications: Education: Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, Architecture, or a related field. Experience: Minimum of 5-7 years of project management experience in the construction industry, with a focus on hotel, commercial, or residential projects. Knowledge: Strong understanding of Singapore’s building regulations, construction laws, and industry best practices. Skills: Proficiency in project management software (e.g., MS Project). Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills. Strong problem-solving abilities and attention to detail. Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and work under pressure. Certifications: PMP, Chartered Project Manager, or other relevant certifications will be an advantage. Why Join Us? Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package. Opportunity to work on prestigious hotel, commercial, and residential projects across Singapore. A collaborative and growth-oriented work environment. Professional development and career progression opportunities.
