title: Senior Manager, Experience & Activation
description: We are Assembly – and we’re not like the rest. We’re the modern alternative agency, bringing together industry-leading data, talent, and tech to Find the Change That Fuels Growth for the best brands on the planet, including Lenovo, Lane Crawford, Marina Bay Sands, Fossil and more. Our diverse, global community of over 1,600 passionate experts combines global thinking with unmatched local expertise in more than 20 markets worldwide – enabling brands to engage and move consumers anywhere. We use STAGE, Assembly’s proprietary, privacy-centric data solutions platform, to surface powerful insights that transform into actionable brand opportunities. We’re at the cutting edge of new media, technology, and platforms embedded in the lives of today’s consumers, and we’re tapped into how culture and communities’ need change. We do this while staying steadfast in our commitment to Leave the World Better Than We Found It through measurable social and environmental impact work. Assembly was named The Drum’s APAC Media Agency of the Year in 2021 WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR A Senior Manager who has a well-rounded digital marketing background, excellent in managing client relations, has a future forward and agile mindset, and is an independent individual who can be collaboratively within the team. Due to the complex nature of these key accounts, the ideal candidate must be able to demonstrate multi-tasking and time-management capabilities to manage client stakeholders and internal teams on global, regional and local levels. WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING ON ANY GIVEN DAY Strategic Responsibilities Lead quarterly and half-yearly media strategy planning for Display and Social channels (Meta, TikTok, YouTube, Programmatic) . Translate business and marketing objectives into full-funnel media plans that balance reach, quality of visits, and conversion . Define and maintain always-on vs. burst strategy by market; advise on budget split by campaign objective, ad format, and platform . Integrate audience segmentation, product life cycle, seasonality, and promotion windows into channel activation. Lead test and learn agenda including creative A/B testing and advocate for performance-driven ad formats . Stay updated with platform changes and propose pilot programs for early adoption. Translate platform partner insights (Meta, TikTok, YouTube, DV360, etc.) into client-ready opportunities that scale across markets. Cross-functional & Stakeholder Management Act as the Display & Social subject matter expert across internal client teams, ASM teams, and platform partners. Partner with SEM and planning leads to ensure cross-channel consistency in strategy, execution, and reporting. Escalate blockers or opportunities tied to budget, performance, or technical issues to internal and client stakeholders. Collaborate with client channel and creative to ensure campaign and creative-specific activations align with sales priorities. Execution Oversight Supervise QA checklist across naming convention, CID tagging, conversion tracking, campaign build Ensure budgets, bid strategies, and optimization goals are accurately implemented across all platforms. Validate setup across platforms with cross-platform and market consistency Coordinate with analysts and the offshore team for market-specific adjustments (e.g., language, localized promo, creative tailoring). Develop integrated audience strategies leveraging 1st party data, 2nd party (publisher segments) and 3rd party (GWI, Bombora, etc) Collaborate with creative and production partners to review and optimize ad creatives tailored for each platform, audience, and funnel stage. Performance Management & Reporting Lead weekly and quarterly reviews of Display & Social performance, covering traffic, conversions, efficiency, and budget pacing. Approve budget reallocations across BUs and markets based on pacing, seasonality, and performance trends. Approve testing roadmap (new platforms, audience models, ad formats, measurement frameworks). Flag underperforming trends and provide actionable recommendations on media mix, audiences, and creative optimizations. Drive insights consolidation across markets for QBRs and regional reviews, connecting channel performance to revenue growth. Requirements DOES THIS SOUND LIKE YOU? Minimum 7 – 9 years in media agency-side roles, with at least 3 years in a people-management and managerial role Fluent English communication, verbal and written Strong knowledge of Display and Social platforms (Meta Ads Manager, TikTok Business Center, YouTube, DV360, and Programmatic buying tools) Experienced in integrated media planning, with a strong understanding of digital channels activation. Strong in budget pacing models and forecasting accuracy across product segments and markets. Advanced Excel / Looker Studio / Tableau / Adobe Analytics reporting skills. Familiarity with incrementality tests and other advanced measurements. Significant experience managing global/regional accounts with multiple stakeholders (clients, media partners). Demonstrable track record of translating client objectives into feasible, performance-oriented digital strategies across markets. Strong knowledge of APAC media landscape to adapt and localise global and regional strategies Proven track record in managing complex accounts with multiple stakeholders across multiple time-zones effectively while developing strong client relationships Experience leading large scale account teams across different offices and time zones Personality Attributes Team leader, collaborator, contributor Proactive, motivated, self-starter Out-of-the-box creative thinker Confident, responsible Process-driven, organised 'Never stop learning' attitude Benefits WHY ASSEMBLY? We’re part of Stagwell, the challenger network built to transform marketing. We’re nimble, smart, and digital-first, and we’re quickly growing to take on the biggest legacy hold cos. We care about your growth – we offer competitive salaries, annual compensation reviews, and keep detailed personal development plans to ensure you’re hitting your personal and company goals. We recognize and celebrate your success…all the time! Whether it’s through company meetups, employee recognition programs, or just a regular day, we make sure our people’s achievements are known and appreciated! We’re truly a people-first organization. That’s why we offer a Flexible Time Off policy that puts you in control of your work-life balance, as well as market-leading primary and secondary caregiver and parental leave policies. We care about social and environmental Impact – we have dedicated Impact Champions that collaborate globally to make sure we’re leaving the world better than we found it. We have an amazing group of employee resource groups committed to guiding the agency to become more inclusive, diverse, and representative of the world around us. Sounds like the right role for you? Click to apply now!

title: Staff Data Platform Engineer
description: Staff Data Platform Engineer, Singapore Airwallex is the leading financial technology platform for modern businesses growing beyond borders. With one of the world’s most powerful payments and banking infrastructures, our technology empowers businesses of all sizes to accept payments, move money globally, and simplify their financial operations, all in one single platform. Established in 2015 in Melbourne, we aim to connect entrepreneurs, business builders, makers, and creators with opportunities in every corner of the world. Today, Airwallex has a global footprint across Asia-Pacific, Europe, and North America. The Role: As a leader level data expert, you will oversee the strategy, architecture, development, and operation of Airwallex's data and AI platforms. You will play a pivotal role in influencing data-driven and AI-powered decision-making processes. A key aspect of your role will also include building a high-performing team that excels in a fast-paced environment. Your leadership will be critical in mentoring the team, promoting a culture of innovation, and driving technical excellence. Responsibilities： Spearheaded the identification and resolution of Airwallex-wide challenges using cutting-edge data platform solutions. Provide visionary technical direction, fostering a community within Airwallex's data realm, and actively leading in solving complex problems hands-on. Advocate for best practices across the data platform, instilling a culture of craftsmanship and innovation. Mentor the data platform team, nurturing both technical and professional development. Minimum Requirements: A minimum of 8 years of experience in Data Platform or an equivalent combination of work and academic exposure in a quantitative field. Proven experience leading company-wide initiatives across multiple teams or influencing tech roadmap planning. Effective collaboration with diverse teams and stakeholders to drive tangible business outcomes. Demonstrated ability to balance execution and velocity with in-depth research, statistical understanding, and scalable design. Track record of mentoring and investing in the development of scientists, engineers, and peers. Experience providing technical leadership on significant projects, covering ETL frameworks, metrics stores, infrastructure management, and data security. Proficiency in building, deploying, and maintaining reliable multi-geographical data pipelines at scale. Familiarity with workflow or orchestration frameworks such as Airflow, DBT, etc. Preferred Qualifications: Hands-on design experience in crafting data processing patterns for a modern Lakehouse architecture. Contribute to the design and development of standard framework modules, high-performance services, and client libraries for big data using tools like GCP, Databricks, BigQuery, DataProc, Kafka, Kubernetes, Spark, DataFlow, Google Cloud Storage, and Airflow. Excellent written and verbal communication skills tailored for diverse audiences (leadership, users, company-wide). Ability to rapidly evaluate various technologies and conduct proof of concepts to drive architecture design. Experience thriving in a complex environment.

title: Integrated Marketing Manager (Hands-On Digital Specialist)
description: Job Description We are seeking a versatile and hands-on Integrated Marketing Manager who can balance strategic planning with strong execution capabilities. This role is responsible for driving brand growth through integrated marketing campaigns across digital, retail, and traditional channels. The ideal candidate is both a strategist and an executor — someone who can develop high-impact long-term and short-term marketing plans while personally running ads, crafting content, and delivering measurable results. This role also requires strong social listening skills to identify emerging trends, audience needs, and competitive insights, as well as the ability to translate those insights into innovative content ideation that fuels engaging, on-brand campaigns. Key Responsibilities 1. Strategy, Planning & Budget Management Lead the planning, development, and execution of integrated, multi-channel marketing campaigns to strengthen brand visibility and customer engagement. Manage and optimise the approved marketing budget in alignment with business goals and ROI targets. Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual performance reports with insights and recommendations for management. 2. Digital Marketing (Hands-On Execution) Independently plan, run, and optimise Google Ads, Meta Ads and other performance-driven campaigns. Manage SEO/SEM strategies including keyword research, content optimisation, and landing page performance. Execute email marketing campaigns (e.g., Mailchimp, Klaviyo), including A/B testing and audience segmentation. Monitor digital KPIs and provide insights using platforms such as Google Analytics and Meta Business Suite. 3. Brand, Content & Social Media Management Oversee official social media channels (Instagram, Facebook, Rednote, LinkedIn, where applicable), including content planning, copywriting, and community engagement. Work with influencers, celebrities, and KOLs to enhance brand reach and credibility. Content Ideation: Provide creative direction and clear briefs for designers, photographers, and videographers to ensure consistent branding across all touchpoints. 4. Public Relations & Media Communications Develop PR strategies to strengthen corporate and brand reputation. Craft press releases, media kits, and spokesperson briefs. Pitch stories to media outlets and cultivate strong relationships with journalists and media partners. Manage media enquiries, brand reputation issues, and crisis communication when needed. Track PR coverage and report impact. 5. Retail Marketing & CRM Plan and manage in-store promotions, premium campaigns, joint promotions, and membership initiatives. Build and maintain a robust CRM program to drive customer loyalty, retention, and targeted communications. Collaborate closely with Retail Operations on promotional calendars, retail activations, and offline marketing. 6. Website, CRO & Analytics Manage website updates and enhancements using CMS platforms such as WordPress or Shopify. Conduct A/B testing and implement optimisation initiatives to improve conversion rates and user experience. Build dashboards and analyse performance across the full funnel (awareness → consideration → conversion). 7. Market Intelligence, Vendors & Cross-Functional Collaboration Conduct market research, competitor benchmarking, and social listening to identify trends and opportunities. Work with internal teams and external vendors/agencies to deliver cohesive campaigns and marketing assets. Manage freelancers, agencies, and partners to ensure quality, timeliness, and alignment with brand objectives. Lead or support other marketing-related projects as assigned. What We’re Looking For Must-Have Skills & Attributes Strong hands-on experience executing SEM (Google Ads) and Meta (Facebook/Instagram) ads. Familiarity with email automation tools (e.g., Mailchimp, Klaviyo), CMS platforms (WordPress, Shopify), and SEO/SEM best practices. Skilled in content creation: content briefing, script writing, and video storytelling. Strong analytical, project management, and multi-tasking abilities with high attention to detail. A proactive team player who is able to work independently, think strategically, and perform well under pressure. Schedule: Mon-Fri 9am-6pm Workplace: Jln Bukit Merah Please note: The job title and salary range etc indicated are for reference purposes only. The final offer may differ based on experience, qualifications, and internal evaluation.

title: Junior Fund Accountant
description: Our Fund Office Support Services in Asset and Wealth Managed Services (“AWMS”) is expanding rapidly and we are looking for motivated professionals to join us. We are Singapore’s leading Asset and Wealth Management team, and you will experience a dynamic and fast-paced working environment. You will be responsible for a portfolio of fund manager, family office and asset owners clients (the “clients’) and will work as part of a team to achieve and meet their needs. You will be responsible for a portfolio of clients engagements to meet objectives and budget successfully. You will also act as the liaison and point of contact for these clients. Effective day-to-day management of clients; building and maintaining client relationships is crucial. You need to ensure that all deadlines and milestones are met as planned. You will also ensure that you hold yourself to the highest standard, in terms of work quality, risk management, issue escalation/resolution and client delivery. You need to understand the client’s issues and manage the expectations of different stakeholders by translating expectations into quality & balanced output/deliverables. You need to work closely with other functions (i.e., Tax, Valuation, etc) within PwC and address client needs by bringing relevant expertise from the firm to client discussions. Specific responsibilities include the following: Fund Accounting Responsible to onboard new clients and set-up new funds and fund structures into the accounting system in accordance to governing documents (i.e., PPM, LPA, etc). Responsible for providing quality accounting/bookkeeping and fund administration service for open ended/closed ended/evergreen Alternative Investments Funds (Private equity/Venture Capital/Real Estate and Fund-of funds), including family offices (the “Funds”). Preparation of monthly/quarterly/semi-annual/annual/ Net Asset Value (the “NAV”) reporting pack which includes detailed trial balance, balance sheet, statement of operations, investment, bank reconciliation and investment holdings report. Drafting of financial statements and related FRS/IFRS/RAP 7/US GAAP disclosures in accordance with accounting standards. Oversee and facilitate audits of the Funds and liaise with the auditors to ensure timely completion of audit. Perform follow-up with clients as necessary on accounting records and issues and develop solutions to these issues. Assist senior fund accountants to take care of day-to-day operation of the funds which includes resolving accounting issues and client service matters. Ensure optimal level of client service matters and all deliverables are on time and meet set quality standards. Transfer Agency Services Responsible for the maintenance of the shareholder/investor register. Prepare capital call/distribution/redemption/conversion/share transfer calculations and process these in a timely manner. Co-ordinate documentation for capital activities to ensure AML compliance. Management of commitment agreements and subscription forms including monitoring of commitments, drawdowns, and uncalled commitments. Deliver the organisation's FATCA/CRS/K1 service offering to our clients. Investors Services Assist with new client onboarding and ensure proper fund and fund structure set ups within the accounting system in accordance to governing documents (i.e., PPM, LPA, etc). Assist senior fund accountants in liaising, discussing, and resolving issues surrounding client onboarding with fund managers and stakeholders. Maintain and enforce adherence to the Fund’s policies and procedures in accordance with their business line operating model to safeguard clients’ interest Prepare investor onboarding documentations to ensure AML/KYC compliance. Take care of day-to-day operation of the funds and following up with third parties such as investors and fund managers. Assist in the organisation of board meetings and shareholder’ meetings. Responsible for maintaining client relationships, inquiries, and meetings. Operational Support Assist in setting up new client accounts and transitions by preparing required documentation and ensuring accurate system entries. Support resolution of routine fund accounting and administration issues by coordinating with relevant stakeholders. Provide timely updates to senior team members on any material issues affecting deliverables. Monitor and track performance to meet the requirements set out in the SLA. Contribute to identifying process improvement opportunities and suggest ways to enhance operational efficiency. Help maintain internal controls and compliance with SSAE 16 requirements through accurate record-keeping and adherence to procedures. FA/TA operational and organisational readiness and system implementation Assist in maintaining and updating Fund Accounting (FA) and Transfer Agency (TA) operational processes and system setups to meet evolving business needs. Support efforts to improve work processes by identifying inefficiencies and suggesting practical enhancements, including leveraging technology for greater scalability and efficiency. Provide administrative and documentation support for system implementation activities, ensuring accurate data entry and adherence to established procedures. ATTRIBUTES & SKILLS: You hold a Bachelor and/or master’s degree in Accounting or Economics. You have a minimum 1 to 3 years of relevant experience in alternative investment fund administration, Big 4 audit firm or domiciliation. You have good knowledge and understanding of the family office; and asset and wealth and management industry landscape and operations (including valuation (in private companies, debts, etc), accounting and payment procedures). You have good knowledge about Singapore fund structures (i.e., Singapore Variable Capital Company (VCC), Singapore Private Limited (Pte. Ltd.), SG limited partnership and unit trusts). You have good knowledge in accounting for investment products, ideally with fund-of-funds, single-manager funds, venture capital, real estate and private equity funds will be considered an asset. Competent accounting knowledge in Singapore FRS/IFRS/ RAP 7 and US GAAP standards. Previous experience in compliance and KYC procedures. Familiar with Singapore regulatory filing requirements. Professional, confident, and highly motivated self-starter, with an ability to handle multiple engagements/projects in a fast-paced environment with tight deadlines, positive attitude to learn and strong team player. Possess strong values, personal integrity, and emotional intelligence. Meticulous and attention to details, possessing an analytical and enquiring mind-set. Possess team spirit, a capacity to build strong client relationships and take initiative to act without waiting for direction (when appropriate). Advanced level MS excel, proficient with MS Word, MS PowerPoint. Knowledge and prior experience on Allvue System a plus. A professional designation such a C.A., C.P.A., or C.M.A. etc., would be an asset.

title: Key Account Executive
description:
Beta Pet Mart is a fast-growing pet supplies distribution startup in Singapore.
We move quickly, experiment boldly on the ground, and learn through action. Our
team works collaboratively across functions, with high autonomy and trust.
You’ll thrive here if you enjoy building as you go, taking initiative, and
shaping how things are done. If you’re looking for meaningful ownership,
rapid growth, and real upside, read on. The Role This is a frontline key account
management role , focused on managing, growing, and servicing our top
retail partners . You will own revenue growth by
strengthening existing relationships, expanding distribution and assortment,
and ensuring strong execution at both account and store level. The role is
highly hands-on (approximately 85% commercial and execution-focused) and best
suited for someone who enjoys being on the ground, partnering closely with
retailers, and driving results across both B2B and B2C touchpoints. You’ll be expected to think like an owner,
move quickly, and deliver outcomes in a dynamic environment where priorities
evolve and continuous improvement is part of the journey. What You’ll Do Manage & Grow Key Accounts (≈60%) • Serve as the primary point of contact for
assigned top retail accounts • Grow existing accounts through upselling, cross-selling, range expansion, and
deeper partnerships • Drive sell-in, sell-through, and promotional effectiveness at account level • Own account performance — revenue, distribution, execution quality, and
relationship health • Build strong, long-term relationships with key decision-makers and store
teams Execute Orders & Deliver Results (≈20%) • Ensure accurate and timely order fulfilment • Proactively resolve delivery, stock, or operational issues • Follow through until issues are fully resolved — strong ownership mindset
required Activate the Brand on the Ground (≈15%) • Execute roadshows, in-store activations,
and product launches with key retail partners • Represent the brand at pet expos and industry events • Support on-site sales activation and customer engagement initiatives Feedback, Data & Collaboration (≈5%) • Share real-time feedback on execution,
pricing, and product performance • Provide market insights, competitor activity, and retailer feedback • Work closely with the manager to refine account and sales strategies E-commerce Exposure (Bonus, Not Core) • Support Shopee, Lazada, and TikTok
promotions, listings, and order execution • Contribute ideas to grow online sales using ground-level insights Who Will Thrive Here • 2–3 years of sales or key account
management experience in the FMCG, retail, or distribution industry • Proven experience managing and growing existing retail accounts • Strong commercial mindset with a focus on account growth and execution • Results-driven, ambitious, and motivated by performance and commission • Genuine love for pets and animals, and comfortable working around
different types of animals • High interest in the pet industry , with curiosity about products,
trends, and customer needs • Comfortable with ambiguity, fast-paced environments, and limited structure • Resilient and solutions-oriented when plans change • Entrepreneurial — you identify issues and act on them without being asked • Strong communication, coordination, and negotiation skills • Able to work independently with minimal supervision Must-haves • Willing to work a 5-day week, including
Saturday or Sunday (weekday off in lieu) • Valid Class 3 driving license required (own car is a strong advantage) • Minimum education: Diploma (required); Degree preferred Nice-to-haves • Experience managing e-commerce platforms
(Shopee / Lazada / TikTok) • Prior exposure to or background in the pet industry 🐾 Compensation • Basic salary + attractive commission
structure • Uncapped upside for strong performers Why Join Us • 🚀 Startup Exposure – Build alongside founders in a scaling
business • 💰 High
Impact, High Ownership – Your results directly influence company growth • 📈 Fast
Growth for Top Performers – Bigger accounts, broader scope, leadership
opportunities • 🔥 Merit-Based
Culture – Output, grit, and ownership matter Final Note This role is not for someone seeking
routine or comfort. It’s for someone who enjoys owning key accounts, driving growth with
partners, and building something meaningful — and being rewarded for it.

title: Sales Manager (1 Year Contract)
description: About Singapore Red Cross Academy (SRCA) The Singapore Red Cross Academy is the training arm of the Singapore Red Cross, dedicated to building community resilience by equipping individuals and organisations with essential humanitarian knowledge and skills. We are the region's go-to institute for humanitarian studies, offering a comprehensive suite of courses in First Aid & Resuscitation, Psychosocial Support, and other humanitarian and caregiving programmes. We strive to empower individuals and communities to help save lives. Job Purpose The job incumbent will be responsible for driving the sales performance of the Singapore Red Cross Academy's range of courses and training programmes to corporate clients, government agencies, educational institutions, and the general public. This role requires a dynamic and results-driven individual with a passion for humanitarian work and a proven track record in achieving ambitious sales targets. Remarks: Competitive basic pay plus commission/ incentive based on performance. Key Responsibilities 1. Sales Strategy and Execution: Develop and implement strategic sales plans to achieve and exceed course enrolment and revenue targets for SRCA. Identify new business opportunities and target markets for SRCA's training offerings, particularly within the corporate and institutional sectors. Conduct market research to stay abreast of industry trends, competitor activities, and pricing strategies. Prepare and deliver compelling sales presentations, proposals, and tender submissions to potential clients. 2. Client Relationship Management (CRM): Cultivate and maintain strong, long-term relationships with key corporate accounts and institutional partners. Act as the primary point of contact for large-scale training enquiries and customised corporate training solutions. Ensure high levels of client satisfaction and secure repeat business and referrals. 3. Sales Operations and Reporting: Manage the end-to-end sales cycle, from lead generation and qualification to negotiation and closing. Maintain accurate records of sales activities, pipelines, and forecasts using the CRM system. Prepare regular sales reports and dashboards for management, detailing performance against targets and key metrics. Work closely with the Operations and Marketing teams to ensure seamless course delivery and effective promotional campaigns. 4. Product Knowledge and Advocacy: Maintain a deep understanding of all SRCA courses, accreditations (e.g., SRFAC, MOM), and subsidy schemes (e.g., SkillsFuture). Act as an ambassador for the Singapore Red Cross humanitarian mission and the value of its training programmes. 5. Team Collaboration (if applicable to team structure): Mentor and provide guidance to junior sales or account management staff, fostering a high-performance sales culture. Collaborate with the Curriculum Development team to communicate market needs and client feedback for continuous course improvement. Job Requirements Qualifications and Experience: Minimum Diploma or Degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field. Minimum 3 years of relevant sales experience. Experience in the training and education sector is a plus. Proven track record of consistently meeting or exceeding sales quotas. Skills and Knowledge: Excellent communication, presentation, and negotiation skills. Strong business acumen and ability to understand client training needs. Proficiency in using CRM software and Microsoft Office Suite. Knowledge of Singapore's training landscape, including government grants and accreditation bodies, is highly advantageous. Demonstrates a high level of initiative, self-motivation, and integrity. Ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative team.

title: Assistant Campus Manager
description: Position Objective The Assistant Campus Manager plays a key operational and facilities support role. The  position is responsible for ensuring all aspects of facilities, operations, safety, and vendor management are delivered efficiently, safely, and cost-effectively. Working closely with the Campus Manager, the role partners with academic leadership teams to ensure that the campus environment supports high-quality teaching and learning, meets all regulatory requirements, and reflects each school’s unique identity. Responsibilities Leadership, Collaboration & Stakeholder Management • Support the Campus Manager in partnering with school principals and academic leaders to ensure the campus meets operational, capacity, and functional needs. • Assist with the fair and effective coordination of shared spaces, ensuring all schools are supported equitably. • Build strong relationships with internal stakeholders, vendors, regulatory partners, and third-party service providers. • Provide day-to-day guidance to maintenance, facilities, and soft services teams, supporting employee engagement, deployment, and performance. • Collaborate with the Facilities Directors/Campus Managers across Singapore to align processes, identify  efficiencies, and support group-wide operational standards. Facilities & Maintenance Management Support the planning, execution, and reporting of all preventive, corrective, and reactive maintenance activities. • Oversee the daily management of all outsourced engineering, mechanical, electrical, air-conditioning, along with soft services such as security, cleaning, landscaping, and all general maintenance vendors deployed on site. • Assist in monitoring and reporting on SLA and KPI performance for all facilities teams and services. • Conduct routine inspections of building systems, learning spaces, playgrounds, and soft services operations, ensuring compliance, upkeep, and readiness. • Support the management, including procurement, inventory control, financial tracking, and the annual budgeting process. • Assist in the development and implementation of long-term campus maintenance and optimisation plans (5- and 10-year outlooks). • Contribute to the planning and delivery of renovation works, enhancement projects, and campus improvements. • Laise with local government agencies where necessary (NEA, BCA, URA, SCDF, LTA ect) Health, Safety, Fire & Risk Management: Support the implementation and adherence to Health & Safety, Fire Safety, Evacuation, and Risk Management policies and procedures. • Coordinate with the external Fire Safety Manager and assist in SCDF inspections, rectifications, and renewal of the Annual Fire Certificate. • Partner with academic teams to ensure classroom setups, learning environments, and early-years spaces are safe, compliant, and developmentally appropriate. • Maintain and deliver training for the CERT team, ensuring drills, exercises, and emergency procedures are executed as scheduled. • Support safe work practices by enforcing Risk Assessments and ensuring contractor compliance with all relevant standards and guidelines. • Maintain and keep updated documentation records for all health and safety matters and prepare documentation for all audits. Operations Management Oversee daily operational support services including cleaning, security, logistics, event support, transportation coordination, and general campus upkeep. Provide operational support for school events, activities, and special programmes. • Support tender reviews, RFP processes, and contract administration in partnership with the Campus Manager. • Assist in the ongoing review and improvement of Standard Operating Procedures, service standards, and operational workflows to ensure efficiency and service excellence. • Drive the adoption of technology, innovation, and productivity improvements to enhance campus operations. • Maintain strong relationships with all internal and external stakeholders to ensure smooth, reliable, and responsive service delivery. • Perform any other duties as necessary to support the smooth and efficient operations. Position Requirements •	Excellent communication skills - written and spoken English •	Good interpersonal skills, with the ability to interact professionally with stakeholders of various nationalities. •	Self-motivated, proactive, and creative problem-solving skills to ensure an outstanding customer service delivery is maintained •	Ability to work independently in a fast-paced and challenging environment, manage teams of third-party vendors to ensure an outstanding service is delivered and maintained •	Proficient in IT skills with good MS Office knowledge Qualifications: •	Degree or Diploma in Engineering (Mechanical/ Electrical/ Facilities Management) or equivalent qualification •	Minimum 5 years of experience in a similar role •	Possession of a valid Fire Safety Manager (FSM) certification to perform FSM duties or the willingness to undertake certification will be advantageous •	Knowledge of Singapore laws and regulations relating to Building Management and Regulations including but not limited to Fire Safety •	Possession of a valid Environmental control coordinator would be advantageous Working Hours: 8:00 am – 5:00 pm, Monday to Friday

title: Receiving & Cost Control Officer
description: GOODWOOD PARK HOTEL One of Singapore’s most established Heritage Hotel and strategically located at Scotts Road, the Goodwood Park Hotel has celebrated its timeless elegance, legendary hospitality, and tradition of excellence. We are committed to building a high-performing team that is thoroughly engaged in achieving service excellence to exceed our guests' expectations. To continue the legacy of Goodwood Park, we are looking for dynamic and committed candidates to join our Finance Department. Reporting to the Cost Controller, your job responsibilities include, but not limited to:- Job Responsibilities include, but not limited to: Receiving Responsibilities Receive all incoming materials, goods and supplies from approved vendors. Verify deliveries against purchase orders, invoices, and delivery challans. Coordinate with the relevant requesting departments to verify the goods delivered. Coordinate with purchasing personnel for any goods that is rejected / damage / shortages or error from the delivery or not delivered on the day. Completes the receiving process by posting all delivery orders or invoice to the inventory system. Ensure proper unloading, handling, safety, and hygiene procedures. Prepare and process Goods, Receipt Notes (GRN) in an inventory system. Label, tag or arrange materials in designated storage locations and manage return-to-vendor and rejection processes as per company policy. Costing & Inventory Control Prepare detailed cost report, standard recipe costing and product/service costing. Monitor actual costs versus budgets and approved cost standards. Analyse details itemize for every promotion or any adhoc event. Verify supplier invoices, delivery bills and cost-related documents. Track wastage, losses and inefficiencies and report deviations. Ensure compliance with company SOPs and cost control standards. Support internal and external audits by providing cost and inventory data. Prepare monthly reports on inventory and cost performance. Ensure FIFO / FEFO practices, support stock audits and cycle counts. Responsible for all inventories in the store and assists in the monthly stock take with the Cost control team. Verify all Delivery Orders/Invoices, ensure they are duly signed, stamped, and attached with relevant supporting documents before passing to Account Payable for further processing. Responsible for all inventories in the store and assists in the monthly stock take with the Cost control team. Conduct inventory stock take, and participate in operating equipment or Asset count when required. Performs any other duties as may be assigned from time to time by Management. Requirements Must possess any relevant qualification Minimally 2 years of experience in similar capacity Must be a team player and possess good communication skills Be able to start immediately

Meeting & Communication: Schedule meetings, prepare materials, and assist with stakeholder communications. Stakeholder Engagement: Support change management activities and facilitate buy-in for new processes. Continuous Learning: Stay updated on industry trends and participate in relevant training programs. Who you work with Group Legal & Compliance takes care of integrity and ensures compliance with all rules and regulations, developing the policies & procedures that manage and mitigate risk. In an evolving world where we’re actively driving digital transformation, it’s no easy task. But we thrive on the challenge because we’re future-ready and focused on driving change. Who you are A recent graduate from a Polytechnic or University with a diploma or degree in the relevant field. Possess a Business, Information Systems, or a related discipline. Demonstrate a proactive approach and a strong work ethic. Exhibit a keen interest in emerging technologies and process improvement. Showcase strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Possess excellent communication and interpersonal abilities. Learning Objectives This role will provide you with: Hands-on experience with process mapping, analysis, and improvement techniques. Practical skills in project management and execution. Exposure to cutting-edge technologies like Power Automate, Power Apps, and Gen AI. Opportunities to collaborate with stakeholders at all levels. A deeper understanding of legal and compliance operations within a leading financial institution. Who we are As Singapore’s longest established bank, we have been dedicated to enabling individuals and businesses to achieve their aspirations since 1932. How? By taking the time to truly understand people. From there, we provide support, services, solutions, and career paths that meet their individual needs and desires. Today, we’re on a journey of transformation. Leveraging technology and creativity to become a future-ready learning organisation. But for all that change, our strategic ambition is consistently clear and bold, which is to be Asia’s leading financial services partner for a sustainable future. We invite you to build the bank of the future. Innovate the way we deliver financial services. Work in friendly, supportive teams. Build lasting value in your community. Help people grow their assets, business, and investments. Take your learning as far as you can. Or simply enjoy a vibrant, future-ready career. Your Opportunity Starts Here. title: Senior Manager (Project finance and M&A expert) - Singapore
description: As Senior Manager on our Project Finance & M&A deal team, you will be right in the middle of all the action. From working on impactful transactions to leading missions with autonomy, you will be accountable for the smooth execution of advisory missions relating to financial structuring, raising required debt or leading M&A equity finance transactions. You will be the primary point of contact for clients, other stakeholders and the rest of the team. This is the ideal job for you to apply your analytical skills and curiosity for out-of-the-box solutions. Singapore Green Giraffe team Our Singapore office opened in 2020 to accelerate the energy transition in Asia-Pacific, and covers the full scope of Green Giraffe services, including development equity, M&A, debt advisory and strategic support, across all renewable and energy transition technologies, including solar, onshore wind, offshore wind, storage and hydrogen. You will be joining a growing Singapore team and will work in close collaboration with the rest of the Green Giraffe “tower” worldwide (Europe & Africa) and especially the team spread out across the Asia-Pacific region (Sydney & Tokyo). By joining the Singapore team, you will have opportunities to learn on multiple fronts, from project execution to developing commercial efforts locally and regionally. If you have an entrepreneurial mindset and want to join an energetic team wanting to accelerate the energy transition in APAC, don't hesitate any longer and send us your application now! Key responsibilities Taking responsibility for advisory missions e.g. relating to financial structuring, raising required debt or supporting M&A equity finance transactions Leading the interactions with clients, banks and other project stakeholders Supervising financial analysts on the development and management of financial models and cash flow valuations for renewable energy projects Overseeing due diligence processes including work prepared by your internal team (associates and analysts) and by other advisors Gathering of market intelligence on sectors of interest to the company such as electricity markets, debt markets or venture capital markets Participating actively in the business development of Green Giraffe in Singapore and the wider APAC region by proactively maintaining relationships with existing clients, developing your network and reaching out to new potential clients Being active in sharing knowledge internally within the local, regional and international team Active participation in an entrepreneurial, international and dynamic team focused on making projects happen and going the extra mile for the client We are looking for someone with a master's or similar degree, preferably in Finance or Engineering, having 8+years of relevant work experience and a proven interest for renewable energies. In addition, here are some attributes that are critical to the job: Fluency in English (written and spoken) Excellent analytical skills and the ability to learn quickly A strong understanding of and mastering of financial concepts and products, and financial modelling techniques for PF & M&A transactions Ability to communicate complex ideas with ease and simplicity The ability to work in a fast-paced environment Initiative, ownership and accountability for allocated tasks The ability to work in cross-border teams with a willingness to travel abroad Empathy and willingness to understand the needs and aspirations of relevant stakeholders Professional experience in the renewable energy sector and project and/or corporate finance Package and application This job is to be filled on a permanent contract basis and full-time. A competitive compensation package including various benefits is offered. The candidate can expect opportunities to travel to other offices and to participate in regular team building activities. Additionally, the candidate will be given the opportunity to grow and take responsibility in a dynamic renewables market environment. If you’re interested in starting your life as a Giraffe, don’t wait and please submit your application! About Green Giraffe If you are passionate about finance, keen to contribute actively to the energy transition, and want to work in a dynamic, international and fast paced environment in a team that thrives on delivering high quality results whilst having a collaborative and fun working atmosphere, you should definitely consider joining us and becoming a Giraffe ! Advancing the global energy transformation towards sustainable power systems requires a rapid scaling-up of the renewable energy ecosystem and a paradigm shift in investment patterns. Every day, over 120 giraffes spread out globally on five continents work together to provide tailor-made advisory services to their clients across the renewable energy value chain. We bring value to our clients and the projects that we work on through the quality of work we deliver, our wide and in-depth knowledge of the markets, and our independent and honest advice. We believe in transparency and teamwork, in taking ownership and going the extra mile for our clients and make time to celebrate successes, milestones and personal achievements together. As Green Giraffe grows, enters new markets, and paves the way ahead for the entire renewable energy business value chain, we are looking for people with an entrepreneurial mindset to challenge the norms and push the envelope of possibilities.

description: At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture and technology to become the best version of you. An effective communicator, you’ll be a confident team player that collaborates with people from various teams while looking to develop your career in a dynamic organization. What working at EY offers We offer a competitive compensation package where you’ll be rewarded based on your performance and recognized for the value you bring to our business. title: Lecturer / Senior Lecturer, Ridge View Residential College
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description Ridge View Residential College (RVRC) is a vibrant "living-and-learning" community of around 650 NUS students, As part of their residency, sudents participate in a two-year academic programme that complements their majors by supporting four of the the NUS General Education pillars. This programme is integrated into the day-to-day life of the College, which has a strong focus on sustainability and workplace readiness. RVRC's education mission is to cultivate students who are intellectually inquisitive, keen to grow, future-oriented, and value social and environmental responsibility. Job Description : RVRC seeks to appoint a committed individual to complement our team of educators. The successful applicant will, among other things, teach interdisciplinary courses, contribute to curriculum design and development, and engage in out-of-classroom learning and other student-centric pedagogies anchored in the different forms of sustainability and / or workplace readiness.  The appointee will also be expected to contribute to the College's student and residential life programmes, which include advising and mentoring students in various living-and-learning activities. We are specifically seeking applicants with expertise in workplace readiness, although the ideal applicant must have strong interdisciplinary capabilities coupled with a passion for their own field of expertise. The ideal applicant must work work well in a team and be committed to student learning, supporting students in their years of university life, and preparing them for their post college experiences. The successful applicant will be employed as a full-time RVRC faculty member on the University's Educator Track. The appointment is targeted for July 2026. We welcome you to apply to join our committed team of educators! Responsibilities : Design and teach academic interdisciplinary courses, specifically with a focus on intrapersonal and interpersonal skills, cultural intelligence, and / or the future of work. Engage students in out-of-classroom learning (including outdoor learning). Mentor students in formal and informal learning settings. Support the College's mission through service contributions, including leading living-and-learning initiatives. Qualifications Requirements: A PhD (or equivalent). Candidates with teaching experience at the tertiary level are preferred. Fully embracing of interdisciplinary, integrative teaching in and beyond the classroom. Motivation and reflectiveness as an educator, Adaptability to dynamic working environments. Passion for student growth and success. Enthusiasm to engage students in a living-and-learning programme. Interest in organising and participating in out-of-classroom learning activities. Application Documents : Cover letter Detailed CV listing:academic qualificationsemployment history (include all academic and non-academic positions held, including leadership roles, if any)summary of relevant courses/modules taughtpublications and conference presentations (if any)names and contact email of 3 referees. Statement of teaching philosophy and reflections on teaching practice (2-page limit) Evidence of teaching quality, including feedback from students and peer evaluations (if available) Other supporting documents We appreciate your understanding in advance that only selected applicants will be contacted. Review of applications will begin early and proceed on a rolling basis, prior to the conclusion of the application period. Closing date : 22 February 2026

title: Talent & Learning Manager (Fixed Term Contract)
description: The Opportunity… At Moët Hennessy, We Don't Just Create Exceptional Experiences – We Live Them. For generations, our 27 Maisons, including Dom Pérignon, Veuve Clicquot, and Hennessy, have defined luxury. Now, it's your turn. Imagine a career fueled by passion and creativity, where you redefine what's possible. We seek individuals who thrive in collaborative environments, sharing our love for craftsmanship, elegance, and integrity. We celebrate diversity and the pursuit of excellence. Ready to drive learning and talent innovation at a global luxury brand? As Talent and Learning Manager for Moët Hennessy in Asia Pacific, your role will involve partnering with regional and global teams to design and implement strategic L&D initiatives. Your work will directly empower our people, build essential capabilities, and cultivate an engaging and inclusive culture that is critical to our ongoing success. If you're passionate about fostering continuous growth, enhancing critical skills, and nurturing an engaging work environment, this is your opportunity to contribute to our success. This is a 9-momth fixed-term contract. How you will contribute… Learning & Development Strategy and Execution Strategic L&D Leadership: Support the development and implementation of the regional Talent & Learning strategy, aligning with regional and global objectives and leverage existing tools and platforms to foster a culture of continuous learning and critical capability enhancement across Asia Pacific. Capability & Needs Assessment: Conduct thorough skills assessments and needs analyses to identify critical skill gaps and development opportunities, directly informing the design of targeted training solutions that support the regional SOAR strategy. Program Design & Delivery: Lead the end-to-end design, development, and facilitation of a diverse learning calendar, encompassing engaging workshops, digital learning, and experiential programs, ensuring high-quality content delivery. Stakeholder Collaboration & Curation: Act as a strong people partner, collaborating effectively with global and regional teams, and engaging with external vendors to curate and deliver high-quality, relevant learning content. Performance Measurement & Improvement: Establish and implement robust evaluation methods for all learning programs and initiatives, utilizing data and feedback to drive continuous improvement and demonstrate impact. Learning Culture & Engagement: Champion a dynamic, feedback-driven learning culture, actively promoting participative learning and growth initiatives throughout the organization. Talent Development & Management: Strategic Talent Advancement: Drive regional talent investment strategies and programs, focusing on upskilling, leadership advancement, and overall capability building to support the long-term talent agenda. Talent Pipeline & Mobility: Collaborate with HR teams to actively build a robust and diverse talent pipeline within the region, ensuring talent mobility and readiness for future organizational needs. High-Potential Development: Identify and nurture high-potential talent, providing targeted development opportunities to accelerate their growth and prepare them for future leadership roles. Other: Budget Management & Operational Excellence: Manage the L&D and Talent Development budget effectively, ensuring efficient allocation of resources, and overseeing administrative processes to support the smooth execution of all programs and initiatives. Cross-Functional Support & Ad-Hoc Initiatives: Provide responsive support for various HR and business initiatives, including contributing to ad-hoc projects, preparing reports, and handling other duties as assigned to advance organizational talent objectives We are looking for unique and amazing talent. Join us if you have… 8+ years of experience in Learning & Development, Talent Management, or a similar HR function, with a strong focus on training and program delivery. Proven experience in designing, implementing, and evaluating learning programs with experience using LMS platforms Strong understanding of adult learning principles and instructional design methodologies. Excellent communication, presentation, and facilitation skills. Ability to work effectively in a multicultural and fast-paced environment. Proactive, results-oriented, and a strong team player. Proficiency in English; additional regional languages are a plus When you join Moët Hennessy… You embark on a career journey with the leaders of luxury wines, champagnes, and spirits, within the prestigious LVMH Group. You enter a world of possibility where career development opportunities across our 5 global regions and within the entire LVMH Group are unmatched. You join a passionate workforce within our internationally diverse and inclusive teams, exploring the rich heritage of our Maisons and shaping the next chapter of our story You discover our essential relationship with our terroirs and the soils that define our products. Through our Living Soils Living Together program, we are committed to regenerating soils, reducing climate impact, and empowering communities for a sustainable future. You will share in our passion for hosting, the art of living, and creating products that celebrate life’s most memorable milestones, always experienced through responsible tasting. Ready for a world of possibilities? Discover Moët Hennessy today!

title: Quantity Surveyor
description: Job Description We are looking for a highly skilled and experienced Lead Quantity Surveyor to join our team. In this role, you will manage and oversee all aspects of quantity surveying, such as cost estimation, cost control, contract administration, and project management. Your contribution will be vital in ensuring the successful delivery of our construction projects on time and within budget. Responsibilities: • Develop accurate and detailed cost estimates for construction projects, covering material, labor, and equipment costs. • Perform comprehensive cost analysis and value engineering to optimize project budgets. • Review and negotiate contracts to ensure compliance with legal standards and project specifications. • Monitor and control project costs during construction, identifying potential overruns and implementing corrective actions. • Work closely with project managers, engineers, and architects to assess requirements and create cost-effective solutions. • Prepare and present regular progress and financial reports to senior management and stakeholders. • Conduct site visits to assess project progress, verify quantities, and address any quantity-related issues. • Stay informed about industry trends, regulations, and best practices in quantity surveying. Requirements: • Bachelor's degree in Quantity Surveying, Civil Engineering, or a related field (Master's degree preferred) • Minimum of 2 years of experience as a Quantity Surveyor. • In-depth knowledge of construction cost estimation, contract administration, and project management principles. • Proficiency in using quantity surveying software and tools such as CostX, Bluebeam, or similar • Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities, with a keen eye for detail. • Solid understanding of construction methods, materials, and building regulations. • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with diverse stakeholders. • Self-motivated and proactive, capable of working independently and leading a team.

title: Actuarial (Financial Services) Senior Consultant, Risk Consulting
description: At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture and technology to become the best version of you. And we’re counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better, too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all. The Opportunity As part of our life actuarial team, you’ll focus on high-impact opportunities to apply your knowledge and experience that will shape our services. You’ll build valuable relationships with clients and develop strong capabilities, through both formal training and working with senior mentors and talented colleagues. You will be at the forefront of issues that are shaping the insurance industry in Asia and gain a wide exposure to a diverse range of actuarial projects. Your Key Responsibilities As a Senior Consultant in the Actuarial team, you will be assisting and leading the delivery of a wide range of client engagements, leading or working as part of a project team, relating to IFRS 17, Appointed Actuary work, actuarial valuation, pricing and product development, actuarial modelling, Merger & Acquisitions (M&A) and due diligence. You will also be performing evaluation in areas related to financial risk, product distribution and capital issues. You will also work with advanced financial modeling approaches and systems, e.g. designing valuation and Asset Liability Management (ALM) frameworks. Skills and Attributes for Success Good understanding of economic or market issues and the ability to interpret their impact on clients Possess strong interpersonal and teaming skills Has strong analytical and creative skills Leverage technology to continually learn, improve service delivery and maintain our leading-edge best practices To Qualify for the role, you must have Min 3 years of experience in the life actuarial team or an actuarial consulting firm Newly or Nearly qualified with either the Institute and Faculty of Actuaries (UK), the Society of Actuaries (US) or the Institute of Actuaries of Australia Good knowledge of Prophet software Good written and verbal communication skills Ideally, you’ll also have Depth of experience in at least one of the following areas: Financial modeling Financial and regulatory reporting Product and distribution strategy ERM / Solvency II M&A due diligence What we look for Highly motivated individuals with excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to prioritize shifting workloads in a rapidly changing industry. An effective communicator, you’ll be a confident team player that collaborates with people from various teams while looking to develop your career in a dynamic organization. What working at EY offers We offer a competitive compensation package where you’ll be rewarded based on your performance and recognized for the value you bring to our business. We also offer you: Support, coaching and feedback from some of the most engaging colleagues around Opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career The freedom and flexibility to handle your role in a way that’s right for you If you can confidently demonstrate that you meet the criteria above, please contact us as soon as possible.

description: Coffee Meets Bagel is a women-founded, women-led organization created to give everyone a chance at love. The ideal candidate will have bias for action, have a passion for experimentation, and can roll-up their sleeves to . They are also extremely detail-oriented, careful, and conscientious. The Lifecycle Product Manager will perform product discovery by performing CRM experiments; doing data analysis; creating regular reporting to understand the progress of the campaigns; and consolidating them into PRDs. Finally, as we are a lean team, he or she should be strong at operations, as they will directly be in charge of setting up CRM campaigns. This role will be based in Singapore and report directly to the Head of Product. The Role Responsibilities Lifecycle Strategy and Execution Develop and own the overall user lifecycle roadmap- from testing and experimentation to scaling automated lifecycle programmes Lead the programmatic transformation of lifecycle management in CMB: Brainstorm and suggest new ideas to motivate behaviour based on user psychology (eg. how to get guys to take better photos?) Conduct ad hoc tests and experiments to validate hypotheses Establish a base set of effective, automated lifecycle campaigns Continuously design new segments and test personalised copy, triggers, and message types to improve performance Collaborate with Product and Engineering teams to prioritise and schedule life cycle-related product and data work Iterate based on data insights and learnings to enhance effectiveness Implement CRM campaigns in Braze, both adhoc and programmatic Lead the ideation and execution of sales (eg. how many % off for who?) strategies and products Lead the direct improvement of onboarding conversion rates through prospective user management Segmentation and Personalisation Strategise, build, and manage audience segments across channels    Programmatically personalise both engagement and monetisation user journeys based on: Demographics: gender, age, country Behaviour: likes received, chat activity, login activity Account Quality: profile completeness, photos, and prompt quality. Triggers: actions requiring follow-up (e.g., abandoned onboarding, inactive users, incomplete purchases) ...via sales campaigns, in-app pushes, push notifications, and email experiences Data-Driven Optimisation Write and execute SQL queries identify key engagement and conversion drivers Set up experimentation frameworks (A/B tests, holdout groups) to measure impact Analyse results and translate insights into actionable improvements across CRM touchpoints Report and recommend on the improvement of lifecycle health according to engagement, retention, and revenue metrics Cross-Functional Collaboration Work closely with Marketing, Design, and Engineering to ensure seamless CRM execution and integration with product experiences Influence messaging logic, in-app surfaces, and technical infrastructure needed for lifecycle management scalability Our Ideal Candidate 0-3 years of managing a consumer-facing product as a Product Manager in a Technology company (open to fresh grads) Has worked in a day-to-day capacity with a scrum team building actual products Strong user empathy and analytical skills Can confidently write and execute their own SQL with no data team support (or, with other programming language knowledge, can prove to us that  they can learn) Is detail-oriented, willing and conscientious to execute on and QA operational lifecycle campaigns by him/ herself Has experience working with other functions (marketing, operations) to launch a product from end to end Is comfortable in a fast-paced environment interacting with global teams Ideally possess practical communication skills across English and Mandarin, both written and verbal to work with mandarin stakeholders. Join us and be part of a team dedicated to crafting meaningful connections through innovative design solutions. If you thrive in a collaborative environment and are passionate about building great products, we’d love to hear from you!

title: Programmes Executive
description: The Programmes Executive plays a crucial role in supporting Habitat for Humanity Singapore’s  programmes, both locally and overseas.  This individual should be passionate about working with vulnerable individuals and families, as well as relating to volunteers. As an entry-level executive, you will be instrumental in the successful management and execution of local projects, including assessing homeowner needs, evaluating living spaces and rehabilitation works required. From there, you will take charge of coordinating and facilitating a range of rehabilitation works from engaging professional services such as pest control and furniture, to working with volunteers on the ground with painting, intensive cleaning and home organisation. You won’t be alone in this effort; You’ll collaborate closely with a dedicated team of staff and volunteers to ensure smooth and efficient service delivery. Beyond local initiatives, a portion of your role will also involve contributing to managing overseas projects for the Global Village programme. Job Scope: Local Programmes Project Management · Plan and coordinate projects, ensuring they align with established timelines and budgets. · Managing project logistics and operations for all local projects such as Project HomeWorks, UnLitter Red Dor and other future local projects/events · Liaising and communicating with referring partners and social workers from various agencies (Family Service Centres, Active Ageing Centres, Hospitals, etc), to assist the beneficiaries and making sure that there is a long-term plan in place for a sustainable change and providing safe and sanitary home environment. · Documenting project details and expenses in a timely and accurate manner. · Work closely with programme teams and volunteers to ensure successful project implementation. This may also involve volunteer recruitment, briefing and training. · Prepare and manage logistics required for the project For Project HomeWorks specifically: · Evaluate the conditions of the homes (clutter, pest infestation, cleanliness, and willingness of the homeowner) and rehabilitation needs through joint home visits with referring partner. · Work with referring partners and social workers to create solutions through discussion and agreements on works to be done to achieve safe and sanitary living conditions. · Work with referring partners and social workers to evaluate the progress and the project completion. Overseas Programmes Project Management · Serve as primary liaison with Habitat partners, managing communications regarding proposals, volunteer itineraries and budgets, and all necessary documentation in accordance with standard operating procedures. · Oversee timely submission of reports to Habitat regional and international counterparts. · Travel overseas as needed to provide on-site oversight and audit project progress. Volunteer / Homepartner Management · Maintain accurate and timely records in our Customer Relationship Management (CRM) and other internal systems, ensuring data is readily available for reporting and analysis. · Manage and maintain organised documentation digitally. · Schedule and coordinate volunteer programmes, leading team briefings and presentations as needed. · Gather and analyse feedback to continuously improve internal processes · Effectively manage emergency situations should they arise. A successful candidate would be · Possesses a diploma from an accredited institution · Possesses strong organisational skills with meticulous attention to detail · Excellent interpersonal and communication skills to effectively engage beneficiaries, volunteers and partners · A self-motivated and mature individual who thrives in a team environment and demonstrates strong initiative · Maintains a reasonable level of physical fitness as the role may involve some physically demanding tasks (Eg. packing and moving logistics) · Proficient in Microsoft Office applications and email communication · Fluent in written and spoken English · The job involves interaction with beneficiaries who may only speak Mandarin, Malay, Tamil or dialects. Knowledge of any of such basic conversational language is preferred. · Possesses a valid Class 3/3A driving license · Available to work on some weekends (1-2 Saturdays per month) · A passion for our charity’s mission and a genuine desire to make a positive impact. · Comfortable to uphold, communicate and affirm Habitat for Humanity’s principles (vision and mission): pls refer to https://www.habitat.org.sg/about-us for details. If you are interested to join us and be part of our exciting and meaningful ministry, please email us your complete curriculum vitae. Do include a cover letter with your salary expectations and email it to hr@habitat.org.sg . Only short-listed candidates will be contacted for an interview. Habitat's Expectations Surrounding Safeguarding: Habitat for Humanity requires that all employees take seriously their ethical responsibilities to safeguarding our intended beneficiaries, their communities, and all those with whom we work. Managers at all levels have particular responsibilities to support and develop systems that create and maintain an environment that prevents harassment, sexual exploitation and abuse; safeguards the rights of beneficiaries and community members (especially children); and promotes the implementation of Habitat for Humanity's code of conduct.

title: Creative & Marketing Intern
description: About Us Bandwagon is a leading music media and marketing company in Asia. We work with forward-looking brands and communities to create culturally relevant campaigns that live across digital platforms and real-world experiences. We are looking for a driven and creative, Creative & Marketing Intern to join our team. This role sits at the intersection of creative design, content, and marketing, offering hands-on exposure to how ideas are developed, shaped, and executed across multiple touch points. If you are passionate about visual storytelling, digital culture, and creative marketing, and want to build real-world skills beyond the classroom, this internship offers an opportunity to learn, create, and contribute meaningfully to impactful campaigns. What You Will Do As a Creative & Marketing Intern, you will support the creative marketing team in ideating, designing, and executing campaigns across digital and physical platforms. You will be involved in the full creative and marketing process, from concept development and content ideation to design production and on-ground execution. This role is ideal for someone who wants to actively create, experiment, and understand how creative and marketing strategies translate into real-world outcomes. Key Responsibilities 1. Creative & Marketing Ideation Contribute to brainstorming sessions for creative campaigns, content concepts, and marketing initiatives. Develop visual and conceptual ideas for digital and social media content. Create mood boards, visual references, and creative directions for campaigns. Translate marketing objectives into creative concepts and visual narratives. 2. Design & Content Production Design visual assets for digital platforms, including social media posts, stories, posters, EDMs, and presentation decks. Adapt and resize key visuals across formats and platforms. Apply design principles such as layout, typography, and colour to produce visually compelling content. Edit and refine creative assets to maintain visual consistency and quality. Support the development of creative outputs for campaigns and activations. 3. Marketing & Campaign Execution Support the planning and execution of integrated marketing campaigns across digital and physical touchpoints. Assist in coordinating campaign deliverables, timelines, and creative assets. Support on-ground activations and campaign rollouts where required. Collaborate with creative, content, and marketing teams to ensure timely and effective execution. Organise and manage campaign materials and creative assets. 4. Video & Motion Content Edit short-form video content for digital platforms. Create basic motion graphics and visual transitions where required. Prepare video assets for social media and campaign use. Explore creative approaches to visual storytelling in video formats. 5. Creative Operations & Team Support Assist in preparing creative and marketing decks for internal and external use. Organise design files, references, and creative assets. Support ad-hoc creative and marketing tasks as required. Learn and adapt to agency workflows and creative processes. Who We’re Looking For Currently pursuing or recently completed a diploma or degree in Design, Visual Communication, Marketing, Communications, Multimedia, or a related field. Strong interest in creative design, visual storytelling, and digital content. Working knowledge of design tools such as Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator, with familiarity in video editing tools. Understanding of design fundamentals including layout, typography, and colour. Comfortable translating ideas into visual and content outputs. Curious, proactive, and willing to experiment with creative ideas. Able to take feedback and iterate on work. Organised, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment. What You’ll Gain Hands-on experience across creative design, content, and marketing execution. Exposure to real-world campaigns and on-ground activations. Mentorship from experienced creatives and marketers. Opportunities to contribute ideas and see them brought to life. A strong portfolio of creative and campaign work. Insight into agency workflows and creative strategy. How to Apply Send your CV, portfolio, and a short introduction to join@bandwagon.sg. This internship is based in Singapore.

Demonstrated problem-solving skills to address customer concerns and resolve issues effectively. Comfortable working with automated chatbot functionalities and learning new technological tools. Strong organization and time-management abilities to meet customer needs promptly. Result-driven and goal-oriented mindset with a track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets. Adaptability and willingness to learn about personal loan products and processes. Self-motivated, proactive, and able to work independently as well as part of a team.

title: Senior Digital Marketing Specialist
description:
The Mandate: Leading the "Growth Pod" First Page Digital is APAC’s highest-rated digital agency. As we pivot to a Net Revenue Retention (NRR) model, we need hands-on leaders who can drive performance, not just manage tasks. We are looking for a Team Lead to manage a "pod" of Strategic Account Managers. You will be responsible for the aggregate retention and growth of your pod’s client portfolio. You are a deal coach, a retention strategist, and a mentor who turns good Account Managers into great Commercial Consultants. Your goal is simple: Ensure your pod achieves >100% Net Revenue Retention (NRR) by coaching your team to protect their base and expand their accounts. Key Responsibilities 1. Pod Performance Management (NRR Focus) Own the Pod P&L: You are accountable for the monthly retention (GRR) and expansion (NRR) targets of your pod. You will run weekly pipeline reviews with your team to forecast renewals and identify upsell opportunities. Deal Coaching: Act as the "Deal Desk" for your team. Help AMs structure complex proposals, pricing strategies, and "productised" upsells to ensure they are closing profitable growth. Churn Defense: You are the primary escalation point for "at-risk" clients. When a client’s health score drops or a complaint comes in, you step in to lead the "save strategy" and stabilise the relationship. 2. Quality Assurance & Coaching Shadow & Certify: Regularly shadow your AMs during Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs) and client pitches. Provide live feedback on their consultative selling style and ability to discuss ROI. Roadmap Approval: Review and approve the "Account Value Vision" (12-month strategic roadmap) for the top 20% of clients in your pod, ensuring every key account has a clear growth plan. Skill Development: Identify skill gaps in your pod (e.g., "weak at negotiating" or "poor financial literacy") and run micro-training sessions to upskill them. 3. Operational Rhythm Enforce Proactivity: Ensure your team is adhering to the proactive engagement
     cadence (QBRs, Monthly Reports, Strategy Calls). Ensure they are not falling into the "reactive support" trap. Cross-Department Synergy: Act as the bridge between your pod and the Technical Teams
     (SEO/Paid Media). Ensure the tech teams are feeding your AMs with performance insights that can be turned into revenue opportunities. What We Need From You Experience: 5+ years in Digital Marketing Account Management, with at least 1-2 years of experience mentoring or leading junior team members. Track Record of Growth: Proven history of growing a portfolio (high NRR) and saving difficult client relationships. Consultative Sales Skills: You are an expert at uncovering client business needs and connecting agency services to C-level business goals (Revenue, CLV, ROAS). Crisis Management: You stay calm under pressure. You can de-escalate an angry client and turn a complaint into a constructive conversation. Analytical Ability: You can look at a CRM dashboard, spot a "churn risk" trend (e.g., declining engagement), and direct your team to fix it. Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) Pod Net Revenue Retention (NRR): >100% (The aggregate growth of your team's accounts). Pod Gross Revenue Retention (GRR): >94% (Minimizing churn in your pod). QBR Completion Rate: 100% of clients in your pod receiving strategic quarterly reviews. Team Development: Progression of your direct reports (e.g., promoting Juniors to Seniors). The Perks Leadership Pathway: Clear progression path to "Head of Client Success." Standard Agency Benefits: Guaranteed yearly increment , flexible work arrangements , medical/dental benefits , and monthly team building. How to Apply Please attach your CV and we'll be in touch for a confidential chat. Let's do great things together!

description: Are you ready to make a real impact on trading profitability? Join a team where your analytical skills and insights drive both cost savings and revenue growth.
